I_kwDOM5SPu86ptqd4.title: Remove extraneous configs
I_kwDOM5SPu86ptqd4.body:
- `collaboratorOnly` from `daemon-pricing`
- `optOpt` from `daemon-disqualifier`

Also report why we have optOpt in the first place I don't understand the benefit of it. 

---

> I noticed that `collaboratorOnly` is specified for `daemon-pricing`. Does this mean that only collaborators can set these labels? Contributors cannot set labels at all, which makes me wonder why we have this feature. The only way contributors can possibly set labels is if we have an issue template with it, but that is something we specificially need to go out of our way to set up so this is an unnecessary feature. https://github.com/ubiquity/.ubiquity-os/blob/development/.github/.ubiquity-os.config.yml#L49
> 
> ---
> ```json
> "collaboratorOnly": {
>   "default": false,
>   "description": "Whether the task is only available for collaborators to be assigned",
>   "type": "boolean"
> }
> ```
> 
> We also have `collaboratorOnly` for `command-start-stop`. So we should remove from `daemon-pricing`? https://github.com/ubiquity/.ubiquity-os/blob/development/.github/.ubiquity-os.config.yml#L19-L29
> 
> ---
> 
> I’m also curious about the utility of `optOut`. [^01^] Obviously I understand that it is a blacklist but why are we even blacklisting? https://github.com/ubiquity/.ubiquity-os/blob/development/.github/.ubiquity-os.config.yml#L382-L387

_Originally posted by @0x4007 in https://github.com/ubiquity/.ubiquity-os/pull/24_

[^01^]: ⚠ 70% possible duplicate - [Installer config file](https://www.github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/.ubiquity-os/issues/4#4)

I_kwDOKzVPS87SMEeQ.title: Migrate from Cloudflare Wrangler to pure Deno server
I_kwDOKzVPS87SMEeQ.body:
Replace Wrangler dev setup with a Deno server and port Pages Functions to Deno handlers.

- Remove Wrangler/Cloudflare config and dependencies
- Add Deno.serve static server and API routes
- Port /issue-scraper and /referral-manager to Deno
- Use Deno KV for referrals
- Update scripts and docs

Endpoints remain the same for the frontend.

I_kwDOKzVPS86sfm_K.title: New User Onboarding Flow
I_kwDOKzVPS86sfm_K.body:
with the addition of:

1. task gating
2. including already assigned tasks.

It seems that the directory may prove to be a frustrating experience for new developers on board and we need to fix this urgently. 

1. Ensure that assigned tasks are filtered out by default
2. Incorporate `command-start-stop` (either as a git submodule or expose a public API) and filter out tasks that can't be started by the logged in user. 
3. Write a proposal for how to include matchmaking results elegantly in this experience. 


---

I think we should fix the directory for this purpose by making new tasks. 

Maybe we should prioritize running `command-start-stop` client side and then later adding a section, or we can annotate those tiles with a color, for matchmaking tasks. 

_Originally posted by @0x4007 in https://github.com/ubiquity/work.ubq.fi/issues/205#issuecomment-2697235480_

I_kwDOKzVPS86qxEF2.title: Improve SEO
I_kwDOKzVPS86qxEF2.body:
We should definitely do some search engine optimization (SEO) for both `work.ubq.fi` and `devpool.directory`.  

1. For `work.ubq.fi` the title and description aren't properly rendered like in `devpool.directory`.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7e105360-5e0b-42fc-810c-007f157b1ed5)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4b317605-2c8b-43cb-bebe-02c3c945c4e4)

2. I don't find the current description good, we should target our audience: talents in blockchain. Perhaps something more invocative like: *Discover blockchain development jobs and opportunities in the DevPool network. Browse, apply, and connect with top Web3 projects at Ubiquity DAO.*.

3. Canonical tag is outdated, since `devpool.directory` has become the standard.

```html
<link rel="canonical" href="https://work.ubq.fi" />
```

4. Keyword targeting is bad, as in 2.

5. We should probably have one descriptive header `<h1>` within the `<body>`, even if not rendered.  [Reference](https://mangools.com/blog/h1-tag-seo/#:~:text=From%20the%20SEO%20perspective%2C%20the,page%20for%20certain%20search%20queries.)

6. Implement JSON-LD Schema Markup for better indexing, GPT suggested something like:
```
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "Organization",
  "name": "Ubiquity DAO",
  "url": "https://work.ubq.fi",
  "logo": "https://work.ubq.fi/favicon.svg",
  "description": "Find blockchain and Web3 development jobs in the DevPool Directory.",
  "sameAs": [
    "https://twitter.com/UbiquityDAO",
    "https://dao.ubq.fi"
  ]
}
</script>
```

7. Add `alt` attribute to all images and icons. This is important not only for SEO but accessibility.

I_kwDOKzVPS86saNoB.title: VSCode Extension
I_kwDOKzVPS86saNoB.body:
We should consider developing a VSCode extension for work.ubq.fi (or any platform for viewing issues with partners) to increase user engagement. While there are many logins on the platform, they aren't converting into active contributors working on tasks. By creating a VSCode extension, we can provide a more seamless and accessible way for developers to interact with tasks directly within their development environment, potentially increasing contributions and task completions.

Possible Extensions:
1. One-Click Issue Setup: Allow developers to easily set up and sync issues from work.ubq.fi directly in VSCode with minimal setup.
2. Task Management: View and update task statuses directly from VSCode.
3. Notifications: Get real-time updates on task changes, comments, or deadlines directly in the IDE.

I_kwDOKzVPS86h-1Jc.title: Search Adjustments
I_kwDOKzVPS86h-1Jc.body:
I was searching for https://devpool.directory/?search=top+up "top up" and its actually in the title of the task I was searching for. We need to increase the weight of the following:

1. task title
2. repository name
3. organization name

I think it could be interesting to prefix special operators to hone in for example imagine typing `@ubiquity/business-development` and then the UI would understand that you want to search only in the scope of that repository. This can be a shorthand hack instead of changing the url to `devpool.directory/ubiquity` but it would also make it clear that we are searching within a repository. Otherwise I suppose we can see if the search begins with a `/` and then it can signal that you are targeting the repository. 

In this case we can search `/business-development` and it would isolate the tasks from that repository. 

In conclusion we should 1. increase the weight of matching task titles and 2. we should allow these fine grained search filters by parsing the symbols `@` and `/` in the first character of the search.

I_kwDOKzVPS86kENEW.title: Show Available and In Progress Tasks
I_kwDOKzVPS86kENEW.body:
It seems that there are two popular use cases of this UI:

1. Finding new available tasks to work on
2. Finding any in progress tasks

I think that by default we should show only the available tasks and we should have a toggle, like a filter button, that shows all of the tasks

_Originally posted by @0x4007 in https://github.com/ubiquity/work.ubq.fi/issues/148#issuecomment-2556836744_

---

This also implies that we need to fix the label logic on the backend. 

I think that descriptive labels can be helpful for further filtering on the UI. 

Some ideas for states to consider:

- available
- assigned
- gating based on priority level or time 

However gating is more complex and should be handled as part of a separate task because it requires interpreting the command-start-stop configs, and the user authentication to determine whether they can start it or not.

I_kwDOKzVPS86IFOjJ.title: Empty directory page after logged in for the first time
I_kwDOKzVPS86IFOjJ.body:
**Summary**: When a newcomer logs in with GitHub authentication, the directory pool appears as an empty page. A screenshot is attached for reference:

![image](https://github.com/ubiquity/work.ubq.fi/assets/266219/05cbebbd-3271-4d9f-9fb9-eb19c1c651ca)

**Description**: Upon login, it seems that the page requests collaborator information for the logged-in user from the repository by calling `/repos/ubiquity/devpool-directory-private/collaborators/{username}` and receives a 404 NotFound result.

**Expected Behavior**: Even if the user is not a collaborator, the devpool listing should still be accessible on the listing page, similar to when the user is not logged in.

I_kwDOKzVPS86eKFs5.title: fix live reload
I_kwDOKzVPS86eKFs5.body:
We've recently changed this repo's `package.json` start command to use wrangler pages because of the referral tracker kv implementation. 

```json
"start": "wrangler pages dev --port 8080"
```

This killed the live reload feature, and https://github.com/cloudflare/workers-sdk/issues/4824 we have to pass in a directory after wrangler pages dev to hear changes from. However, passing in `./` wont work since it only reads inside the received directory (non-recursive).

I_kwDOKzVPS86bcZp6.title: Mobile Touch Event List Transparency
I_kwDOKzVPS86bcZp6.body:

When scrolling on mobile, after hitting one of the list items with my thumb, it makes that single list item "active" and fades out the rest, even after lifting up thumb.

I_kwDOKzVPS86bN6Xj.title: Allow a third click to disable sorting
I_kwDOKzVPS86bN6Xj.body:
Right now, once you click to sort issues by some criteria there is no way to unsort them. Usually apps have a three clicks cycle where first click sorts top to bottom, second: bottom to top and a third disables the sorting. Not having this is not harmful but it's a worthy detail. Also we could eventually implement a standard sorting (priority based or paid placement by our partners) and we would want to let the user see it more than once.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bda2629d-41c2-418e-a909-1cdb734409dc

I_kwDOKzVPS86ZzI9E.title: Add text filter query to URL and filter on render
I_kwDOKzVPS86ZzI9E.body: We should add a parameter to the URL according to the current "Text Filter" component's state. This will allow for people to share specific issues, organizations and repos. An example of how this could look is: `https://devpool.directory/?q=ubiquity-os-marketplace` where on page load a checker function should be called to set the text filter to be `ubiquity-os-marketplace` from the URL. The filter's state should be made persistent which it currently is not, this means that toggling between directory and proposals should keep the filter on.

I_kwDOKzVPS86XHD0Y.title: Progressive Web App
I_kwDOKzVPS86XHD0Y.body:
At this conference, there were several instances where I had bad signal, and I generally like to pitch by showing this interface. 

The problem is that it loads the issues after you load the page so it takes a moment and I'm staring at an empty screen essentially.

Turning this into a progressive Web app should allow us to instantly load the cached issues, even when off-line or disconnected

I_kwDOKzVPS86K52yA.title: Proposals/Request for Comment View (`rfc.ubq.fi`)
I_kwDOKzVPS86K52yA.body:
I've opened up a couple of proposals, essentially requests for comment. It could be useful to aggregate them onto a single view for ease-of-access to the DAO. Recent examples:

- https://github.com/ubiquity/.github/issues/111
- https://github.com/ubiquity/.github/issues/110

I imagine that we can find issues without pricing and populate them under the "proposals" view. 

1. Fork this repository, make `rfc.ubq.fi` repository
2. Fork `devpool-directory` repository, make `devpool-rfc` repository 
3. `rfc.ubq.fi` UI should load from `devpool-rfc` "backend"

### A Note on Forks

It seems that we have use cases for similar UI code but for different purposes. Perhaps it makes sense to fork and create new repos (leaderboard.ubq.fi, rfc.ubq.fi.)

In addition, we already have https://github.com/ubiquity/devpool-directory and https://github.com/ubiquity/devpool-directory-private. Ideally we could consolidate everything into a single aggregator repository, and collect even the github issues without pricing. That way we only need to maintain a single repository (instead of potentially three) for our "backend" of issues. 

For now lets just do another fork and then we can worry about consolidating them into a "single backend" in a future project.

I_kwDOKzVPS86ZN0tg.title: Remove Redundant Calls and Optimize Caching

I_kwDOKzVPS86ZdZu8.title: Move to Top of Page on Issue Load
I_kwDOKzVPS86ZdZu8.body:
If you scroll down at work.ubq.fi and then sort or toggle it will temporarily look like the page is blank, this happens because issues appear from top to bottom with that gradual popping animation. A simple scroll to top should fix it. I am creating an issue just for this to be documented and discussed.

How it currently looks:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a249e008-6de8-42f6-9518-4292c6325ef7

I_kwDOKzVPS86ZaonS.title: Text Filter Always Available
I_kwDOKzVPS86ZaonS.body: Due to limitations before we could not offer the text filter box for users who were not logged in. Restore this capability.

I_kwDOKzVPS86TaSGR.title: preview deployment auth
I_kwDOKzVPS86TaSGR.body:
The problem is that our preview deployments redirect us to the production portal and so the auth is not valid and we cannot actually test the deployment.

We use `Supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth` to "Login via GitHub". We can optionally pass a `redirectTo` in the options which may resolve this issue. Either generally redirecting back to the same URL it came from or strictly `work.ubq.fi` and a wildcard to match our preview deployment URLs.

I think it would be safer to restrict.

I_kwDOKzVPS86YyWhD.title: Task Gating & Filtered Task Display on UI
I_kwDOKzVPS86YyWhD.body:
When this is implemented, we should detect if the user is a collaborator and then only display what they are eligible to start on: 
- https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/issues/62

I think increasing coupling between this plugin and this UI is fine because otherwise it is not possible for external DAO contributors to work on tasks. I can't think of another way around this. 

1. Check if they are a collaborator of the org
2. If they are not, we need to parse the settings, possibly by exposing an API[^1^]
3. Filter out the tasks they are not eligible for from the UI. 


[^1^]: We can make a new endpoint on `command-start-stop` worker endpoint to POST an array of repository IDs (because this can be configured down to the repository level) and then read the config. Is this possible to read the repository configs from a plugin endpoint? Also should we use GET with a header for caching purposes?

I_kwDOKzVPS86YCpyv.title: Redo Cache System
I_kwDOKzVPS86YCpyv.body:
Now we compile all the issues (proposals and tasks) in a single json [here](https://github.com/ubiquity/devpool-directory/blob/development/devpool-issues.json).

This will solve the following problems:
1. rate limits/requirement for authentication
2. simplify cache

Load this instead of loading everything individually from the GitHub API

I_kwDOKzVPS86X8JsX.title: Loading Icon
I_kwDOKzVPS86X8JsX.body:
A loading icon that matches the style should appear when there are no rows displayed. There tends to be a delay when the page is initially loaded. I envision a white circular spinner.

- It should render in the center of the viewport
- It should be animated
- It should fade out when the rows begin to load. 
- It should timeout after 5-10 seconds and display some type of failure if there is an actual page load failure. 

![work ubq fi_ (1)](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3741cbc3-6f0f-4d24-8a8a-d3e67cd9f573)

I_kwDOKzVPS86X8s96.title: "Server Side" Compile Data
I_kwDOKzVPS86X8s96.body:
As we increase the amount of tasks and proposals being loaded, we need a more clever cache mechanism. I keep hitting rate limits and the app breaks. 

We can pre-render a json inside of DevPool directory git repo, similar to how we generate our total rewards json, in order to make it a single request for users to load everything. 

> Cache on my phone is not cooperating so it's hard to fully test but on narrow resolutions on my computer it worked as expected. 

Rate limits are more difficult to deal with now with the additional issues. We need to handle this. 

_Originally posted by @0x4007 in https://github.com/ubiquity/work.ubq.fi/pull/95#issuecomment-2375547827_

I_kwDOKzVPS85845GD.title: Internal Task Listing
I_kwDOKzVPS85845GD.body:
### Objective

List private tasks for organization collaborators.

1. fork devpool-directory to be devpool-directory-private
2. modify the code of private to get all our private repositories. 
3. check if the user is a member of the organization by querying the user profile details when they log in.
4. if they are a member, then display the private issues from devpool-directory-private

### Original Spec

I want to start a brief discussion/brainstorm around how we should handle this scenario:

Due to a tremendous amount of non-disclosure agreements signed related to card issuance, the card issuance repository has been made private for core team only. 

We have a new task which is of high priority (should be functional in time for fundraising in March) https://github.com/ubiquity/card-issuance/issues/34 

I think that the work.ubq.fi interface is the best for our team to find new tasks to work on, but it can only see public issues, as it loads all of them from the [devpool-directory's issues](https://github.com/ubiquity/devpool-directory/issues). 

https://github.com/ubiquity/work.ubq.fi/blob/69ca27e25a1670cb8c60925de0a670ea61349c36/src/home/fetch-github/fetch-issues-preview.ts#L16

Perhaps the directory can load every issue, including private issues? I'm not sure if its a privacy concern because it exposes the issue title, but without the issue body! Seems like a suboptimal solution but it is the most direct solution.

The issue body in the devpool-directory (mirror) is only a link to the source issue (which requires the user to have permission to view) however from our work.ubq.fi UI, we can use the user's authentication to fetch the issue contents.

I_kwDOKzVPS86I0fH5.title: Better offramping docs for new hunters
I_kwDOKzVPS86I0fH5.body:
I'll likely be left for dust shortly now that new hunters who seem like they'll give me a good run for my money are starting to drip into the devpool, I love to see it.

With that said, I came in knowing my way around Web3 having been in it for a year previous, so I was off to a good start. Jordan has cleared a couple of tasks and attempted to move his funds but unfortunately has lost that payout due to user error. He contacted me asking for assistance, and I gave as much as I could in his situation.

Every one of us has done it at one point or another, and lost funds due to a bad transfer/misunderstanding, no shade on Jordan at all, it's commonplace for those new to Web3.

<hr/>

This issue aims to set up a document/reference point that covers team-trusted/contributor-trusted methods in moving their funds from Permit > Fiat.

Core team methods would likely carry more weight than say my own methods, but I understand everyone may not be as comfortable detailing each step they take to off-ramp exactly, however a structured document with paths to follow and common problem solutions is 100% required. 

Jordan took it like a champ and put it down to user error, but this should not be an event that is easily repeatable for a new hunter. In this case, an attempt was made to bridge the assets cross-chain without actually using a bridge and so the funds were delivered to an inaccessible wallet on the same chain.

<hr/>

RFC @0x4007 @gentlementlegen @EresDev @sergfeldman @molecula451 @rndquu @devpanther

_original context https://github.com/ubiquity/pay.ubq.fi/issues/217#issuecomment-2066259371_

I_kwDOKzVPS86FFv46.title: Lower requested permissions on user registration
I_kwDOKzVPS86FFv46.body: When a new user registers to the website and logs in via GitHub, sensitive rights are requested by the app, which is `read / write` on all `public / private` repositories. This seems to be excessive and worrisome for newcomers as they would wonder why we need to write on their private repos. Ideally, we should just request for read permission on public repos. I do not know myself the reason of the request of such elevated rights, so it would be worth investigating. If elevated rights are indeed needed for core members or insiders of the organization, these rights should be requested specifically for these members not for every new user registering.

I_kwDOKzVPS86BDTne.title: GitHub Rate Limiting Modal on Load
I_kwDOKzVPS86BDTne.body:
There's a bug that shows the rate limit modal even after logging in. 

### Steps to Reproduce

1. get rate limited as not logged in user. 
2. press the login with github button
3. page automatically reloads with you signed in, but the rate limit modal still appears

at this step we would just refresh and it works fine, but I fear that this may not be intuitive for new users.

### Fix

Please make sure that the page waits to see if you're logged in before displaying the rate limit modal.

Notice: you can get rate limited while logged in so also make sure that this is handled.

I_kwDOKzVPS86C2XHy.title: GitHub Registration DevRel Tracker
I_kwDOKzVPS86C2XHy.body:
### Registration Tracker

- As we ramp up our recruiting efforts, we will provide incentives for developer relations managers to spread the word of the DevPool.
- A major incentive is to reward the DevRel responsible for onboarding any developers that we end up hiring. 
- I anticipate that they can coax and encourage the new candidate to finish one task to make it to getting paid. We may even be able to automate payment generation for the DevRel in the future!

### Scope

We should enable support for trackers i.e. `work.ubq.fi?devrel=diamondnegroni` and then when the developer signs up, we capture the dev and associate them with the devrel for future reference. Refer to https://github.com/ubiquity/work.ubq.fi/issues/30#issuecomment-2008148491 for implementation details. 

# Deadline = EthSeoul Conference 25 March 2024!

I_kwDOKzVPS859OAmX.title: New Cache Schema
I_kwDOKzVPS859OAmX.body:
###### Draft

There's several properties we must associate with issues in the context of displaying them correctly. 
1. `origin` - where the issue was loaded from `localStorage | octokit`
   - Any `octokit` origin issue (freshly loaded from the API) should have a visual indicator as being "new"
2. `fullIssueId` - we only load issue previews from `@ubiquity/devpool-directory/issues` and we need to associate the preview issue ID to the "full" issue ID that it points to. 

- I also think that we should immediately display from cache first, and then fetch updates in the background. 
- If an issue is no longer present according to octokit, we need to sync our localStorage (remove the issue) so that it does not display anymore.

I_kwDOKzVPS858bnJ9.title: Unreliable Organization Avatar Rendering
I_kwDOKzVPS858bnJ9.body:
There's a bug where the organization avatar images do not reliably display on each tile. The code is a bit messy around the state management of this so I expect this to be a pretty tricky problem to solve.

For me, when I first open up the UI, when the tasks are loaded from the cache they never render the images. However when I login with GitHub, they do render:

![work ubq fi_](https://github.com/ubiquity/devpool-directory-ui/assets/4975670/fc0a4220-edab-4179-869f-cac64d5c3b64)

I remember the original obstacle was related to rate limits (when not logged in, can only make something like 60 requests in a minute; this is problematic when there are 30+ issues (issue contents, and organization avatar is already two requests.) 

I tried implementing a cache system for issues (done, and well implemented) as well as the avatars (not well implemented.)

I_kwDON4Znbs7J83QL.title: Nudge to claim DEMO currency
I_kwDON4Znbs7J83QL.body:
> @whilefoo @mentlegen 
>

> 2. We need to update the flow to ask the user to register their wallet before starting the demo so that they can claim the rewards. And after the rewards are posted, we should nudge them to click the link and claim the reward.

I_kwDOQg3DRc7eFK4_.title: All Branches Supported for Previews
I_kwDOQg3DRc7eFK4_.body:
As of now, we have the ability to deploy and access production and preview deployments.                          i.e. `pay.ubq.fi` and `preview-pay.ubq.fi`

`preview-` is any non default branch deployment.

A useful upgrade will be to support any branch as the prefix instead of collapsing all of them into `preview-` prefixes. i.e. branch name `feat/widget` can deploy to `feat-widget-pay.ubq.fi` 

This may require creating a separate Deno deploy project. We MUST have some branch deletion detection, and it deletes the Deno project that corresponds to it to reduce pollution, and save on limits.

I_kwDOQg3DRc7bmuZa.title: Standardize Deno Deploy rollout for ubq.fi subdomains
I_kwDOQg3DRc7bmuZa.body:
Deploy the new reusable Deno Deploy workflow across the remaining ubq.fi subdomain sites so we can serve static builds via a shared serve.ts and org-inherited Supabase secrets. Track per-repo PRs under this issue for auditability.

Acceptance:
- Reusable workflow is referenced by each 404 subdomain repo with correct project names and entrypoints
- Static assets are served via the shared serve.ts and build outputs are deployed to Deno
- Org-level Supabase secrets and DENO_DEPLOY_TOKEN are used; no repo-specific secrets needed unless unique vars

Files:
- .github/workflows/deno-deploy-reusable.yml
- README.md

<!-- @ubiquity-os/daemon-merging update 2025-12-12T01:43:22.200Z -->
<!-- @ubiquity-os/text-vector-embeddings update 2025-12-12T01:49:39.151Z --> [^01^]

[^01^]: ⚠ 75% possible duplicate - [Deploy Hardening](https://www.github.com/ubiquity/deno-deploy-workflow/issues/2#2)

I_kwDOQg3DRc7dYQXG.title: Deploy Hardening
I_kwDOQg3DRc7dYQXG.body:
1. Probe and check static assets referenced in the index.html to verify successful deployment (once the core deploy logic is stable, this would suggest a problem in the project specific server/routing logic. Currently supports hardcoded but should be dynamic. 
   a. Check directly on deno.dev
   b. ~~Checkas well on ubq.fi which can help isolate problems in our router/dns (requires special cf token auth)~~ (cf seems to block github actions runners. We would need proxies etc)
2. GitHub Actions syntax linter, to ensure that any adjustments are valid syntax. 
3. Exponential dropoff for when Deno complains about too many concurrent deployments.

I_kwDOL-In887eC-dH.title: Allow user id and installation login
I_kwDOL-In887eC-dH.body:
Currently, it is not possible to use an installation token to use this endpoint. This is problematic because that means the bot cannot be used to call this endpoint.

For userId, only the user id number can be used. It would be convenient to also accept the user login to avoid having to always query the user account.

## What is to be done

- accept both id (`12345`) and login (`userLogin`) for the `userId` argument
- handle installation token for logged in requests to the endpoint

This issue also help solving https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugins-wishlist/issues/75

I_kwDOL-In887efB2U.title: Change the endpoint for `4xx` returns
I_kwDOL-In887efB2U.body:
When querying the endpoint, if a user can't be assigned to the issue on a `GET` it will return a 400 error which is not appropriate. It should be changed to a 200 with the response populated properly, saying the user can be assigned with the reason.

In the response, assigned issues should filter out archived repositories as well.

## What should be done
- [ ] change the `4xx` to `2xx` when appropriate
- [ ] filter out archived repositories from assigned issues

<!-- @ubiquity-os/text-vector-embeddings update 2025-12-16T02:47:21.651Z -->

I_kwDOL-In887aghjm.title: Intergrate Swagger and change limiter
I_kwDOL-In887aghjm.body:
mixed feeling about this, because users can be spamming the endpoint to overload it. It might be better to use the IP address to limit in this case, I guess we can change this within another PR.

I_kwDOL-In886tw_bl.title: Expose Public API Endpoint
I_kwDOL-In886tw_bl.body:
For interoperability with https://devpool.directory/ and automatic task matchmaking assignment, we must host and expose a public API endpoint using Deno Deploy.

## Requirements

### Directory
When users log in, they should see only tasks they can start, considering task gating constraints. Users assigned to too many tasks should still see all directory tasks.

### Matchmaking
The system must select the top candidate for task assignment. If the top candidate is saturated with tasks, it should failover to the next best candidate.

### API Specification
- **Request Parameters**:
  - `userId` (number)
  - `issueUrl` (string)
  - `mode` ("validate" or "execute")
  - `userAccessToken` (optional)

### Repository Access Validation
- Validate GitHub App installation on target repositories
- Handle cross-repository access
- Graceful permission failure handling in development

### Dependencies
- Requires awareness of repository/organization configuration (resolved via merged feature in issue #54)

## Remarks
The public API approach replaces the git submodule solution due to Octokit's Node.js dependencies being incompatible with browsers. This provides a unified solution for both kernel and external consumers.

I_kwDOL-In887ZCHRK.title: Allow multiple URLs for the `GET` endpoint
I_kwDOL-In887ZCHRK.body:
It would be beneficial if the `GET` was capable to handle multiple urls at once. A common use-case scenario will be that `devpool-directory` and `daemon-planner` will check what tasks can be started by a user, and would probably have to check for hundreds of them. Since you also added the rate-limit, we might hit it pretty quickly if we have to check one by one

_Originally posted by @gentlementlegen in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/issues/185#issuecomment-3545515273_

I_kwDOL-In887XwKKF.title: Re-open PR if re-assigned by admin
I_kwDOL-In887XwKKF.body:
Perhaps this is redundant but I've seen it a handful of times within the last few days.

- contributor submits PR
- user is ejected for deadlines or similar
- admin(s) decide to re-assign the contributor
- PR remains `closed` or it's re-opened and is stale due to commits pushed while `closed`
- in [this instance](https://github.com/ubiquity/stake.ubq.fi/pull/10) I'd expect to see the PR update automatically with the commits that the contributor pushed while `closed` (as is the case with [my own](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/pull/185) I suspect)

---

1. If an admin re-assigns the contributor, automatically re-open their most recent PR.
2. I'm unsure (able to test it with my own PR #185 when it gets re-opened) if pushing a single new commit after it's re-opened will update all missing commits. However, if it does, then we should consider automating this too.

---

Outcomes:

1. We improve reviewer QOL. They re-assign and don't have to deal with telling the user they need to push a fresh commit or open a new PR.
2. We improve DX by automating the process of pulling in the most recent commits from that branch without them needing to force a commit just to refresh the PR for reviewers.

I_kwDOL-In887FInz-.title: Permanent Disqualifications
I_kwDOL-In887FInz-.body:
We should leave our tasks open and available for recruiting purposes. However there are clearly some contributors that take more resources than they add. The system should restrict them from their ability to participate. 

We need a means to keep track of when the user is disqualified, and store a negative XP that corresponds with the wasted effort. [^01^]                         Perhaps we can store a "permit" with no permit details other than a negative amount. Not sure what makes the most sense given our current database architecture. 

The simplest would be to store the task amount as a negative, but ideally we should actually calculate the sum total of the rewards that should have been paid out. For example if a contributor took the review time and effort from five other team members, we should sum total their rewards and count it as a negative to the contributor if they get disqualified. 

When the user completes tasks successfully, we save their permit details in the database which can be used to calculate their positive XP.   

In the config of this plugin, we should be able to set minimum XP to start based on priority levels and/or time levels. 

Then we can do something like: 

```
p0: -2000
p1: -1000
p2: 0
p3: 1000
p4: 2000
p5: 3000
```

We also should check the account age or activity so that we can't easily be Sybil'd. For example, the GitHub account must have activity from over a year ago to contribute.

I_kwDOL-In887Iu6LA.title: Collaborator assignment
I_kwDOL-In887Iu6LA.body:
## What happened

I tried to assign myself with /start in this issue https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/personal-agent/issues/7 and I got the message saying `External contributors are not eligible for rewards at this time. We are preserving resources for core team only.`

Metadata:
```json
{
  "userRole": "collaborator",
  "price": 9,
  "userAllowedMaxPrice": -1,
  "issueNumber": 7,
  "caller": "start"
}
```
Metadata shows that I was correctly identified as a collaborator but the max allowed price is -1 which is not correct because the config is set to Infinity for collaborators:
```yaml
taskAccessControl:
  usdPriceMax:
    contributor: -1
    collaborator: Infinity
```

## Expected outcome

I should've been assigned because I'm member of the org / collaborator.

I_kwDOL-In886jLwQe.title: Improve Task Assignment Limit Handling
I_kwDOL-In886jLwQe.body:
**Problem**  
Contributors are blocked from assigning new tasks due to the current task limit, even when they’ve addressed all open review threads and are waiting on reviewers. This bottleneck discourages contributors and slows down productivity.

**Proposed Solution**  
1. Add logic to check if the assignee is the last commenter on all unresolved review threads.[^01^]  
2. Introduce a 24-hour timeout after the last comment before considering tasks "reviewer-lagged."  
3. Allow contributors to bypass the limit in such cases without affecting their task slots.

**Concerns**  
- Assignees might misuse this by "resolving" threads without addressing the issues.  
- Additional safeguards may be needed to ensure resolution integrity.

**References**  

- Original comment thread: [#100](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/issues/100#issuecomment-2535532258).




[^01^]: ⚠ 50% possible duplicate - [Improve Task Limiter](https://www.github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/issues/103#103)

I_kwDOL-In886s3dMD.title: Price Gating
I_kwDOL-In886s3dMD.body:
Allow price gating but the behavior should support ranges. 

To make it clear I envision that in the config I can specify 

```
priceMax:
  contributor: 200
```

Something like this. 

---

> > I feel like gating based on priority is suboptimal compared to gating based on time
> > 
> > <15 min <1 hour
> > 
> > We should reconfigure.
> 
> I agree we should definitely reconfigure. 
> 
> As an outsider, I feel that is still suboptimal. The longer the task estimate the higher the value and low value tasks sit for a long time usually.

Another idea is to gate based on price amount. Meaning high priority fast tasks and low priority long tasks might make sense for contributors to jump on. 

After a quick look at the directory I think 200 USD or less seem pretty straightforward for anybody to take on. 
 
_Originally posted by @0x4007 in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/issues/295#issuecomment-2703639325_

I_kwDOL-In8862fjkz.title: Determine the name of the function dynamically based on the repository's name and user login
I_kwDOL-In8862fjkz.body:
Ha yes makes sense. Instead of having it in the secrets we could also determine this from the repo name.

_Originally posted by @gentlementlegen in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/issues/161#issuecomment-2868748648_
            

Since the names of the function have to be unique even from different Azure instances (due to the uniqueness of the url) we should dynamically generate that url so there is no collision, or at least have a way to override it if necessary.

I_kwDOL-In886mtCT8.title: Do not close pull-requests linked to an issue with no price tag
I_kwDOL-In886mtCT8.body:
I think it basically should be disabled in respect to automations for normal GitHub issues without pricing. Then it won't get in the way for partners easing into using the system

_Originally posted by @0x4007 in https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-kernel/issues/234#issuecomment-2599513665_
            

## What happened

The pull-request that was linked to the issue got closed because the related issue had no price tag set.

## What was expected

The pull-request should not get closed when the price label is missing.

## How to reproduce

- create an issue with no price tag, only time estimate for example
- link a pull-request that would close it (Resolves #)
- notice that the pull-request gets closed by the bot

I_kwDOL-In886o6KKP.body:

What happens is that the plugin properly recognizes that the task cannot be started ("`⚠ This task does not reflect a business priority at the moment. You may start tasks with one of the following labels: Priority: 3 (High), Priority: 4 (Urgent), Priority: 5 (Emergency)`") but fails to post that comment because the starting event comes from the pull-request and not the issue, thus failing with `› Cannot post comment because issue is not found in the payload`. This comment should be posted otherwise users could think that the plugin is not working properly.

## What is to be done

- add related tests

I_kwDOL-In886h0Dyw.title: Add allowed roles to `requiredLabelsToStart`
I_kwDOL-In886h0Dyw.body:
There's the [requiredLabelsToStart](https://github.com/ubiquity/.ubiquity-os/blob/cde4662181090201af15fa2412b8fe2c60dc7f31/.github/.ubiquity-os.config.yml#L18) property which works this way:
1. User prints the `/start` command
2. https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop plugin checks that if the github issue (which user wants to self assign) contains at least 1 label from the `requiredLabelsToStart` config property then the plugin allows self assigning a github issue

We need a more sophisticated control over that parameter and want to support these use cases:
1. Collaborators (i.e. organization members) should be allowed to self assign only high priority tasks with longer time estimates
2. Outside contributors should be allowed to self assign only low priority tasks with lower time estimates

What should be done:
1. Refactor the https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop plugin to support specific roles for specific labels
2. Add unit tests for the features

Expected config (example):
```
        requiredLabelsToStart:
          time:
            - name: "Time: <1 Hour"
              roles:
              	- MEMBER
                - OUTSIDE_CONTRIBUTOR
            - name: "Time: <2 Hours"
              roles:
              	- MEMBER
                - OUTSIDE_CONTRIBUTOR
            - name: "Time: <4 Hours"
              roles:
                - MEMBER
            - name: "Time: <1 Day"
              roles:
                - MEMBER
            - name: "Time: <1 Week"
              roles:
                - MEMBER
          priority:
            - name: "Priority: 1 (Normal)"
              roles:
                - OUTSIDE_CONTRIBUTOR
            - name: "Priority: 2 (Medium)"
               roles:
                 - OUTSIDE_CONTRIBUTOR
            - name: "Priority: 3 (High)"
              roles:
                - MEMBER
            - name: "Priority: 4 (Urgent)"
              roles:
                - MEMBER
            - name: "Priority: 5 (Emergency)"
              roles:
                - MEMBER
```

P.S. Not sure if the `OUTSIDE_CONTRIBUTOR` role exists in the github API but there should be something similar

I_kwDOL-In886UYgdZ.title: Properly handle task limit
I_kwDOL-In886UYgdZ.body:
The original implementation allowed for productive contributors to work on two tasks concurrently. With a starting two task limit:
| Condition                                              | Task Adjustment       | Explanation                                                                                             |
|--------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| If any reviewer approved the pull                      | +1 task per pull      | It's likely that the pull is good, just waiting on other slow reviewers to confirm.                      |
| If reviewers take longer than 24 hours                 | +1 task per pull      | Don't wait for lazy reviewers to be able to start a new task.                                            |
| If any reviewer requested changes                      | -1 task per pull      | They should focus on addressing the changes as a top priority.                                           |

_Originally posted by @0x4007 in https://github.com/ubiquibot/command-start-stop/pull/19#discussion_r1731441502_
            

For the sake of this conversation a completed PR is when the conditions are met in the above table.

In the current state the code checks if any review is approved or if there is 0 reviews but 24 hours have passed since the creation of the PR, however it doesn't check if there's any requested changes so it will count it as completed even if there 1 approve and 1 requested changes.

There is still a problem when the reviewer requests changes and the contributor resolves those changes but waits for the reviewer to make a new review so they can't start another task.
There's two possible solutions: check if the reviewer was requested more than x hours ago or check that changes have been marked as solved

I_kwDOL-In886dZXe0.title: New config property `requiredLabelsToStart`
I_kwDOL-In886dZXe0.body:
Right now our main goal is to show investors that the project is capable of generating a revenue. 

So basically we need to concentrate on tasks related to:
1. Partner onboarding (i.e. [UbiquityOS Marketplace GUI](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-plugin-installer))
2. Charging fees
3. Making the kernel stable
4. Making core plugins stable

We have the following organizational issues right now:
1. All core team members are doing everything with a lack of focus. 
2. There's too much PRs to review (from outside contributors) and none-important issues (that don't "move the needle") to discuss.

For example check these issues:
- https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugin-sdk/issues/34
- https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-kernel/issues/154

Although both of the issues above have the "Priority: 3 (High)" label in reality their business value is 1 (on a scale from 1 to 4). **The "Priority" label must reflect a business priority, not a development one.**

As a part of this issue we should add a new property `requiredLabelsToStart` which accepts an array of labels required to call the `/start` command.

Example config:
```
- plugin: http://localhost:4000 # or the URL where the plugin is hosted
  name: start-stop
  id: start-stop-command
  command: "\/start|\/stop"
  with:
    requiredLabelsToStart: ["Priority: 3 (High)", "Priority: 4 (Urgent)", "Priority: 5 (Emergency)"]
    # ...other props
```

In the example above it will be possible to start an issue only if it has required business priority labels.

When current issue is finished we'll:
1. Review all github issues and update the "Priority" label to reflect a business value
2. Set `requiredLabelsToStart: ["Priority: 3 (High)", "Priority: 4 (Urgent)", "Priority: 5 (Emergency)"]` for all of our organizations

This way we:
1. Shift core team focus only on issues that have a business impact
2. Reduce number of PRs to review and issues to discuss (because outside contributors will have to solve only high business priority issues)
3. Save money and time only to what matters

I_kwDOL-In886YkJTH.title: Collaborator Gating Based On Label
I_kwDOL-In886YkJTH.body:
- Since we still are ways off to implementing XP, we should implement a simpler gating system. 
- Similar to how we manage max concurrent tasks per role, we should also have a config to gate based on the label. 
- There's no point in making a config for admin.

```yml
  - uses:
    - plugin: https://ubiquibot-assistive-pricing-development.ubiquity.workers.dev
      with:
        labels:
          time:
            - "Time: <1 Hour"
              collaboratorOnly: false
            - "Time: <2 Hours"
              collaboratorOnly: false
            - "Time: <4 Hours"
              collaboratorOnly: true
            - "Time: <1 Day"
              collaboratorOnly: true
            - "Time: <1 Week"
              collaboratorOnly: true
            - "Time: <1 Month"
              collaboratorOnly: true
          priority:
            - "Priority: 1 (Normal)"
              collaboratorOnly: false
            - "Priority: 2 (Medium)"
              collaboratorOnly: false
            - "Priority: 3 (High)"
              collaboratorOnly: true
            - "Priority: 4 (Urgent)"
              collaboratorOnly: true
            - "Priority: 5 (Emergency)"
              collaboratorOnly: true
        basePriceMultiplier: 2
        publicAccessControl:
          setLabel: true
          fundExternalClosedIssue: false
```

We could even embed metadata in the label description, although for this purpose maybe via the config here is simple enough.

I_kwDOL-In886WpJuh.title: Fix default schema values
I_kwDOL-In886WpJuh.body:
It appears to me that the logic to decode the schema is wrong, and this would be introduced by Jordan's PR addition regarding the configuration accepting the amount of parallel tasks allowed for each user. If not provided, the default configuration fails which is why it is failing in your last try, on ubiquity / ubiquibot orgs, but not on mine where I provide the full config. Working on a fix right now.

_Originally posted by @gentlementlegen in https://github.com/ubiquibot/permit-generation/issues/60#issuecomment-2351912070_
            

The default values when not provided via the config are:
```
{ admin: Infinity, member: 10, contributor: 2 }
```
`Infinity` does not satisfy `T.Record(T.String(), T.Integer())` since it is not an integer value.

Tasks to be carried out:
- properly log the errors so it is easier to identify
- add a test related to the default configuration if none
- fix the default values

A possible cause would be that value missing in the current configuration. If that is the case, the default threshold should be set, and definitely above 0 days.

- do not display the warning if it is under that threshold

- related tests

I_kwDOL-In886S-JFl.title: New text config param for empty wallet
I_kwDOL-In886S-JFl.body:
Depends on https://github.com/ubiquity/safe.ubq.fi/issues/3

We're going to have 2 registration flows:
1. Our usual flow via the `/wallet` command
2. A new one via webauthn and `safe.ubq.fi` ([example](https://github.com/ubiquibot/plugins-wishlist/issues/35#issue-2465056368))

The `command-start-stop` plugin displays a warning message when `startRequiresWallet = true` and user doesn't have a registered wallet:
```
Please set your wallet address with the /wallet command first and try again.
``` 

Since we're going to have 2 flows the message above is not applicable for the "webauthn" flow. Possible solution could be to add a new plugin option for the warning message like `emptyWalletText`.

I_kwDOOG47587ZF5zn.title: Include PR context
I_kwDOOG47587ZF5zn.body:
> Could you can try running `/rewrite` on https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/issues/154 please?

> It meets all the criteria for it, and would be great to see it in action. (My concern is that it does not factor in the PR or any code at all, which is a huge amount of missing context as it's unreasonable to expect all technical details to be mentioned in the conversation but their acceptance by reviewers is specification discovery in and of itself)


As a contributor who over the years has often taken on ambiguous specs and saw them through to completion, it has been my experience that, oftentimes a lot of ambiguous specs shift product-design and engineering effort onto contributors and it often goes unrecognized the amount of work that gets put in.

For this plugin to work as best as it can it must also have context of the active PR because, in my experience, a lot of what I implement gets accepted without it being mentioned in the spec/issue convo. It only appears in conversation through review if it needs tweaked or rejected so this means:

- anything off-spec which is undiscussed (an inherent acceptance of that deliverable) is unknown to the llm
- it does not surface reviews or PR comments

Finally, it's unreasonable to expect (I think) contributors who have done the extra product-design and engineering work to list all tech specs of their implementation into the issue, before then calling `/rewrite`. Better UX if the plugin/agent handles this themselves.

One recent example (although there are tens more I could think of):

- https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/issues/154 - barren, very little activity, no discussion around my suggestions which have all been accepted at this point
- https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/pull/185 - context rich, laden with activity, huge amount of undiscussed technical details that have been accepted, plenty of changes requested by reviewers

^ All the llm gets to see is an empty conversation spanning back to March, then my impl details being ignored (so it likely won't (or shouldn't) accept them as new spec details), while the PR continues to move.


 _Originally posted by @Keyrxng in [#448](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/issues/448#issuecomment-3552015169)_

I_kwDOPpQwy87PCfJ7.title: WalletConnect/Reown Integration – Handoff
I_kwDOPpQwy87PCfJ7.body:
# WalletConnect/Reown Integration – Handoff

Objective
- Implement wallet connection UX using WalletConnect/Reown only (no Coinbase SDK, no extra connector stacks) on top of wagmi v2 + viem v2; ensure parity across mainnet and local anvil (31337) modes.

Context
- Repo: `stake.ubq.fi` (branch `pr-1`)
- Stack: React 19, wagmi 2.x, viem 2.x, TanStack Query 5
- Modes: `dev` (public RPC), `local-node` (anvil on 31337), `prod`

Deliverables
- Integrate Reown (WalletConnect) as the sole wallet provider:
  - Use `@reown/appkit` (+ minimal controllers if needed) with wagmi v2 + viem v2.
  - Read `VITE_WALLETCONNECT_PROJECT_ID` from env; if missing, hide WalletConnect UI and show a simple disabled state with guidance.
  - If Reown offers injected/browser support via the same stack, enable it through Reown only (do not add separate injected connectors).
- A `ConnectWalletButton` component with states (disconnected, connecting, connected) and chain awareness, powered by Reown.
- Auto-chain handling:
  - `local-node` mode: support 31337 and do not attempt chain ID suffixing on RPC URLs.
  - dev/prod: support mainnet; prevent auto-switching without user consent.
- Persist connection state (wagmi default).

Acceptance Criteria
- In `dev` mode with Reown configured, `Connect` → shows account and chain; disconnect works.
- In `local-node` mode with anvil running, able to connect and read chain `31337`.
- Only Reown/WalletConnect UI is present; Coinbase and other stacks are not included.
- No blocking console errors; network calls respect `src/constants/config.ts` RPC behavior.

Constraints & Guidance
- Keep bundle size in mind; lazy load Reown UI if possible.
- Avoid adding large UI kits; implement a small, styled button consistent with existing CSS.
- Do not hardcode secrets; rely on `VITE_*` env vars.

Suggested Steps
1) Add Reown packages: `@reown/appkit` (and controllers if needed) and WalletConnect provider as required by Reown docs.
2) Create `src/wallet/config.ts` to centralize wagmi + viem + Reown configuration and chains.
3) Implement `ConnectWalletButton` under `src/components/connect-wallet.tsx` using Reown hooks/APIs.
4) Wire into `src/App.tsx` top bar or dashboard header.
5) Manually test dev and local-node modes.

Validation
- Commands:
  - Dev: `bun run dev` with `VITE_WALLETCONNECT_PROJECT_ID` set → connect via Reown.
  - Local: run anvil + `bun run local` → connect on 31337 via Reown.

Out of Scope
- Full transaction flow QA (approve/stake/claim); addressed separately.

I_kwDOPpQwy87PCfLM.title: Bundle Performance Split – Handoff
I_kwDOPpQwy87PCfLM.body:
# Bundle Performance Split – Handoff

Objective
- Reduce the main JS bundle size via code‑splitting and vendor chunking while preserving current functionality.

Context
- Build produced a large main chunk (~543 kB minified; see build output).
- Heavy deps likely include wallet/connectors and query libs.

Deliverables
- Vite/Rollup configuration to split vendor chunks (e.g., `manualChunks`) for wallet/connectors and other heavy libs.
- Lazy load connector UI and other rarely used routes/components.
- A short report in this file (append at bottom) with the before/after sizes from `bun run build`.

Acceptance Criteria
- Main application chunk (non-vendor) gzip size reduced meaningfully (target < 140 kB gz if feasible; any significant reduction acceptable if functionality unchanged).
- App still builds and runs (`bun run dev` and `bun run build` both OK).
- No broken dynamic imports.

Constraints & Guidance
- Do not regress DX: dev server should remain fast.
- Be careful with CSS splitting to avoid FOUC; keep existing styles intact.
- Avoid over‑engineering; start by lifting large deps into separate vendor chunks.

Suggested Steps
1) Inspect `node_modules` imports via `vite-bundle-visualizer` or similar (optional).
2) Update `vite.config.ts` → `build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks` to separate:
   - `react` + `react-dom`
   - `@tanstack/react-query`
   - `wagmi` + `viem` + connectors (walletconnect/coinbase)
3) Convert wallet UI to lazy import where appropriate.
4) Rebuild and record sizes in this document.

Validation
- Commands:
  - `bun run build`
  - Confirm chunk list and sizes in terminal output.

Report (fill in)
- Before: main chunk gzip size ≈ 168 kB (from prior build).
- After: main chunk gzip size ≈ ___ kB.
- Notes: ____.

I_kwDOPpQwy87PCfIz.title: CI (GitHub Actions) – Handoff
I_kwDOPpQwy87PCfIz.body:
# CI (GitHub Actions) – Handoff

Objective
- Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs lint, unit tests (Bun test), build, and E2E smoke (Playwright/preview) on PRs and pushes to `development` and `main`.

Context
- Repo: `stake.ubq.fi` (branch `pr-1`)
- Tooling: Bun, Vite, ESLint, Vitest (new), Playwright (new)

- Workflow at `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with jobs:
  - `lint`: install + `bun run lint`.
  - `unit`: install + `bun test`.
  - `build`: install + `bun run build` cache artifacts.
  - `e2e`: depends on `build`, serves preview (`vite preview`), runs `bunx playwright test` (Chromium only).
- Bun setup using `oven-sh/setup-bun@v1`.
- Node version pinned to `22.12.x` or newer `22.x`.
- Cache `~/.cache/bun` and repo `node_modules/.bun-*` automatically (action handles).

Acceptance Criteria
- CI runs on PRs and push to `development` and `main`.
- All steps green on the current branch.
- E2E job starts preview server and hits base URL successfully.

Constraints & Guidance
- Keep workflow readable and < 150 lines.
- Fail fast: unused matrix entries or browsers not required.
- Use `if: always()` only for uploading artifacts on failure when helpful.

Suggested Steps
1) Add `oven-sh/setup-bun@v1` and `actions/setup-node@v4`.
2) Split jobs and set `needs` for ordering.
3) Use `bun install --frozen-lockfile` if lock constraints enforced.
4) Add `PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=0` only if downloading browsers in CI; otherwise rely on `npx playwright install --with-deps chromium`.
5) Gate E2E with a short timeout for server readiness.

Validation
- Open a PR; confirm 4 jobs run and pass.

Out of scope
- No Docker use.

I_kwDOPpQwy87PCfMN.title: Error Handling & Status Toasts – Handoff
I_kwDOPpQwy87PCfMN.body:
# Error Handling & Status Toasts – Handoff

Objective
- Implement consistent error/success handling and user feedback via lightweight toasts/snackbars across key flows (RPC read failures, approve/stake actions, reward claim), without introducing large UI kits.

Context
- Repo: `stake.ubq.fi` (branch `pr-1`)
- Existing context provider: `src/context/status-message.tsx` (extend or replace with a small toast system)
- Styles live under `src/css/*`; maintain visual consistency.

Deliverables
- A minimal toast system (provider + hook) with variants: `info`, `success`, `error`.
- Wrap top‑level app with the provider; expose `useToast()` for components.
- Standardize error surfaces:
  - Network/RPC errors (query layer) → show succinct error with retry guidance.
  - Transaction flows (approve/stake/claim) → show pending → success/failure.
- Ensure messages collapse duplicates and auto‑dismiss after 4–6s (except persistent critical errors).

Acceptance Criteria
- Clear, non‑noisy toasts appear for simulated failures (e.g., forced RPC error) and success flows.
- No console spam or unhandled promise rejections.
- Visual style aligns with existing CSS; no heavyweight UI library added.

Constraints & Guidance
- Reuse TanStack Query error boundaries/`onError` handlers where possible.
- Avoid large dependencies; implement ~100–200 lines max for the core toast system.
- Keep i18n/out‑of‑scope polish minimal; focus on correctness and UX clarity.

Suggested Steps
1) Create `src/ui/toast.tsx` (provider, portal, hook) and `src/css/toast.css`.
2) Wrap `<App />` with provider in `src/main.tsx`.
3) Wire calls in strategic places (pool actions, fetches) and consolidate error shapes.
4) Add a few unit tests around the toast hook with Bun test.

Validation
- Dev: `bun run dev`, manually trigger success/error; verify toasts.
- Unit: `bun test` for hook behavior and dedup logic.

Out of Scope
- Accessibility/a11y polish beyond basic focus and readability.

I_kwDOPpQwy87PCfHX.title: E2E Smoke (Playwright) – Handoff
I_kwDOPpQwy87PCfHX.body:
# E2E Smoke (Playwright) – Handoff

Objective
- Add a minimal Playwright E2E suite that validates the app loads, renders the home view, and shows a WalletConnect/Reown connect‑wallet entry point in both `dev` and preview modes.

Context
- Repo: `stake.ubq.fi` (branch `pr-1`)
- Dev: `bun run dev` (http://localhost:5173)
- Preview: `bun run build` then serve `dist/` via `vite preview`

Deliverables
- Playwright installed and configured for Chromium only (keep it small to start).
- Two tests:
  - `loads-homepage`: Page title contains “Stake” or app brand and root content renders.
  - `connect-wallet-visible`: A visible WalletConnect/Reown connect trigger (e.g., a button named “Connect”) is present; do not require an actual wallet connection.
- Scripts in `package.json`:
  - `e2e`: `playwright test`
  - `e2e:ui`: `playwright test --ui`
  - `preview`: `vite preview --port 4173`
- Helper to auto-start server:
  - For dev: launch `bun run dev` before tests, wait for `http://localhost:5173`.
  - For preview: `bun run build` then `bunx vite preview` before tests, wait for `http://localhost:4173`.

Acceptance Criteria
- `bunx playwright test` passes locally against preview (build + preview).
- Tests run headless in CI and skip if port unavailable (clear error message).
- No dependence on wallets, RPC keys, or network calls.

Constraints & Guidance
- Prefer using a test fixture that starts/stops the server.
- Use a generous timeout on server startup (e.g., 20–30s).
- Avoid brittle selectors; match on role/name for the connect button (WalletConnect/Reown UI). Do not include Coinbase‑specific selectors.

Suggested Steps
1) Add devDeps: `@playwright/test`.
2) Generate minimal config `playwright.config.ts` with baseURL switching via env (`E2E_MODE=dev|preview`).
3) Create `tests/e2e/*.spec.ts` with two smoke tests.
4) Implement lightweight server launcher in `tests/e2e/fixtures/server.ts` (child_process + wait-on).
5) Add scripts and document how to run both modes in repo README or a new `docs/testing.md`.

Validation
- Commands:
  - Dev: `E2E_MODE=dev bunx playwright test`
  - Preview: `E2E_MODE=preview bunx playwright test`

Out of Scope
- Wallet interactions or chain forking/anvil; that belongs to a separate task.

I_kwDOPpQwy87PCfFw.title: Unit Tests (Bun Test) – Handoff
I_kwDOPpQwy87PCfFw.body:
# Unit Tests (Bun Test) – Handoff

Objective
- Establish a minimal, fast unit test baseline using Bun’s built‑in test runner.

Context
- Repo: `stake.ubq.fi` (branch `pr-1`)
- Stack: React 19, Vite 7, TS 5, wagmi 2, viem 2, TanStack Query 5
- Target modules: `src/constants/config.ts`, `src/components/button.tsx`, `src/components/pool-display.tsx`

Deliverables
- Bun test configured (jsdom) and runnable via:
  - `bun test`
  - `bun test --watch`
- At least 3 tests:
  - Config resolution logic in `src/constants/config.ts` (RPC selection per mode/env).
  - Render test for `src/components/button.tsx` (props/disabled state/class).
  - Pure UI/data mapping test for `src/components/pool-display.tsx` with minimal props.

Acceptance Criteria
- `bun test` exits 0 locally.
- Tests are deterministic; no network, wallet, or RPC needed.
- Production behavior unchanged.

Constraints & Guidance
- Use Bun’s `test`, `expect`, and `mock` APIs; avoid extra test libs unless strictly necessary.
- For React component tests, use `@testing-library/react` and `@testing-library/jest-dom` for assertions.
- Ensure a DOM‑like environment (Bun provides a browser‑compatible runtime); if jsdom is preferred, configure via preload/setup.
- Keep tests colocated under `src/**/__tests__/*` and each file < 150 lines.

Suggested Steps
1) Add devDeps: `@testing-library/react`, `@testing-library/jest-dom`.
2) Create `test/setup.ts` to register `@testing-library/jest-dom` and set up any required DOM shims.
3) Write 3 tests covering config, button, and pool-display.
4) Add `"test": "bun test"` to `package.json` scripts.

Validation
- Commands:
  - `cd stake.ubq.fi && bun install`
  - `bun test`

File Pointers
- `stake.ubq.fi/src/constants/config.ts`
- `stake.ubq.fi/src/components/button.tsx`
- `stake.ubq.fi/src/components/pool-display.tsx`

Out of Scope
- E2E flows, wallet interactions, or anvil integration.

I_kwDOPpQwy87PCfNt.title: RPC Robustness & Fallback – Handoff
I_kwDOPpQwy87PCfNt.body:
# RPC Robustness & Fallback – Handoff

Objective
- Harden RPC configuration and runtime behavior: validate env resolution, add fallbacks, timeouts, and basic health checks to reduce flaky UX.

Context
- Repo: `stake.ubq.fi` (branch `pr-1`)
- Current rules documented in README and implemented in `src/constants/config.ts`.
- Modes: `dev` (public RPC), `local-node` (http://localhost:8545, chain 31337), `prod` (relative `/rpc`).

Deliverables
- Strengthen `src/constants/config.ts`:
  - Validate `VITE_RPC_URL` format; surface clear console warnings when misconfigured.
  - Implement a small fallback list for mainnet (e.g., `https://rpc.ubq.fi`) when the primary URL fails a quick HEAD/eth_chainId query.
  - Respect `local-node` mode with no chain suffixing.
- Add a lightweight `rpcHealthCheck()` utility to test `eth_chainId` with a short timeout (e.g., 1–2s) and return typed status.
- Optional: a diagnostic panel in dev builds to display detected RPC, chainId, and recent error counts (hidden in prod).

Acceptance Criteria
- In dev mode with an invalid `VITE_RPC_URL`, app logs a clear warning and falls back to a known-good endpoint (if allowed by the mode rules).
- In local-node mode, health check targets `http://localhost:8545` and indicates unavailable state if anvil is not running, without crashing the app.
- No breaking changes to existing consumers; exported config remains backward compatible.

Constraints & Guidance
- Avoid introducing heavy deps; use native fetch with abort/timeout.
- Keep logic well‑typed and small; unit test the key branches with Bun test.

Suggested Steps
1) Update `src/constants/config.ts` with validation and fallback logic.
2) Create `src/utils/rpc-health.ts` with `rpcHealthCheck()` using `eth_chainId`.
3) Add unit tests for `dev`, `local-node`, and `prod` behaviors.
4) Optionally add a small dev‑only panel in `src/components/dev-rpc-panel.tsx`.

Validation
- Unit: `bun test` passes for config and health utilities.
- Dev: `bun run dev` with broken URL shows graceful fallback or readable error.

I_kwDOJGfp8c6ftbnV.title: ci(empty-string-checker): Resource not accessible by integration
I_kwDOJGfp8c6ftbnV.body:
Check [this](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-plugin-installer/actions/runs/11951850176/job/33316343058) failing empty string check CI run which fails with the following error:
```
Error: An error occurred: Resource not accessible by integration - https://docs.github.com/rest/checks/runs#create-a-check-run
```

The [pull_request](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-plugin-installer/blob/6ad2d0abb069113a2a96313b8c4def3d9252f673/.github/workflows/no-empty-strings.yml#L4) trigger runs in the context of a forked repository. In order to use the [checks.create()](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-plugin-installer/blob/6ad2d0abb069113a2a96313b8c4def3d9252f673/.github/empty-string-checker.ts#L49) API the workflow must use the `checks: write` [permission](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/checks/runs?apiVersion=2022-11-28#create-a-check-run). But forked instances can [only have](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-for-github-actions/security-guides/automatic-token-authentication#permissions-for-the-github_token) the `read` permission.

What should be done:
- refactor [no-empty-strings.yml](https://github.com/ubiquity/ts-template/blob/7b94011f7e080380dd5517db6a0b80ec6d5ae965/.github/workflows/no-empty-strings.yml) to run on `workflow_run` (the same way as [knip.yml](https://github.com/ubiquity/ts-template/blob/7b94011f7e080380dd5517db6a0b80ec6d5ae965/.github/workflows/knip.yml) and [knip-reporter.yml](https://github.com/ubiquity/ts-template/blob/7b94011f7e080380dd5517db6a0b80ec6d5ae965/.github/workflows/knip-reporter.yml)) work

I_kwDOJGfp8c6Y8HKW.title: Automatic sync v2
I_kwDOJGfp8c6Y8HKW.body:
[This](https://github.com/ubiquity/ts-template/pull/56) PR introduced a [workflow](https://github.com/zyrafal/ts-template/blob/89b942ed3a2d87c00f2bf61057586e0ead5119d3/.github/workflows/sync-template.yml) for syncing https://github.com/ubiquity/ts-template repository template with all of our other repositories.

There are a couple of issues:
1. It doesn't work as expected. Check [this](https://github.com/ubiquity/work.ubq.fi/pull/101) PR for example which:
  a) [Clears](https://github.com/ubiquity/work.ubq.fi/pull/101/files#diff-3e8602c86d6cf04a5c74b954f9f957b652617102cf8f4b647fe46989f480861d) the main page
  b) [Removes](https://github.com/ubiquity/work.ubq.fi/pull/101/files#diff-7ae45ad102eab3b6d7e7896acd08c427a9b25b346470d7bc6507b6481575d519) required dependencies
2. For "auto sync" to work we need to keep the [sync-template.yml](https://github.com/ubiquity/audit.ubq.fi/blob/205cc736abac8019debc28ab2d4a2d21344d3cef/.github/workflows/sync-template.yml) in all of our repositories

"Auto sync" may be useful for small changes like CI updates, ts config edits, etc... [Such](https://github.com/ubiquity/work.ubq.fi/pull/101) PRs introduce too many changes which are hard to debug and fix.

What should be done:
1. Remove [sync-template.yml](https://github.com/ubiquity/ts-template/blob/858ab74ead68d8954a8e32074c9b0eb805c5b6ca/.github/workflows/sync-template.yml) from all of our repositories and keep it only in a single place like https://github.com/ubiquity/.github
2. Refactor [sync-template.yml](https://github.com/ubiquity/ts-template/blob/858ab74ead68d8954a8e32074c9b0eb805c5b6ca/.github/workflows/sync-template.yml) to sync **only whitelisted files** (like `deploy.yml` or `tsconfig.json`) with the following strategy:
  - `ts-template`: file added, `target-repo`: file missing => propose adding a file
  - `ts-template`: file modified, `target-repo`: file exists => propose change
  - `ts-template`: file deleted, `target-repo`: file exists => propose delete

P.S. For authentication you may use github PAT of this user https://github.com/ubiquity-devpool

I_kwDOJGfp8c6ivSsY.title: Better template structure
I_kwDOJGfp8c6ivSsY.body:
Based on [this](https://github.com/ubiquity/ts-template/issues/67) discussion and [this](https://github.com/ubiquity/uusd.ubq.fi/pull/13) PR we agree that right now the https://github.com/ubiquity/ts-template lacks structure and complex UIs (like `pay.ubq.fi`) are turned into a mess.

Since we don't want to use a js framework it makes sense at least to structure https://github.com/ubiquity/ts-template properly.

As as part of this issue the https://github.com/ubiquity/ts-template should be modified this way:
1. Add 2 separate html pages ([example1](https://github.com/zugdev/uusd.ubq.fi/blob/f4c6623cec130cff75e299ca47f0a45b63ace819/static/home.html), [example2](https://github.com/zugdev/uusd.ubq.fi/blob/f4c6623cec130cff75e299ca47f0a45b63ace819/static/mint.html))
2. Add 2 separate js "controllers" for those html pages ([example1](https://github.com/zugdev/uusd.ubq.fi/blob/f4c6623cec130cff75e299ca47f0a45b63ace819/src/pages/home.ts), [example2](https://github.com/zugdev/uusd.ubq.fi/blob/f4c6623cec130cff75e299ca47f0a45b63ace819/src/pages/mint.ts))
3. Add 2 reusable js components ([example](https://github.com/zugdev/uusd.ubq.fi/blob/f4c6623cec130cff75e299ca47f0a45b63ace819/src/common/collateral.ts))
4. Add a router ([example](https://github.com/zugdev/uusd.ubq.fi/blob/f4c6623cec130cff75e299ca47f0a45b63ace819/src/router.ts))
5. Add global `on-load.ts` and `listeners.ts` files for easier review and state management (not sure how it's gonna look like and if it's worth impleneting it)

In the end we should get a project structure similar to the one from https://github.com/vercel/next.js where it should be clear where exactly to put UI components, pages, handlers, etc...

I_kwDOJGfp8c6cFN3o.title: New PR template
I_kwDOJGfp8c6cFN3o.body:
Suggestion below. If I place the comment at the top I lose formatting for the three sections but if it can be solved it would be better at the top maybe.

---

Resolves https.../yourTaskURL

---

Changes:
- ...

---

QA: 
- ...

---
```
<!--- 

Insert the number of the task you are completing if within this repository or paste the url of the task

Resolves https:... OR #<taskNumber>

/**
We advise that you give a brief description of your changes either that it meets
the specification exactly as described or what you had to do outwith the spec to resolve it. 
This is not required but again, it is advised.
*/

Changes:
- I had to do abc because of xyz but the spec was implemented otherwise

/**
NOTICE: This is required for all pull requests and will be requested by reviewers if not present.

- Include a screenshot/video or some other visual confirmation that your changes solve the task
- If this is not applicable, build a unit test to prove it works as intended
*/
QA:
<url to your plugin fork or test issue in your own org showcasing the end result>
<screenshot of some kind of UI fix>
<E2E video of using the software>
```

I_kwDOJGfp8c6BxV9J.title: Empty String `""` Code Review Warning Annotation
I_kwDOJGfp8c6BxV9J.body:
Annotate them with warnings in code review to more closely scrutinize the logic that interacts with the variable. 

### Rationale
I see a lot of abuse for empty strings. It makes code logic sloppy and more error prone. But since there are some legitimate uses for empty strings, we will just set a warning instead of error?

### Examples

###### https://github.com/ubiquity/audit.ubq.fi/blob/08fba85a9a592ca2bda5eaeaa2ff3b9fdd4632be/static/scripts/audit-report/audit.ts#L26C1-L27C21

- Should be unset (which implies `undefined`) instead of passing an empty string as a credential or URL. Then the code should check if the value is truthy before expecting authentication.
```typescript
let REPOSITORY_URL;
let GITHUB_PAT;
```

###### https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquibot-kernel/blame/c553d5a7866c1ef76d4f589d8095c8bb751cbd49/src/github/types/config.ts#L29

- Should be `value.ref ?? "@" + value.ref` as I'm assuming the developer never expected the `""` value to be returned there. 

###### https://github.com/ubiquity/pay.ubq.fi/pull/189/files#r1518549017

- This shouldn't have been here at all. It's not a valid RPC endpoint.


---

###### From codebase search: https://github.com/search?q=org%3Aubiquity+%5C%22%5C%22+language%3ATypeScript&type=code

I_kwDOJGfp8c6R2rj6.title: Automatic monthly sync
I_kwDOJGfp8c6R2rj6.body:
It could be interesting to bake in a CRON job that runs once a month to attempt to sync the template. 

This will ensure that our repositories in the future are effortlessly synced. 

A pull request can be opened up against the `development` branch, and any conflicts would be expected to be manually resolved. 

Add this to our active repositories as well. I suppose a script could be made to add the action file to all of our active repositories. "Active" can be defined as updated within the last month.

I_kwDOJGfp8c6B-Z6S.title: Auto tag / release system
I_kwDOJGfp8c6B-Z6S.body:
Currently there is no tags for versions in the projects we create. I think it would be quite beneficial to have a release tag, with a changelog on major updates to keep track of our latest version of a product. Since we already use conventional commits, it would be simple to add a GitHub action such as https://github.com/googleapis/release-please that would keep track of everything without us worrying.
It would be even more interesting for projects like `npm` packages, that would have automatic changelogs, and publishing.

I_kwDOJGfp8c6BHGf7.title: No Ternary
I_kwDOJGfp8c6BHGf7.body:
Ternary statements are abused in our codebases on occasion for very long and complex logic. I think they are not very readable. Disable it in favor of normal if else blocks.

```

```

I_kwDOJGfp8c5rEOlx.title: Create a reusable web deploy workflow
I_kwDOJGfp8c5rEOlx.body:
Right now for each repository which requires a web deploy we use separate github workflows ([example 1](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/blob/development/.github/workflows/continuous-deploy.yml) and [example 2](https://github.com/ubiquity/pay.ubq.fi/blob/main/.github/workflows/continuous-deploy.yml))

Github has a [feature of reusing workflows](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/reusing-workflows#creating-a-reusable-workflow) across all repositories in a single organisation

What should be done:
1. Create a reusable web deploy workflow (which will deploy to cloudflare pages in our case)
2. Remove old deployment workflows and apply the new reusable workflow to these repos:
- https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar
- https://github.com/ubiquity/pay.ubq.fi
- https://github.com/ubiquity/ubq.fi
3. Add feature of supporting custom domains like `2aa376b3.pay-ubq-fi.ubiquitydao.dev`

So in the end we should get a reusable workflow which:
- can be easily integrated into any organisation's repo
- produces the following [deploy result comment](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/pull/700#issuecomment-1602409352) by the [ubiquibot](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/blob/development/.github/workflows/continuous-deploy.yml#L80)

---

Improvement of https://github.com/ubiquity/ts-template/issues/1

I_kwDOJGfp8c5kk53X.title: Continuous Deployment for Bounty Hunters
I_kwDOJGfp8c5kk53X.body:
Please create a GitHub Action that will automatically create a new Cloudflare Pages instance automatically (we eventually will need a dedicated Ubiquity Cloudflare account to deploy to!) and then build/host pull requests made by public forks of bounty hunters.

We need to create (or fork) a custom GitHub Action to use Cloudflare Wrangler to automatically deploy a preview when bounty hunters open up a pull request in order to make the review process more efficient.

> Every time you open a new pull request on your GitHub repository, Cloudflare Pages will create a unique preview URL, which will stay updated as you continue to push new commits to the branch. This is only true when pull requests originate from the repository itself.

[Source](https://developers.cloudflare.com/pages/platform/preview-deployments/).

I_kwDOK0Dxds55BedA.title: Setup `slither`
I_kwDOK0Dxds55BedA.body:
