2. Setup a workflow that runs slither check on any PR and push to the `development` branch (make sure it fails if there are any warnings, [example](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/blob/8fd2ec2a608aded67238b17ce62e995e8f3deeb9/.github/workflows/slither-analysis.yml))

I_kwDOK0Dxds57BPoP.title: Invalidate nonce
I_kwDOK0Dxds57BPoP.body:
Owner should be able to invalidate [nonce](https://github.com/ubiquity/nft-rewards/blob/31871eb28c84fe0b95b413e2ea420c8c52191255/src/NftReward.sol#L23) for already signed mint request.

What should be done:
1. Add a new method `invalidateNonce()` where owner can pass a nonce value and invalidate mint request.
2. Cover with tests the `invalidateNonce()` method

I_kwDOK0Dxds545KyT.title: Make `NftReward` contract upgradeable
I_kwDOK0Dxds545KyT.body:
There is the [NftReward](https://github.com/ubiquity/nft-rewards/blob/f3aff74902c9c9814bdb0ffb1c213fbe7cba9360/src/NftReward.sol) contract responsible for minting NFTs using a minter's signature. We should make the contract to be upgradeable.

What should be done:
1. Make [NftReward](https://github.com/ubiquity/nft-rewards/blob/f3aff74902c9c9814bdb0ffb1c213fbe7cba9360/src/NftReward.sol) contract upgradeable (using openzeppelin's [UUPS contracts](https://docs.openzeppelin.com/contracts/5.x/api/proxy#UUPSUpgradeable))
2. Update the [deployment script](https://github.com/ubiquity/nft-rewards/blob/f3aff74902c9c9814bdb0ffb1c213fbe7cba9360/script/Deploy001_NftReward.s.sol)

I_kwDOK0Dxds545Mbw.title: Deploy with `CREATE2`
I_kwDOK0Dxds545Mbw.body:
There is the [deployment script](https://github.com/ubiquity/nft-rewards/blob/f3aff74902c9c9814bdb0ffb1c213fbe7cba9360/script/Deploy001_NftReward.s.sol) responsible for creating a new [NftReward](https://github.com/ubiquity/nft-rewards/blob/f3aff74902c9c9814bdb0ffb1c213fbe7cba9360/src/NftReward.sol) contract.

The [NftReward's](https://github.com/ubiquity/nft-rewards/blob/f3aff74902c9c9814bdb0ffb1c213fbe7cba9360/src/NftReward.sol) contract address should be the same across all networks.

What should be done:
- update the [deployment script](https://github.com/ubiquity/nft-rewards/blob/f3aff74902c9c9814bdb0ffb1c213fbe7cba9360/script/Deploy001_NftReward.s.sol) to use `create2` so that we could have the same [NftReward's](https://github.com/ubiquity/nft-rewards/blob/f3aff74902c9c9814bdb0ffb1c213fbe7cba9360/src/NftReward.sol) contract address across all networks

P.S. Note to self. When current issues is solved:
1. Redeploy the NFT reward contract
2. Update [readme](https://github.com/ubiquity/nft-rewards) with the new address
3. Set minter (defined [here](https://github.com/ubiquibot/comment-incentives/blob/d68ab4b4635e19d7facbd06259d25f4f30ea0e90/src/handlers/issue/generate-erc721-permit-signature.ts#L6)) in the contract's storage via `setMinter()`
4. Update NFT contract address [here](https://github.com/ubiquibot/comment-incentives/blob/d68ab4b4635e19d7facbd06259d25f4f30ea0e90/src/handlers/issue/generate-erc721-permit-signature.ts#L7)

I_kwDOO7dL4s7CDd7w.title: Do not appen " with ..." imports if already present
I_kwDOO7dL4s7CDd7w.body:
## What happened

If a `with` statement is used in an import statement, it will get duplciated during compilation which will break the build.

## What was expected

If the import statement already has a `with`, it should get ignored.

## How to reproduce

- use "import pkg from "./package.json" with { type: "json" };"
- notice that the compilation into Deno will fail

I_kwDON4DCF87bxiN5.title: V1: Backfill and validate historical permit payouts (GitHub + on-chain)
I_kwDON4DCF87bxiN5.body:
## Goals

- Recover historical permit data from GitHub issues and comments.
- Rebuild permits into the current schema and de-duplicate against the database.
- Validate remaining permits against on-chain activity on Ethereum and Gnosis.
- Produce a final report of matched, withdrawn, and unresolved permits.
- Improve the foundation of the XP system
- have near complete auditability with 1:1 ratio of `issue label totals == stored permit totals`, or at the very least, each task having verified/tracked payouts

***

## Scope

### 1. Scrape and reconstruct permits

- Enumerate all Ubiquity-owned GitHub orgs and repositories.
- For each issue:
    - Filter comments created by the `prod` & `dev` UbiquityOS.
    - Extract any base64-encoded permit URLs from comment bodies.
    - Decode the base64 payloads and rebuild permit objects into the current expected shape.
- Exclude any permits that already exist in the production database.


### 2. Ad-hoc / manual permits

- In addition to bot comments, include comments by:
    - `@0x4007` for manually sent claim URLs when the bot was down or broken (personally happened to me).
    - `@gentlementlegen` where claim URLs may have been copy-pasted manually when things were down/broken (I'm positive I saw it happen for another contributor, if not myself).
- Parse and process these comments for claim URLs using the same pipeline as bot comments.
- Treat these as best-effort coverage to improve audit completeness.


### 3. Permit validity checks

- For all reconstructed permits not already in the DB:
    - Check on-chain whether the permit nonce has been invalidated.
        - Case A: The permit was claimed by the `beneficiary`.
        - Case B: The signer invalidated/withdrew the permit via the `owner` address.
- If claimed:
    - Match the permit directly to an on-chain transaction involving the `beneficiary`.
- If not claimed:
    - Attempt to locate the invalidation transaction from the `owner` address; otherwise classify as “assumed withdrawn”.

***

## On-chain validation (Ethereum + Gnosis)

Both options require mapping `beneficiary` addresses to users in the production DB.

### Common requirement

- Map `beneficiary` → user by:
    - Using the current wallet mapping in the production DB where possible.
    - Where mappings have changed, parse the `username` from the original bot permit comment to resolve historical ownership.


### Option A: CSV-based, thresholded

- Group reconstructed permits by `beneficiary` address and sum total amounts.
- Exclude addresses with a total under a configurable threshold (e.g. 1,000 USD equivalent).
- For remaining addresses:
    - Manually export transaction history CSVs from Etherscan and GnosisScan.
    - Parse CSVs and group transfers that match `permit transfer from` patterns from known funding wallets.
- Accept that low-earning contributors below the threshold may remain unresolved.


### Option B: Programmatic, full coverage

- Build a structure such as `Record<IssueUrl, Array<{ ...Permit, txHash }>>`.
- For each distinct wallet address:
    - Page through full transaction history from RPC / explorer APIs.
    - Attempt to match permit parameters to on-chain call arguments or transfer patterns.
- For permits without an associated transaction hash:
    - Group them by contributor and issue.
    - Enforce that each contributor can only successfully claim once per issue.
    - Write leftover unmatched permits to a file for manual inspection.
- Store matched permits (with tx hash) keyed by issue/repo/org.

***

## Scale / complexity

- Expected scale: thousands of transactions and hundreds of missing permits.
- Non-trivial data volume and debugging effort across:
    - GitHub API scraping and parsing.
    - Base64 decoding and schema normalization.
    - On-chain history queries on two networks.
    - Heuristic matching and manual review for edge cases.


rfc @gentlementlegen @0x4007 

---





> Tip
> 
> [@Keyrxng](https://github.com/Keyrxng) currently has 8698.31 XP.




> 1. Would be amazing to see a UI breakdown how it's calculated and from which areas/repos/orgs you earned the most in as well as any reductions that have been applied.
> 2. The amount is incredibly off. Anyone can download the tx history as a CSV from https://gnosisscan.io/exportData?type=addresstokentxns. My wallet has only ever been used to collect payments from Ubiquity.

I_kwDOLUK0B864o3pb.title: Multiple permits in the same issue
I_kwDOLUK0B864o3pb.body:
> It has always been that way, I think it is to prevent multiple claims on the same issue, so only one is allowed.

The nonce is already enforce by the smart contract so it's not an issue

> If the amount changes but it is already claimed, it won't be claimable again anyway.

If there are 2 permits on the same issue (with different amounts) and only the second is claimed and pay.ubq.fi V2 adds a transaction hash to the permit, it would add it to the first permit so I think this should be fixed

_Originally posted by @whilefoo in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/issues/347#issuecomment-2913514803_

Currently the `nonce` column has a unique constraint - this means that if an issue is re-opened and closed multiple times thus generating multiple permits, only the first permit will be in the database. 

The amount can be changed between multiple permits so this can lead to a number of issues down the line, a few of them are:
1. [^01^]                                 If we use the DB as a way to calculate all amount it would return the wrong result
2. pay.ubq.fi v2 adds a transaction hash to the permit after it has been claimed so it will be added on the first permit which might have different amount than was was claimed
3. pay.ubq.fi v2 also queries permits from the DB to batch claim them so it would to lead to users getting a lower reward if the second permit has higher reward
...and other issues

That's why I recommend to store all permits generated - this can be done just by removing the unique constraint
Unique constraint on `signature` column would still ensure that if 2 permits are identical in terms of token, amount, spender, nonce, owner and deadline it would generate the same signature so it would not be inserted.

---

I realized this when working on pay.ubq.fi V2, the `transaction` column is also marked as unique but when we have batch permits claiming, multiple permits can have the same transaction hash so the unique constraint should also be removed here.

I know I recently updated my wallet, doubtful @0x4007 has, maybe that is a factor?

I_kwDOLUK0B87akw0V.title: `/generate-permit` slash command
I_kwDOLUK0B87akw0V.body:
> 1. I think the generate-permit command is interesting and useful for ad-hoc payments. If it can be access controlled to only work for admins it could be nice to use.


Since permits are always related to rewards or compensation in some form or another, and this is the only plugin in the eco-system with permit generating ability I think it makes sense to build it here. If it should be it's own plugin we can move this issue into `plugin-wishlist`.


### Command

``/generate-permit [githubUsername] [chainId | chainName] [amount] [tokenAddress | tokenSymbol]``

This structure makes things a bit easier for invokers of the command otherwise they must call `/query` first and then `/generate-permit` after finding the contributor's wallet addr

- `githubUsername > githubId > supabase wallet`


Note: maybe just `/permit`?

### Access Control

We already have access control implemented for generating permits we can hook into that




 _Originally posted by @0x4007 in [#80](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugins-wishlist/issues/80#issuecomment-3580187899)_
<!-- @ubiquity-os/text-vector-embeddings update 2025-12-08T18:25:38.779Z -->

I_kwDOLUK0B864pvt4.title: Original author bug
I_kwDOLUK0B864pvt4.body:
When making #356 I discovered two problems with original author implementation

First problem is that the regex doesn't match this format (which is automatically made by Github):

```
_Originally posted by @whilefoo in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/issues/347#issuecomment-2913514803_
```
From my testing it seems that the format is different when you reference a comment from an issue or from a PR so we need to cover both cases.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           

The second problem is that the issue author can be the original author and while the reward will be correct, the Github comment will show two same comments with different weights so it's best to just skip if original and issue author are the same.

I_kwDOLUK0B86yInxb.title: Specialized Prompts
I_kwDOLUK0B86yInxb.body:
> Cost is not a concern anymore there is Gemini and Deepseek offered both on OpenRouter for free. It makes more sense to have separate prompts for separate evaluations. For example:
> 
> 1. Rank from 0-1 how relevant these comments are to solving the spec.
> 2. Rank from 0-1 how helpful these comments are to help and answer contributor questions.
> 3. Rank from 0-1 how useful these comments are for adding research and insights to the project.
> 
> Then we apply weights to the results and return a score.  

 _Originally posted by @0x4007 in [#330](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/issues/330#issuecomment-2771703444)_

I_kwDOLUK0B86t9dI7.title: Implement Differential Reward Distribution for Reopened Issues
I_kwDOLUK0B86t9dI7.body:
When an issue is reopened and closed again, the reward system should calculate and distribute only the difference between previously granted rewards and new rewards.

**Requirements**:
1. Track Previous Rewards
   - Store transaction history with amounts per beneficiary
   - Record payment mode (direct/permit) used for each distribution

2. Differential Calculation
   - Compare new reward amounts with previously distributed amounts
   - Calculate positive differences only (additional rewards)
   - Skip beneficiaries with no change in rewards

3. Distribution Logic
   - Only process transactions for beneficiaries with positive reward differences
   - Handle cases where original payment failed but issue was reopened

4. Error Handling
   - Validate transaction history before processing
   - Handle missing or incomplete previous distribution data
   - Log detailed comparison between old and new reward amounts

**Technical Considerations**:
- Must not conflict with wallet insolvency fallback (generates permits if wallet can't afford payouts) 
- Extend Supabase schema to track complete distribution history
- Implement comparison logic in `payment-module.ts`
- Update GitHub comment format to show differential amounts

**Example Scenario**:
```typescript
// Original distribution
{
  "user1": { total: 100, paid: true },
  "user2": { total: 50, paid: true }
}

// New calculation
{
  "user1": { total: 150, difference: 50 },
  "user2": { total: 50, difference: 0 },
  "user3": { total: 25, difference: 25 }
}
```

**Success Criteria**:
- Only processes payments for positive differences
- Provides clear audit trail of all distributions
- Handles edge cases: failed payments, partial distributions should never happen with the insolvency fallback. 
- Handle what happens if we change the mode (direct, permits only) between distributions 

_Originally posted by @ubiquity-os-beta in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/pull/235#issuecomment-2722879064_
<!-- @ubiquity-os/text-vector-embeddings update 2025-12-07T03:21:42.456Z -->

I_kwDOLUK0B87AVAen.title: GitHub Decoupling
I_kwDOLUK0B87AVAen.body:
As we start market research on repurposing our tech for other use cases, I realized that if we rely on GitHub for this to work, it limits us to servicing developers. 

I propose building an integration with any popular task management software like asana or even Google Sheets possibly (to save you headache, find one with good APIs and docs) 

This will force you to begin decoupling the architecture from the GitHub dependency. Then this plugin will be portable to other project management software. 

It is not clear to me if our kernel supports this, but assuming that it does, then I imagine these changes can be applied on this plugin in less than a week.

I have mixed feelings on Google Sheets because it will take time to figure out how to make the UX make sense. For example you need to figure how to make a "formula" that can render a button or checkbox, that represents the project is closed as complete. 

I suppose as another project we also need to associate one humans identity across several platforms.

I_kwDOLUK0B87aOxIo.title: Do not reward user if they have their multiplier set to 0
I_kwDOLUK0B87aOxIo.body:
When a contributor reviews a pull-request, if that contributor has its multiplier set to `0`, it will not receive any incentives for its content (comments). However, the code review will still be counted towards its rewards. Instead, it should have the rewards set to zero.

Note: there are several approaches to this problem. The main reason why this happens is because the multiplier for the user is located within the `formattingEvaluator` section of the configuration, which is not used by `ReviewIncentivizerModule`. We do not apply such multipliers to the review. If we did, the value of the review would be much greater if the user multiplier is high. We are using a flat review reward so far, so maybe the best approach would be to allow / disallow roles to get rewarded?

I_kwDOLUK0B86wBHzr.title: Migrate tests from Jest to Bun Test
I_kwDOLUK0B86wBHzr.body:
## Task: Migrate Test Suite from Jest to Bun Test

**Goal:** Replace the current Jest test runner setup with Bun's built-in test runner (`bun test`) for improved performance and simplified tooling.

**Context:**
This project currently uses Jest for running tests. Migrating to `bun test` aligns with the project's adoption of Bun for other tasks (runtime, package management) and can potentially offer faster test execution, plus should help us dealing with ESM import issues and required transformers.

**Required Changes:**

1.  **Remove Jest Dependencies:**
    *   Uninstall Jest-related packages (`jest`, `@types/jest`, `ts-jest`, `babel-jest`, `ts-node` etc.)
    *   Remove the `.babelrc` file
2.  **Remove Jest Configuration:**
    *   Delete the `jest.config.ts` file.
3.  **Update `package.json` Scripts:**
    *   Modify the test script(s) to use `bun test` instead of `jest`.
4.  **Refactor Tests:**
    *   Go through all files in the `tests/` directory.
    *   Replace Jest's global functions (`describe`, `it`, `test`, `expect`, `beforeEach`, `afterEach`, etc.) with imports from `"bun:test"`.
        ```typescript
        import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, mock } from "bun:test";
        ```
    *   Adjust test matchers as needed. While many are similar, there might be differences. Refer to the [Bun Test documentation](https://bun.sh/docs/test/writing).
    *   Update mocking logic. Replace `jest.mock`, `jest.fn`, `jest.spyOn` with Bun's mocking capabilities (e.g., `mock`, `spyOn` from `"bun:test"`).
    *   `mswjs` does not work with `bun` for network mocking. Consider looking into https://github.com/DRFR0ST/bun-bagel or mock Octokit altogether.
5.  **Verify:**
    *   Run `bun test` and ensure all tests pass successfully.
6.  **Update CI/CD:**
    *   Modify `jest-testing.yml` that execute tests to use the new `bun test` command.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

*   All Jest dependencies and configuration files are removed.
*   All tests in the `tests/` directory are successfully migrated to use `bun test` APIs.
*   `bun test` runs without errors and all tests pass.
*   CI/CD pipelines are updated to use `bun test`.

I_kwDOLUK0B87LxBHt.title: Support Custom Tokens Per Contributon Style
I_kwDOLUK0B87LxBHt.body:
## Overview
Enhance configuration to support custom token rewards per user role, enabling mixed payouts (e.g., cash for core team members and XP for others). This feature aims to improve plugin adoption by other DAOs.

## Requirements
- Simplify configuration by supporting these user roles for reward allocation:
  - Admin
  - Collaborator
  - Contributor
  - Billing manager (optional, based on implementation)
- Allow setting custom token rewards per role block in the configuration
- Fall back to XP rewards if no custom token is specified for a role
- Support mixed payout assets (e.g., cash and XP) based on contributor roles

## Rationale
- Streamline plugin configuration ergonomics by reducing complexity
- Replace previous 8 contribution style categories with generalized role-based rewards
- Enable targeted rewards for core team members versus general contributors

I_kwDOLUK0B87I4YMU.title: Improve error display for an invalid permit key
I_kwDOLUK0B87I4YMU.body:
> 
> As for the wallet, it is probably due of the private key used in this organization that is not able to generate any permits:
> 
> Yeah, I forgot about that security feature. It makes sense, but we should definitely catch and throw this error to make that clear on the posted comment. 

 _Originally posted by @0x4007 in [#61](https://github.com/devpool-directory/devpool-directory-tasks/issues/61#issuecomment-3239448637)_

## What happened

The permits failed to generate due to a key that had no permissions to work in the given repository / organization. However, no clear error was displayed and only "Failed to fetch wallet" warning was visible.

## What was expected

We should have a clear message helping to identify the error.

## How to reproduce

- run the reward generation using a key that has no permissions on this repo

I_kwDOLUK0B86nTFsG.title: Web UI bug
I_kwDOLUK0B86nTFsG.body:
Now that https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugin-sdk/pull/51 is merged, the Web UI doesn't work because the SDK expects the payload in new unified format (previously Action format). The UI needs to be modified to create a dummy payload with unified format.

Another thing I noticed is that when you run the UI in dev mode and it's running on port `5173` and the API is running on port `4000`, there are CORS errors which need to be fixed.

I_kwDOLUK0B86mkDaZ.title: Avoid sending the user comments twice by directly evaluating them from the list with all the comments
I_kwDOLUK0B86mkDaZ.body:
Yeah that's why we could leave `allComments` so it makes more sense to the LLM but remove `userComments` and specify that the LLM should evaluate comments of user `x`. 
If I understand correctly comments include id and author so the LLM would know which comments to evaluate, but I might be wrong maybe it will miss comments

_Originally posted by @whilefoo in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/pull/225#discussion_r1918324483_
            

To evaluate user comments, we currently send the whole list of the comments under the issue, then the list of user comments. This means we send twice the same content, since the user comments are already in the comment list. Instead, we should tell in the prompt what user we are evaluating to extract them during the LLM evaluation, which would greatly reduce the amount of tokens used, allowing us to send more data.

We could also look into prompt caching, although it might be specific to OpenAI API.

I_kwDOLUK0B87AQo4L.title: Handle invalid links properly
I_kwDOLUK0B87AQo4L.body:
Improper link currently halt the `ExternalContentModule` parsing of external elements because the `fetch` function will fail after exhausting all the retries. An invalid link can be of diffent forms:
 
- dead link (e.g. `https://1234-I-dont-exist.com`)
- incomplete link (e.g. `/new` to create a new task in the repo)

In such case I think it is safe to skip these links.

Example of crash: https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/actions/runs/16240021883/job/45855171653#step:3:75

Copilot is a special user that does not behave like the standard one and causes problems in our workflow. The issue should be investigated and addressed.

I_kwDOLUK0B87GaI30.title: One Review Incentive Per Pull
I_kwDOLUK0B87GaI30.body:
The review incentive is broken when paying out per issue when a pull request addresses multiple issues.

## Requirements

- Review rewards must be paid per pull request, not per issue
- Generate unique permits using pull request ID (similar to current issue ID handling) instead of separate nonce system
- Calculate review reward amount using weighted average of priorities and time estimates from linked issues:
  - Formula: `(Priority₁ × Time₁) + (Priority₂ × Time₂) + ...` divided by total time
  - Handle edge cases (e.g., division by zero)
  - Cap maximum reward at highest task reward among linked issues
- Post review rewards exclusively in pull requests (not in issues)
- Maintain issue rewards (for assignees) in original issues
- Use batch claim functionality to manage separate reward permits
- Convert time estimates to consistent units (hours) for calculation:
  - Example conversions:
    - < 15 minutes = 0.25 hours
    - < 1 hour = 1 hour
    - < 4 hours = 4 hours
    - < 1 day = 24 hours
    - < 1 week = 168 hours

## Implementation Notes

- Repository configuration must be inherited for consistent behavior
- Priority levels and time estimates must be included in reward calculation metadata
- Weighted average should emphasize where developers spend actual effort
- Separate issue rewards (for assignees) and PR rewards (for reviewers)
- Maximum one review reward per pull request

<!-- daemon-spec-rewriter - 2025-10-07T05:09:06.250Z -->

I_kwDOLUK0B87LOni-.title: Support negation for gitattributes file
I_kwDOLUK0B87LOni-.body:
For convenience, we should support negation when parsing the `.gitattributes` file, and also support overrides.

Example:

```
* linguist-generated
*.ts !linguist-generated
```

Should result in all files ignored expect `typescript` files. `*.ts linguist-generated=true` should also be a valid syntax.

Example: https://github.com/ubiquity/permit2-rpc-manager/issues/5#issuecomment-3283987545

I_kwDOLUK0B86wBoQY.title: Retry and token limits
I_kwDOLUK0B86wBoQY.body:
The plugin currently uses OpenAI-specific method to get token rate limit but since we will be only focusing on OpenRouter we don't need to worry about token rate limit as OpenRouter only has request-based rate limits.

What needs to be done:

- Use retry function from the SDK to avoid duplicating code
- Use OpenRouter API to get model's token limits and use it for determining the chunk size in content evaluator module

I_kwDOLUK0B87B5ilb.body:
Ever since the Web UI was modified to run on multiple issues, when you try to run it on a single issue that's still open it won't work because it filters out open issues. 
The fix is to not filter open issues out if it's running in single issue mode.

I_kwDOLUK0B87DeqAA.title: Review-only contributors missing from rewards output
I_kwDOLUK0B87DeqAA.body:
## Issue Description

Users who submit reviews but have no other contributions (comments, issues, PRs) are not appearing in the final rewards output, causing them to miss their review rewards. [^02^]       

## Problem Details

In the rewards calculation system, users who only contribute reviews are not being included in the final results. [^03^]                                             The system appears to only create entries for users who have other types of contributions, filtering out review-only contributors. [^04^]                                              

## Evidence

From the rewards output in [issue #395](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/issues/395#issuecomment-3136383330), we can see:
- Only users with multiple contribution types appear in results
- The `"reviewRewards": []` array is empty even when reviews exist
- Users with only review contributions are completely missing from the output

## Root Cause

The reward collection/aggregation logic likely filters out users who don't have other contribution types (comments, issues, PRs), causing review-only contributors to be excluded from the final results. [^01^]                                                            

## Suggested Fix

Modify the rewards aggregation logic to ensure that users with any type of contribution (including review-only) are included in the final output with their appropriate rewards.                                                   

---

*Originally posted by [@0x4007](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/issues/395#issuecomment-3137330485)*

I_kwDOLUK0B86py5Ta.title: Cryptic Error for Non Existent Smart Contract
I_kwDOLUK0B86py5Ta.body:
I was making a new tool demo.ubq.fi that makes new encrypted keys and configs automatically. I realized that the default token this plugin selects is WXDAI which REQUIRES network ID 100. 

I had a config with network ID 1 and we would receive this cryptic error. 

Catch this error and explain that the smart contract doesn't exist here. [^02^] [^01^] Basically it was unable to get the token symbol. [^05^] But we can safely assume that every token has a symbol, so you can say something like "This token `0xe91D153E0b41518A2Ce8Dd3D7944Fa863463a97d` was not found on network ID `1`"

```

I_kwDOLUK0B86s85Yl.title: Credit for research
I_kwDOLUK0B86s85Yl.body:
In this situation the assignee spent time researching the task. 

I think they should receive comment rewards but we need to think more carefully about how to minimize gaming this system but also receiving credit. 

Perhaps we can have everybody receive comment credits except for the assignee upon task completion. 

This would include previous assignees. 

Make sure that we don't credit their pull requests though, for their work reviews.  

Setting this to high priority because I don't want to disincentivize people from starting tasks. It can be perceived as risky if they find out the task is not possible according to the spec. 

> whilefoo you have used all available deadline extensions and have been disqualified from this task.

I_kwDOLUK0B87CvFdw.title: Runtime Error: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '0') in external-content-module.ts
I_kwDOLUK0B87CvFdw.body:
## Issue Description

A runtime error is occurring in the `external-content-module.ts` file causing plugin execution to fail. [^02^]

## Error Details

**Error Message:** `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '0')`

**Stack Trace:**
```
TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '0')
    at retry_retry.maxRetries (file:///home/runner/work/text-conversation-rewards/text-conversation-rewards/dist/src/parser/external-content-module.ts:132:1)
    at processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)
    at altContent (file:///home/runner/work/text-conversation-rewards/text-conversation-rewards/dist/src/helpers/retry.ts:11:1)
    at processElement (file:///home/runner/work/text-conversation-rewards/text-conversation-rewards/dist/src/parser/external-content-module.ts:107:1)
    at ExternalContentProcessor.body [as _handleExternalElements] (file:///home/runner/work/text-conversation-rewards/text-conversation-rewards/dist/src/parser/external-content-module.ts:165:1)
    at ExternalContentProcessor.evaluateExternalElements (file:///home/runner/work/text-conversation-rewards/text-conversation-rewards/dist/src/parser/external-content-module.ts:38:1)
    at ExternalContentProcessor._result [as transform] (file:///home/runner/work/text-conversation-rewards/text-conversation-rewards/dist/src/parser/external-content-module.ts:181:1)
    at Processor.context [as run] (file:///home/runner/work/text-conversation-rewards/text-conversation-rewards/dist/src/parser/processor.ts:48:1)
    at result (file:///home/runner/work/text-conversation-rewards/text-conversation-rewards/dist/src/run.ts:88:1)
```

## Associated Action Run

[GitHub Actions Run #16538734397](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/actions/runs/16538734397)

## Root Cause

The error originates from line 132 in `external-content-module.ts` where the code is trying to access the first element (`[0]`) of an undefined array or object. [^01^]

## Suggested Fix

Add proper null/undefined checks before accessing array indices to prevent this runtime error.

---

*Originally Posted By: [ubiquity-os-beta[bot]](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-ask/issues/87#issuecomment-3121586680)*

Another reference: https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugins-wishlist/issues/2#issuecomment-3124426196

I_kwDOLUK0B86_I6A5.title: Make text-conversation-rewards MCP Compliant
I_kwDOLUK0B86_I6A5.body:
To enable broader interoperability, we should make the `text-conversation-rewards` plugin MCP compliant. This would allow any LLM or external system to interact with it using the Model Context Protocol, rather than relying on UbiquityOS-specific conventions.

### Key Points & Discussion

- **MCP Integration:**  
  Use the [typescript-sdk](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk) for standard MCP integration. This may require changes to the `manifest.json` schema or an adapter for compatibility.

- **Deployment:**  
  All plugins should have a Deno Deploy endpoint. For plugins currently running as GitHub Actions, the Deno endpoint should trigger the Action (see [daemon-pricing](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-pricing) for an example).

- **Streamable HTTP:**  
  MCP works through Streamable HTTP. Deno supports streaming, but it's not strictly required for all MCPs.

- **Context Handling:**  
  The main challenge is providing the necessary context (e.g., repo/issue/task info) when running outside GitHub (e.g., Telegram, CLI). Platform adapters may be needed to capture and provide this context.

- **Platform Agnosticism:**  
  `text-conversation-rewards` can be made platform-agnostic if we decouple content retrieval from GitHub and allow input from other sources (e.g., Google Docs, Telegram). The main non-agnostic part is currently content retrieval.

- **Standardization:**  
  Standardizing Deno deploy templates (similar to Cloudflare) will help. We have implemented some MCP-like standards already, but aligning with industry standards is beneficial.

- **Next Steps:**  
  - Prototype MCP compliance with `text-conversation-rewards` first.
  - Consider making a special AI system capability for plugins that use the current manifest format.
  - If successful, expand to other plugins as needed.

---

**Transferred from:** [ubiquity-os/plugins-wishlist#73](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugins-wishlist/issues/73)

I_kwDOLUK0B8697OW-.body:
I have noticed we have a lot of invalid permits or permits with missing data in the database:

- 231 permits with missing token ID and partner ID - these are from 2024 so it was fixed but they are still in the DB
- 77 permits with amount 0 - not sure why we save those or maybe it's a bug with amount not getting set
- 13 permits with missing deadline and invalid signature format (for example `f4dd8d94-b849-4745-a987-39b5f7fb678a` which is not even similar to a signature) - these are recents permits from June and May

Not sure why `token_id` and `partner_id` are nullable but I'd set it to be not nullable just in case

You can enforce structured outputs with these Deepseek models: https://openrouter.ai/models?arch=DeepSeek&fmt=cards&supported_parameters=structured_outputs

Docs: https://openrouter.ai/docs/features/structured-outputs

- I am unsure if we have problems with this now so just putting here for reference. 
- It appears that no free Deepseek models support this (or at least, they don't show up on the search results page.)

I_kwDOLUK0B86jJZJY.title: Relevance Augmentations
I_kwDOLUK0B86jJZJY.body: There's a few high impact elements to determine the relevance of a comment. Which is why in the default formatting scoring we have high ($5) credit per instance. ### image Relevance currently doesn't understand image contents. We can insert the image description inside of its "alt" attribute. ```html ``` ### link It could also be really interesting to pull the text contents from links to also score their relevance. We can insert a summary inside of its title attribute. ```html Example ``` ### code It would be interesting to discuss this in a separate proposal because this seems like it could be really complex to implement well. Original context https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-wallet/issues/28#issuecomment-2540293850

I_kwDOLUK0B86gUkrh.title: Use diffs on comments to extract human content from bot modifications
I_kwDOLUK0B86gUkrh.body:
Reliably depends mostly on GitHub API I suppose, sometimes I must say it looks weird because the "diff" is just the comment you posted not a diff formatted content. Making a separate spec to explore this.

_Originally posted by @gentlementlegen in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/pull/188#discussion_r1856110897_
            

We should try to use diff to understand the edits on bodies and comments and avoid crediting the bot for its changes, only the human made edits.

What should be done:
- diff the whole history on the bodies of issues to remove the bot content from evaluation
- likewise for comments
- likewise for review comments

A library that might help: https://www.npmjs.com/package/diff

Example of edit: https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/issues/315

I_kwDOLUK0B86Q6xgM.title: "Originally posted by" crediting
I_kwDOLUK0B86Q6xgM.body:
In a conversation, using the context menu, we are able to instantly create a new issue and prefill the body with the comment contents. It is common that the original author of the comment does not receive credit for creating the issue (when another team member creates the issue for them.)

Detect when the following string is detected (full example with context):

```
Originally posted by @0x4007 in https://github.com/ubiquibot/conversation-rewards/pull/58#discussion_r1691159526
```

So it will be a regex like `^Originally posted by @<username regex> in <url regex>$`

And when calculating the rewards, the original author should receive part of the author credit. Make it configurable with a percentage. I would default to 50% of the credit.

It would be ideal to switch to it, because there are so many runs that currently get stuck forever due to the RPCs stalling.

I_kwDOLUK0B86s89nW.title: Slash command to override generating rewards
I_kwDOLUK0B86s89nW.body:
We need to think through this more but my feeling is described below. 

I think any collaborator should be able to override and force rewards to generate even if a pull is open. 

Maybe `/finish`? 

If there is a way to get the bot to close the pull that would be even better. But I don't think that's possible if even an admin can't. 

---

> @EresDev can you close https://github.com/EresDevOrg/personal-agent-bridge/pull/3

I feel like we need a slash command to handle this edge case. I don't like the idea of even an admin (me) not being able to push this through. 

_Originally posted by @0x4007 in https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugins-wishlist/issues/3#issuecomment-2705183260_

I_kwDOLUK0B862Blh2.title: `/finish` command won't work if it was triggered with LLM router like `@UbiquityOS can you generate rewards`
I_kwDOLUK0B862Blh2.body:
I realized this won't work if it was triggered with LLM router like `@UbiquityOS can you generate rewards` but currently there's no easy fix for this so I'm just leaving this comment to acknowledge this issue

_Originally posted by @whilefoo in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/pull/343#discussion_r2080353080_

# Command routing via `@UbiquityOS` mention is not handled correctly

## What happened

Commands triggered via LLM routing (e.g., mentioning `@UbiquityOS` with a prompt like `@UbiquityOS can you generate rewards`) are not correctly processed by the system. This is due to current limitations in how mentions and command parsing are handled (we chack for bot events, triggerd by user events).

## What was expected

Commands issued through mentions (such as `@UbiquityOS can you generate rewards`) should be routed and handled in the same way as directly issued commands, and understand that this was triggered through the LLM in the kernel

## Reproduction steps

1. Mention the bot in a comment or message with a natural-language request (e.g., `@UbiquityOS can you generate rewards`).
2. Observe that the command is not executed correctly.
3. Compare this to behavior when the same command is issued without using the LLM call.

## Notes

Currently, there is no simple fix for this limitation. This issue exists to acknowledge the problem and track potential improvements in command parsing or mention handling in the future.

I_kwDOLUK0B86kf3pK.title: Transfers then Permits
I_kwDOLUK0B86kf3pK.body:
I would normally post this under the dedicated permit generation repo but it isn't live yet and it isn't clear how long that will be. 

We should make the default payments behavior to be a transfer and then render permits if the full payouts can't be afforded. 

For example, we have a low balance now and I haven't had the chance to top it up. If it can't afford all the payouts from that GitHub issue being closed, then it renders permits for everyone. 

However under normal circumstances we can afford to pay for everybody so it should do a direct transfer. 

This behavior should be able to be toggled as well- something like a reference to "automatic transfers mode"

This way, permits will never be fully phased out, but transfers would take precedence if the funding wallet can afford it.

I_kwDOLUK0B86wFFZw.title: Review Incentives `.gitattributes` Requirement
I_kwDOLUK0B86wFFZw.body:
I was thinking that relying on `.gitattributes` for ignoring files is shaky for other partners. I think we should use a dictionary of common lockfiles and other files that should not be reviewed to ignore and hard code it in.

Or ideally lets check all the ignores across all common configs like prettier and eslint.

I_kwDOLUK0B86nPhGn.title: Cap the specification to the reward label
I_kwDOLUK0B86nPhGn.body:
> Shouldn't the rewards be capped to the assignee reward, which is 0? @gentlementlegen  

 _Originally posted by @0x4007 in [#37](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-merging/issues/37#issuecomment-2608650113)_


## What happened

The user got rewards when the reward label was zero.


## What was expected

Since the label was zero, the user should have had its rewards capped to zero as well.

## How to reproduce

- write a specification
- time it, price it, and put a priority to `0` which generates a 0 label tag price
- close the task as resolved
- notice that the user gets the rewards for the spec

I_kwDOLUK0B86vSz3i.title: Fix Word Count Exponential Score Calculations

I_kwDOLUK0B86vHo6r.title: Mock RPCs for local testing
I_kwDOLUK0B86vHo6r.body:
Often, Jest testing suite fails due to tests exceeding their allowed runtime. This is due to RPCs being extremely slow at times. For testing, it would make more sense to mock these because there are not needed and would allow for much faster and reliable testing.

Options to consider:
- mocking the RPC package via Jest
- mocking endpoints via MSWJS
- using a local server like Anvil

I_kwDOLUK0B86qbtvQ.title: Conclusive Review Credit Adjustment
I_kwDOLUK0B86qbtvQ.body:
$25 flat seems excessive at times

New idea is to make line review credit only apply if review is set to approved or request changes.

I think this might scale better with smaller/larger sizes proportionally.

I_kwDOLUK0B86qQAaK.title: Deduplication Should Not Receive Credit
I_kwDOLUK0B86qQAaK.body:
We use footnote links for `/annotate` and issue deduplication

Excessive credit is being given to the author of the comment with any of those generated footnotes

https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-wallet/issues/47#issuecomment-2661604653

Do not count deduplicated footnotes.  I suppose we can target the `0` prefix in the footnote ID but it also makes me think we should think of a more robust targeting system for the future.  For now regex footnotes with ids starting with that - should be fine to remove from the content during preprocessing.  

In the near future, we should actually prefix the generated footnotes with something more precise like `dedupe-` or `no-credit` or even better, check the diff and only offer credit if the user wrote the link, not a bot. [^01^] 

If we can identify who wrote what, then we can extremely precisely reward credit where its due. 






[^01^]: ⚠ 77% possible duplicate - [Crediting for unique links only](https://www.github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/issues/155#155)

I_kwDOLUK0B86ps6wb.title: Review Incentives: Deleted Files
I_kwDOLUK0B86ps6wb.body:
Count the amount of removed lines for deleted files and subtract from the total lines changed count. 

The reason for this is because when reviewing it takes little/no cognitive overhead on the files view to skim over "file deleted" 

---

> Seems arbitrary and would eventually fail in some cases. It is complex to evaluate line changes because renaming a function for example could involve changing all the import across files and you would end with a +20 -20 for one rename.

This would yield $0.40 which isn't bad. 

The problem is that not every line changed takes the same cognitive overhead to process. 

Obviously most of the 17k removed was cypress code which can be easily reviewed just by reading "file deleted"

Perhaps we can reduce credit based on the deleted files removed line count. 

This will also solve for renames in case git doesn't catch it!

I_kwDOLUK0B86kwXFE.title: Flesch-Kincaid Clarity Module
I_kwDOLUK0B86kwXFE.body:
> We are exploring methods to evaluate comment readability and conciseness using **Flesch-Kincaid** readability metrics. These formulas assess the complexity and clarity of text based on sentence length and word syllables:

> 1. **Flesch Reading Ease:** Rates text on a scale from 0 (difficult) to 100 (easy). Scores can help determine how easily a comment can be understood.

> These metrics can help us identify verbose comments vs. concise, high-value inputs

This seems a lot more interesting compared to word count but we should test. 

_Originally posted by @0x4007 in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/issues/97#issuecomment-2363145914_
            
We can use an npm package to do most of the work, but I think that tweaking the weighting of the output of this module might take time to get right, which is why I made the time 4 hours instead of something like 1 hour. 

As an extension of this, if it is successful, then we can consider removing word counting from the scoring criteria, and instead use the amount of html tags as a proxy for comment length. 

For example, a comment with many list items, images, and links is more likely to be "longer" than a comment without. This may place the emphasis on higher Flesch Reading Ease (more easily understandable) and useful elements (images, links) vs writing verbose comments with low information density etc.

## What happened

## What was expected

Because the drafted pull-request would not close the issue and should be discarded, and because none of the admins have rights to close it, the bot should check the assignee list and the author of the pull-requests, and ignore them if they are not authored by someone from the assignee list.

I_kwDOLUK0B86lXrPR.title: Implement retry mechanism for LLM failures
I_kwDOLUK0B86lXrPR.body:
I would like to do that, but the endpoint is changeable so if another API is used (I know that shiv recommended others) the return code of the API can differ (and actually the return code of OpenAPI doesn't indicate that the comment is too long as far as I remember, just a failure) and parsing the error message feels unreliable.

See error code 429: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/error-codes

_Originally posted by @gentlementlegen in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/pull/225#discussion_r1903285110_
            

## Description

Currently if the LLM fails, we throw and abort the run completely, which cancels all the rewards and results, and needs a new run to be triggered afterwards. While some errors are not recoverable, the ones like us sending too many tokens to be handled can be avoided by splitting the prompt and retrying with a smaller prompt until it fits with the given model.

## Needed changes

We should implement:
- a retry mechanism which, on errors that are not fatal, would give another try with the LLM
- covering cases like the token amount sent being too large, responses having truncated JSON content, network failures
- post a message saying the results are being retried
- set a failure limit
- throw in the end if every try is unsecessfull
- add related tests

I_kwDOLaI9PM6bjD11.title: Improve trust ratio of rpcs served
I_kwDOLaI9PM6bjD11.body:
@EresDev has made it known it's possible for pay.ubq.fi' new card feature to be taken over/hijacked by an RPC provider if it turns out they are nefarious.

```ts
const chains = await fetcher("https://chainid.network/chains.json");
const chainTvls = await fetcher("https://api.llama.fi/chains");
```

This is how chainlist fetch their RPC list which we also use, they validate chains based on DeFiLlama TVL stats.

Their UI produces a "Score" value but I don't think that's something we can use to determine a level of trust and I think it may be UI specific as there's nothing like that returned from in this [json](https://chainid.network/chains.json)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6048509f-9d40-4787-8092-46d62d2d7a08)


Problems:

1. Using a service like Alchemy or Infura drastically decreases the number of networks that we can support
2. Services like above have a service/subscription fee normally and we avoid that at all costs.
3. I'm not aware of any other free service or service provider that fills our need here.

Solutions:
1. general whitelist - not scalable, requires manual validation across hundreds of networks.
2. [service provider](https://www.alchemy.com/best/rpc-node-providers) - solves the trust issues but involves costs and limits our offering.
3. specific whitelist - cards I think are limited to our designated chains, if so, we could build a specific whitelist for those chains and only use that list for the card feature logic.

I was contacted on TG a while back with a shill for this https://github.com/erpc/erpc, which is our `rpc-handler` on steroids by the looks of it, idk if we are considering just replacing this package completely in place of a 3rd party offering, are we?

_original [context](https://github.com/ubiquity/pay.ubq.fi/pull/345#discussion_r1811910326)_

I_kwDOLaI9PM6qHkga.title: Better RPC handler
I_kwDOLaI9PM6qHkga.body:
`rpc-handler` may return RPC node which is:
- out of sync (i.e. [^05^] [^04^] [^02^] [^01^] not on the latest block) 
- not accessible for the end user (like https://eth.llamarpc.com for me which works only via VPN)
- not reliable like `https://eth.drpc.org` (which responds to `eth_getBlock` but hangs for further requests which [causes](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/actions/runs/13383655396/job/37376464060) `text-conversation-rewards` plugin to compute rewards for ~15 minutes instead of 15 seconds)

As a part of this task we should fix all of the above issues. [^03^]

I_kwDOLaI9PM6qpzuS.title: refactor: init, utilities, and test envs
I_kwDOLaI9PM6qpzuS.body:
During the initial build review everything was made to be `null` rather than optional which makes it a mess when initializing `rpc-handler` because everything must be explicitly set to `null` or `non-null`.

We should make all of the nullable init values optional to make it easier for the end user to set things up by removing the burden of having to consider if they actually need to define `runtimeRpcs` (which they don't) etc.

---

Maybe it would be helpful to build a utility class with the various helper functions already built and consider if there is a need for any others?

---

Since `pay.ubq.fi` tests has been a point of contention and it's likely to be used elsewhere too, we could build an `Anvil` module similar to the one used for testing there and beef it up to include more redundancies like caching the intended steps and if we fail kill and spawn another and rerun the script.

Idk if we can mess with `Anvil` at a low level, I know I can't, but other than that I don't see a clear solution to that problem from a `rpc-handler` perspective.

I_kwDOLaI9PM6SVCFc.title: Make fastest network finding more efficient
I_kwDOLaI9PM6SVCFc.body:
Instead of trying to resolve all the promises to see which network is the fastest, it would be more efficient to return the first one that resolves the network call successfully as it is probably the fastest.

              @Keyrxng @0x4007 I changed the logic a bit so instead of naively removing the first RPC of the list, the failing RPC is actually removed.

This also made me wonder, why do we test every endpoint when we are only interested in the fastest one, aka the first one to successfully fulfill the response? If we have to wait for slow RPCs, for example one that take 5 seconds to complete, doesn't it defeat the purpose of the function?

_Originally posted by @gentlementlegen in https://github.com/ubiquity/rpc-handler/issues/33#issuecomment-2255230177_

I_kwDOLaI9PM6RCl-w.title: Fix RPC tests
I_kwDOLaI9PM6RCl-w.body:
As seen in [this run](https://github.com/ubiquity/rpc-handler/actions/runs/10120429568/job/27990176188), the tests do not run successfully. There is also an issue with the run logging after the tests are done.
The tests should all be successful and the logs should not happen after Jest is torn down. This requires to investigate all the async calls and see what runs beyond the end of the tests.

I_kwDOLaI9PM6LETVj.title: Proxify returned provider
I_kwDOLaI9PM6LETVj.body:
Rather than returning a list of RPCs considered valid based on their `eth_getBlockNumber` response and the burden being on the package user to handle call failures > endpoint reselection via the module, it should be built-in directly.

To do this, the package should return a JS `Proxy` to the provider. This `Proxy` should effectively handle call retries, RPC reselection, error feedback and be configurable.

This means that any read/write op a user makes will now pass through the module (as opposed to only read ops in the linked issue) which is a lot of ground to cover in terms of potential errors.

A proxy user should never need to get involved with errors unless they have caused them more or less and since what they assign as their `"app.provider"` is our `Proxy` they should never need to change provider.

<hr/>

- The proxy should be the default
- Expose both the proxy and a non-proxied version (basically what the package does now)
- configurables: breakpoints, tiered log output, log message constants
- tests should cover read & write ops, as well as any new functionality

<hr/>

The intention is that for a package user everything out of their control is abstracted away, they ask for a provider and it just works unless ofc it's user error.

The expectation is that the user would receive valuable error feedback only if the package is not able to complete the op and this really should only ever happen because of user error.


User-side [Proxy implementation](https://github.com/ubq-testing/pay.ubq.fi/blob/development/static/scripts/rewards/web3/onchain-call-handler.ts) for reference

_original context https://github.com/ubiquity/pay.ubq.fi/issues/235_

##### time: hard to say, <1 day maybe more

I_kwDOLaI9PM6KU4R-.title: Include All RPCs
I_kwDOLaI9PM6KU4R-.body:
By default, the tool should include all of the RPCs. I was reading the read me and it says that it automatically excludes the RPC endpoints that have tracking, but that wasn't part of the specification. 

Instead, if the developer cares, then they should pass in a property in the config to exclude. I have a feeling that most devs implementing this package don't care and just want fast RPCs.

1. Make the default behavior include all available RPCs
2. Make a config property to exclude based on tracking status. 
3. Consider adding other config RPC filters if any come to mind.

I_kwDOLaI9PM6J6oRy.title: Published without types
I_kwDOLaI9PM6J6oRy.body:
I'm unsure what's caused this but it's been published without types.

- https://www.npmjs.com/package/@ubiquity-dao/rpc-handler?activeTab=code 

- https://www.npmjs.com/package/@keyrxng/rpc-handler?activeTab=code what it should look like

running `yarn build` then `yarn pack` followed by `tar -tf ubiquity-dao-rpc-handler-v1.1.0.tgz` locally reveals:

```bash
...
package/dist/types
package/dist/cjs/index.js
package/dist/esm/index.js
package/dist/types/constants.d.ts
package/dist/types/handler.d.ts
package/dist/types/rpc-handler.d.ts
```

Looking at the [run](https://github.com/ubiquity/rpc-handler/actions/runs/9180797875/job/25246055017) reveals:

```bash
npm notice 📦  @ubiquity-dao/rpc-handler@1.1.0
npm notice === Tarball Contents === 
npm notice 2.5kB   README.md        
npm notice 1.5MB   dist/cjs/index.js
npm notice 572.7kB dist/esm/index.js
npm notice 573.1kB dist/index.js    
npm notice 2.6kB   package.json     
npm notice === Tarball Details === 
```

Looking at both versions of the UBQ package neither has `/types` or any `d.ts` files. While all four of my versions have either `/types` or `.d.ts` files.

<hr/>

The only difference between the two workflows is `publish` is broken off into an additional step (don't see why that would affect it).

Locally the types are built as expected.

I_kwDOLaI9PM6CQxNq.title: Implement Dynamic RPC pick handler
I_kwDOLaI9PM6CQxNq.body:
We want to use Chainlist's list of RPC endpoints. Create a class that will handle this:
- We should import the Chainlist repository as a submodule to stay in sync.
- The URLs should be tested upon initialization (i.e. page load) via `Promise.race`
- The class should rank sort based on latency. 
- The class should drop failed endpoints. 
- The class should have a wrapper method to make a network request using the optimal RPC endpoint, and to drop it/automatically rotate to the next best from the list if a network request fails. 

We will use this across all of our products that require RPC calls so: 
- Make a standalone and polymorphic (frontend and backend compatible) npm module.
- Make sure to have unit tests in Jest.

### Original Spec

The code should drop bad RPCs dynamically. I had a prototype implemented where it would delete from local storage if there was an error for example. Later in the code it would only select from local storage options. 

> This blacklist idea doesn't seem like a robust approach. We can merge it in but it should be handled dynamically. I think I implemented this in an unstable manner on my branch, I vaguely recall.

_Originally posted by @pavlovcik in https://github.com/ubiquity/pay.ubq.fi/pull/169#discussion_r1502430625_

I_kwDOO3QP6c7JSPLt.title: Import Nonces
I_kwDOO3QP6c7JSPLt.body:
I was working on [this](https://github.com/ubiquity/pay.ubq.fi/issues/413#issuecomment-3240339002) and I realized that a redeployment could be nice to have to: 

1. rename the smart contract to permit3 for disambiguation.
2. Allow us to import already used Nonces as an array (intended to clone the used ones on mainnet and gnosis chain) during deployment, 
3. Add a method we can call later for batch nonce imports. This beats having to send thousands of individual invalidation requests.  
4. A versioning setter, so we can leave a comment if its deprecated etc.

I hope its easy to batch read the entire nonce bitmap in a single RPC call, otherwise this can be tedious.

I_kwDOMyHsAM6uEbN1.title: premade configs that are hands-off for partners
I_kwDOMyHsAM6uEbN1.body:
## Premade Configurations vs. Default Plugin Manifests

We aim to streamline the experience for pilot partners by providing **premade configurations**. This approach reduces the need for partners to manually configure plugins. In addition, we provide two alternative ways to modify configurations:

1. Utilize [this feature request](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugins-wishlist/issues/65).
2. Contact customer support for manual adjustments.

### Default Plugin Behavior

1. When a plugin uses `defaults` that are **not** sourced from a **premade config**, it retrieves its configuration from the plugin's `manifest.json` in its respective repository.
2. These defaults originate from `pluginInputs.ts` and are used across multiple infrastructure components, including:
   - This UI
   - Natural language configuration edits
   - Plugin schema and input validation (kernel-side)
   - Other relevant subsystems

#### Opinionated Defaults

There is a general concern about **opinionated defaults**. Instead of enforcing such defaults, we should **only share premade configs with partners**, eliminating the need for them to define their own configurations.

Reference: [Discussion on opinionated defaults](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/pull/151#discussion_r1991092437)

### Configuration Modes

Currently, there are three configuration modes:

- **Minimal**
- **Full Defaults**
- **Custom**

These modes, up to this point, have not been enforced to be unopinionated. Some configurations are highly opinionated and may not align with the principle of **non-opinionated defaults**.

---

## Proposed Solutions

### 1. Establish a Collection of Premade Configurations
- Define a structured set of **premade configs**.
- Ensure each premade config abstracts its functionality at a high level.
- Reduce the cognitive load on partners.

### 2. YAML-Based Template Structure
To further simplify configuration management, I suggest we use a **YAML template** with the following structure:

```yaml
metadata:
  name: "UbiquityOS Standard Config"
  description: "The fully-enabled and battle-tested configuration which we use across all organizations at Ubiquity."
  version: "1.0"

plugins: ...
```

This template includes:
- **Metadata**: Describes the premade configuration.
- **Plugins**: Lists installed plugins with relevant settings.

This abstraction ensures partners **"don’t have to think about it."**

---

## Handling Plugin Expansion

### Default Non-Expanded Behavior
@gentlementlegen [suggests](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-plugin-installer/issues/40#issuecomment-2670919061) that plugins should be installed in a **non-expanded state**. This means:

- **Infra fetches only the non-opinionated defaults** when retrieving the plugin’s manifest.
- **If plugin details are expanded**, infra should fetch its specific config **before** relying on the manifest.

### Supporting Only Premade Configs
If we only allow **premade configs** and restrict editing to **already installed plugins**, then:

- Each `premade` configuration will be expanded by default.
- This ensures we avoid relying on **non-opinionated defaults**, which may not align with intended user experiences.

---

## Conclusion

- **Premade configurations** should be the standard approach.
- A **YAML-based template** simplifies configuration abstraction.
- **Non-expanded installations** should default to non-opinionated configurations.
- **Expanded plugin configurations** should fetch predefined configurations before falling back to defaults.

By enforcing these principles, we create a **streamlined, user-friendly**, and **low-maintenance** approach to plugin configuration management.

I_kwDOMyHsAM6xG7v9.title: Allow Users to Select and Configure Specific Plugin Branches
I_kwDOMyHsAM6xG7v9.body:
Currently, the plugin installer GUI assumes users always want to configure and install the version of a plugin found on a single, predefined branch (initially hardcoded to `development`, later updated to the repository's default branch). However, plugins often have different versions, features, or configuration schemas across various branches (e.g., `main`, `development`, feature branches, release tags).

Users need the ability to explicitly select the desired branch for a plugin they are configuring, and the system must ensure that the correct manifest is used for rendering the UI and the correct identifier (Action reference or URL) is saved in the configuration file.

**Desired Behavior / Acceptance Criteria:**

1.  The configuration editor should display a dropdown allowing users to select from the available branches of the plugin's source repository.
2.  When editing an existing plugin configuration, the dropdown should pre-select the branch specified in the saved configuration identifier (`owner/repo@branch` or parsed from URL).
3.  When editing an existing plugin configuration, the editor UI should initially render based on the manifest fetched from the *configured* branch.
4.  When the user selects a different branch from the dropdown, the editor UI should dynamically update by fetching and rendering the manifest from the newly selected branch.
5.  When saving the configuration, the `plugin` identifier stored in the `uses` array must accurately reflect the user's selected branch:
    *   For Action-based plugins: `owner/repo@selectedBranch`
    *   For URL-based plugins: The correctly transformed URL corresponding to the `selectedBranch` (e.g., `https://...-selected-branch-suffix.domain.com`).
6.  UI elements should be consistent with the rest of the application.
7.  Informational/success toast notifications should auto-dismiss.

## Main issues

We will have problems saving different branches because it is technically correct to have two similar plugins pointing to two different branches. Similarly the plugin would not know we want to change the target branch but will instead stack plugins from different branches together.

I_kwDOMyHsAM6eqPpC.title: Predefined configs
I_kwDOMyHsAM6eqPpC.body:
Depends on https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-plugin-installer/pull/13

Right now partner is able to setup bot config individually per plugin.

We should offer predefined configs so that instead of customizing each plugin individually partner could simply select a predefined config.

Simply put partner should be able to:
1. Setup each plugin individually (already implemented)
2. Create a new config from a predefined config template in one click

For the start I think we should support 2 config templates:
1. Config with all plugins from https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace and all default values
2. Minimal:
  - https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop
  - https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-pricing
  - https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards

Notice: the UI should support multiple predefined configs.
