I_kwDOMyHsAM6fUNMp.title: Add parameter descriptions for core plugins in the `plugin-input.ts`
I_kwDOMyHsAM6fUNMp.body: Right now all core plugins have a `manifest.json` file ([example](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/blob/ee4f7c698f0e221381df7c73f56457e1ca4d22ea/manifest.json)) describing plugin's: - name - description - listeners - command examples - configuration parameters The issue is that when plugin configuration is displayed in https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-plugin-installer there're no any configuration parameter descriptions so it's really hard for a partner to find out what parameters are meant to be used for: What should be done for all of the core plugins in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace: 1. Make sure the readme file is relevant (i.e. has a relevant config example and configuration parameter descriptions) 2. Make sure there's a `name` property in the `manifest.json` (some of the core plugins miss it) 3. Make sure there's a `description` property in the `manifest.json` 4. Add descriptions of plugin configuration parameters in the `plugin-input.ts` file (for example [this](https://github.com/Meniole/command-query-user/blob/042533ebbbfebaf5f5754b1d4a2fcf1d8afd94ad/src/types/plugin-input.ts#L7) code generates [this](https://github.com/Meniole/command-query-user/blob/ecb3c906d0e21b1557b23a8465fb2760b3f87abf/manifest.json#L16) manifest). Simply put we should add the `description` field for all of the plugin parameters in the `plugin-input.ts` file. Description should include general info and possible parameter options. Notice that in the `manifest.json` file the `configuration` schema is created dynamically: 1. [update-configuration.yml](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/blob/ee4f7c698f0e221381df7c73f56457e1ca4d22ea/.github/workflows/update-configuration.yml) workflow runs 2. Configuration schema is fetched from [plugin-input.ts](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/blob/ee4f7c698f0e221381df7c73f56457e1ca4d22ea/src/types/plugin-input.ts) 3. [manifest.json](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/blob/ee4f7c698f0e221381df7c73f56457e1ca4d22ea/manifest.json) is updated ([example commit](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/commit/56232c8c67d198cc46cf61c9b3fd0b90cce57df7)) Then we'll display all configuration parameter descriptions in https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-plugin-installer on creating/editing a config. Update: parameter description PRs: - https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-vector-embeddings/pull/49 - https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-pricing/pull/56 - https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/pull/94 - https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-disqualifier/pull/52 - https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-wallet/pull/24 - https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-query/pull/25 - https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-ask/pull/41 - https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/pull/202 - https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-merging/pull/30

I_kwDOMyHsAM6jvFl7.title: Set `organization/repository` names in the plugins' configs
I_kwDOMyHsAM6jvFl7.body:
Depends on https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-kernel/issues/224

Plugin installer should always set `organization/repository` value in the config's [plugin](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/.ubiquity-os/blob/67df7f5643edcaa3899304481f7ff4ef74a1041a/.github/.ubiquity-os.config.dev.yml#L3) property for all of the plugins.

For example [here](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/.ubiquity-os/blob/67df7f5643edcaa3899304481f7ff4ef74a1041a/.github/.ubiquity-os.config.dev.yml#L3) instead of
```
- plugin: https://ubiquity-os-comment-vector-embeddings-development.ubiquity.workers.dev
```
plugin installer UI should set `org/repo` value:
```
- plugin: ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-vector-embeddings
```

I_kwDOMyHsAM6iup9G.title: Manifest Name Restriction
I_kwDOMyHsAM6iup9G.body: It never made sense to me why the manifest names have to match the repository name to verify that they are installed. Just take the URL from the repository name instead.

I_kwDOMyHsAM6eqEhU.title: UI improvements
I_kwDOMyHsAM6eqEhU.body:
Depends on https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-plugin-installer/pull/13

What should be done:
1. The "select configuration" step should be removed. Always use the "production" config.
2. Show plugin readme on installing a new plugin (original [comment](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-plugin-installer/issues/15#issue-2646661367))
3. Show indicator if plugin is already installed (original [comment](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-plugin-installer/issues/16#issue-2647181893))
4. Load current config values in the editor (if config is present)
5. Add "Reset to defaults" button which resets plugin options to default values (original [comment](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-plugin-installer/issues/17#issue-2647183231))
6. Add github redirect to the same page ([example](https://github.com/ubiquity/work.ubq.fi/blob/5ad2731d99f3c03cdb52fe5f7122e412669ca0bd/src/home/rendering/render-github-login-button.ts#L37))

I_kwDOMyHsAM6f8A40.title: Organization selection choices
I_kwDOMyHsAM6f8A40.body:
> I am only seeing two orgs but I am in more orgs that that, how are they chosen?

Orgs to select for editing come from the below method currently

https://docs.github.com/en/rest/orgs/orgs?apiVersion=2022-11-28#list-organizations-for-the-authenticated-user

```ts
public async getGitHubUserOrgs(): Promise<string[]> {
    const octokit = await this.getOctokit();
    const response = await octokit.rest.orgs.listForAuthenticatedUser();
    return response.data.map((org: { login: string }) => org.login);
  }
```

1. Who is using this installer? I'd assume a redirect after https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-kernel/issues/171 is complete to the installer UI which would naturally be the owner of the org. Then potentially who after that? Admin & Owner? Partner defined?

2. What orgs should be shown when a partner uses the installer?

- Orgs that they are an admin of or just owner of, collaborator of?
- Should the partner determine which roles can edit the config or do we enforce rules, if we enforce then what should they be?

We sort of scrapped global config variables from `ubiquity-os-config.yml` but we could leverage that to enforce partner defined roles which can edit (at least via the UI). 

E.g: The org owner must use it after `kernel-setup` and we allow them to input roles that can edit and we write this to the config separate from `plugins:` and we read this on fetch and check against our authenticated user.

_Originally posted by @Keyrxng in https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-plugin-installer/issues/25#issuecomment-2493797026_

I_kwDOMyHsAM6eqLoB.title: Allow selecting individual repository
I_kwDOMyHsAM6eqLoB.body:
Depends on https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-plugin-installer/pull/13

RIght now partner is able to select only an organization where the bot' config should be edited.

Partner should be able to add/edit config either for an organization either for an individual repository.

I_kwDOMyHsAM6WoYYh.title: Install Plugin UI
I_kwDOMyHsAM6WoYYh.body:
When visiting a plugin URL from web browser, this UI should help us install the plugin and see/configure the default values. 

This will require GitHub authentication to commit on behalf of the user and make changes to one's `ubiquibot-config.yml` file. 

- [x] #2
- [x] #3
- [x] #4

I_kwDOMyHsAM6WoZez.title: Create add/remove config logic
I_kwDOMyHsAM6WoZez.body:
1. We need to be able to select which `ubiquibot-config.yml` to edit, so we at minimum need an organization picker after GitHub authentication. 
2. We need to parse the YML file, and target the config based on the plugin's URL/location. Then edit the YML file and push the commit using the user's credentials.

More thoughts on [the readme](https://github.com/Ubiquity-OS/ubiquity-os-plugin-installer?tab=readme-ov-file#add--remove-config).

### LLM 

Without research, the lazy answer is that LLMs can easily solve the problem. We could even consider passing in the most important/relevant files of the plugin so that the LLM can figure out all the default values and other miscellaneous details. 

But it would certainly be preferred to implement this with traditional code. I think with a bit of research it can be pretty straightforward.

I_kwDOMyHsAM6WoZg2.title: Parse & import remote `manifest.json`
I_kwDOMyHsAM6WoZg2.body:
This needs some thought, but the idea is that when we navigate to any plugin's root URL, it should redirect us to this installer (maybe installer.os.ubq.fi?)

Or an iFrame could make it look nicer potentially (no redirection.)

We can pass in the query parameter where we are coming from and then the UI can import the manifest.json (or we can pass it in directly, whatever makes more sense.)

I_kwDOMyHsAM6WoZbr.title: Setup GitHub Authentication
I_kwDOMyHsAM6WoZbr.body:
As per the `readme.md` of this repo, we need to be able to authenticate to write to the config as the user for this tool to work. 

The logic can likely be lifted from https://github.com/ubiquity/work.ubq.fi

I_kwDOLTwaCM6EnQMg.title: Automatic Transfer
I_kwDOLTwaCM6EnQMg.body:
The permit generation plugin should also optionally be able to automatically invoke a transfer to the beneficiaries. 

This introduces additional complexities because it must be able to estimate the gas fee of the transaction (given that we must support multiple EVM networks, with varying levels of activity, it is not viable to hard code this.)

In an org/repo config we can have a property related to payments `transfer: true`

It should also allow for the kernel operator (@ubiquity) to take a fee[^1^]. This fee should be configured elsewhere, perhaps in Cloudflare Worker Secrets (partner projects should not be able to change this.) Any fees that we take should be direct transfers of Ubiquity Dollars to `ubq.eth`.

[^1^]: This means we have to start thinking about how to capture value from payments moving around in our system. I think that we need to make a superior developer experience with our SDK and permit requests, so that plugin developers can easily capture value from the transactions their plugins are helping with.

I_kwDOLTwaCM66i-wI.title: Remove RPC package
I_kwDOLTwaCM66i-wI.body: Since we now have our own RPC provider, we can safely remove the rpc package to speed up the process.

I_kwDOLTwaCM6bJscQ.title: Convert plugin to cloudflare worker
I_kwDOLTwaCM6bJscQ.body:
Depends on https://github.com/ubiquity-os/permit-generation/issues/68

Related comments:
- https://github.com/ubiquity-os/permit-generation/pull/69#issuecomment-2399775638
- https://github.com/ubiquity-os/permit-generation/pull/69#issuecomment-2415718521

As far as I understand the reasons to convert this plugin from github action to cloudflare woker are:
1. It fits with the `pay.ubq.fi` rolled up feature because cloudflare functions located at `pay.ubq.fi` can call API endpoint and can not call (as well as get result) a github action
2. Other plugins (or kernel) will be able to call "generate permit" endpoint

So as a part of this issue we should:
- convert https://github.com/ubiquity-os/permit-generation to a cloudflare worker

I_kwDOLTwaCM6kEBsX.title: Worker authorization
I_kwDOLTwaCM6kEBsX.body:
Depends on https://github.com/ubiquity-os/permit-generation/issues/92

The cloudflare worker implemented in https://github.com/ubiquity-os/permit-generation/issues/92 does not have any authentication mechanic.

Right now calling the permit generation worker in a secure way should be supported by: 
- `pay.ubq.fi` worker backend
- kernel worker backend

I_kwDOLTwaCM6Z-xh_.title: Optional NFT Types
I_kwDOLTwaCM6Z-xh_.body:
> We should fix the type checks in another pull and make these optional probably?

_Originally posted by @0x4007 in https://github.com/ubiquity-os/permit-generation/pull/69#discussion_r1797709276_

Do it with TypeBox

I_kwDOLTwaCM6WkeiT.title: Convert to "Plugin"
I_kwDOLTwaCM6WkeiT.body:
- Make a GitHub Action that allows this repository to receive permit generation requests, and return permits. 
- This should be the only way to generate permits in our system, so that we can enforce capturing value from plugin developers. 
- The `X25519_PRIVATE_KEY` should be in this repository's secrets. 
- Unclear if this is viable, but add footer property so we can promote a message
   - Example: https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquibot/issues/719#issuecomment-1726227243

### Payload Schema

```
[{ [username]: amount }]
```

On the backend this should convert the username into their user ID for record keeping purposes (they can always change their username)

I_kwDOLTwaCM6VsGDy.title: Downgrade to `Ethers@v5`
I_kwDOLTwaCM6VsGDy.body: This repo I think is the only one which is using `Ethers@v6` and it would be best if the package was downgraded and any affected logic be refactored to work with `V5`

I_kwDOLTwaCM6UqyMv.title: Duplicated nonces
I_kwDOLTwaCM6UqyMv.body:
Nonces for some github issues are duplicated hence reward claiming is broken for those issues.

Check the following reward URLs:
- https://github.com/ubiquibot/user-activity-watcher/issues/14#issuecomment-2317208648
- https://github.com/ubiquibot/command-start-stop/issues/14#issuecomment-2308374805

In the decoded base64 input all nonces (per user) are equal while the expected behavior is that they should be different.

It happens because [here](https://github.com/ubiquibot/permit-generation/blob/b08723fb0ca280162053013b66873da82320f286/src/handlers/generate-erc20-permit.ts#L111) the nonce is derived from user's github id and github issue number while earlier (not sure when the code was updated) it'd been derived from `issue.node_id`.

What should be done:
- make sure nonces are not duplicated both for ERC20 permits and NFT rewards

I_kwDOLTwaCM6Q8_mC.title: fix package exports
I_kwDOLTwaCM6Q8_mC.body: The package is not error free when it is used in other codebases because of an issue with main entry of the `package.json`

I_kwDOLTwaCM6L8gUr.title: Dollar permits with fees
I_kwDOLTwaCM6L8gUr.body:
User stories:
- Partner should be able to set any ERC20 token as a permit reward (we plan to support only USD-pegged coins initially)
- Ubiquity owner should be able to set permit fees in some env variable. Notes:
  - Fees are transferred to Ubiquity treasury, if permits are regenerated then only the difference is sent
  - Use either direct transfer to ensure partner solvency either permits to the treasury
- Ubiquity owner should be able to whitelist some ERC20 tokens so that they don't incur any fees (ex: Dollar token)

This issue touches the following repositories / docs:
- https://github.com/ubiquibot/permit-generation
- https://github.com/ubiquibot/conversation-rewards
- https://github.com/ubiquity/pay.ubq.fi
- partner docs

I_kwDOLTwaCM6NX5s1.title: CI: `release-please-action` downgrades version
I_kwDOLTwaCM6NX5s1.body:
Check [this](https://github.com/ubiquibot/permit-generation/pull/23) PR which was created automatically by [this](https://github.com/ubiquibot/permit-generation/blob/37e28cca5f9a31f62a03e622eeeb7dcfad132aa4/.github/workflows/publish-package.yml) workflow.

[Initially](https://github.com/ubiquibot/permit-generation/pull/23/commits/a43f502d23c00362897b98b21dd2c950cd399941) that PR downgraded the package version to `1.0.0` although the expected behavior was to bump the version.

What should be done:
- find out and fix the root cause why [google-github-actions/release-please-action](https://github.com/ubiquibot/permit-generation/blob/37e28cca5f9a31f62a03e622eeeb7dcfad132aa4/.github/workflows/publish-package.yml#L17) downgrades the version instead of bumping it 
- make sure that on every new commit with message pattern `feat: *` a new PR is created with a bumped version

P.S. Expected release [flow](https://github.com/ubiquibot/permit-generation/pull/23#issuecomment-2178005450)

I_kwDOLTwaCM6MP0jl.title: Use `@ubiquity-dao/rpc-handler` instead of hardcoded RPC urls
I_kwDOLTwaCM6MP0jl.body:
Right now RPC urls used for reading onchain data are hardcoded in [this](https://github.com/ubiquibot/permit-generation/blob/e2633b36678a6e1749eb703dfc87bd294f47018f/src/utils/payoutConfigByNetworkId.ts) file.

What should be done:
- refactor the code to use https://github.com/ubiquity/rpc-handler that fetches the best RPC url for the provided network id
- remove hardcoded RPC URLs

I_kwDOLTwaCM6FI4sH.title: Rate Limiting
I_kwDOLTwaCM6FI4sH.body:
> ```diff
> + Evaluating results. Please wait...
> ```

We got rate limited https://github.com/ubiquibot/comment-incentives/actions/runs/8617933701/job/23619113189

I_kwDOLTwaCM6EmRzv.title: Package and GitHub agnostic option
I_kwDOLTwaCM6EmRzv.body:
The package is usable as a GitHub action but does not offer any option to be directly imported in the code of another project.
Having it as a NPM Package would greatly facitate integration within other projects without the hassle of importing submodules, or using Actions.

The package should also offer an option to be GitHub Action agnostic, because its purpose is simply to generate permits.

Tasks to be carried on:
- package, bundle and type the functions within `.d.ts` files
- export the package to NPM
- make overloads of GitHub agnostic permit generation

I_kwDONLx-s86vofeJ.title: Octokit types mismatch
I_kwDONLx-s86vofeJ.body:
I've encountered an issue multiple times when writing tests that require spying on an Octokit method. The problem arises because `@octokit/rest` is not explicitly listed in `package.json` but is installed indirectly via `plugin-sdk`.

### Example:
From [this PR](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-pull-review/pull/27):

```ts
import { RestEndpointMethodTypes } from "@octokit/rest";

jest.spyOn(context.octokit.rest.issues, "get").mockImplementation(
  async (params) =>
    ({
      data: {
        number: params?.issue_number,
        title: "issue",
        url: `https://github.com/${params?.owner}/${params?.repo}/issue/${params?.issue_number}`,
        body: "This is an issue",
        repository: {
          name: params?.repo,
          owner: {
            login: params?.owner,
          },
        },
      },
    }) as unknown as RestEndpointMethodTypes["issues"]["get"]["response"]
);
```

### Problem:
- The `plugin-sdk` includes `@octokit/rest` in its `package.json`, but it is only used for an empty string checker.
- The SDK actually uses a custom Octokit (`customOctokit`), which is created by attaching plugins such as `@octokit/plugin-rest-endpoint-methods` to `@octokit/core`.
- `@octokit/rest` itself is another version of Octokit that also uses `@octokit/plugin-rest-endpoint-methods`, but potentially a different version.
- This version mismatch causes type incompatibilities when trying to use `RestEndpointMethodTypes` from `@octokit/rest`.

### Proposed Solution:
1. Remove `@octokit/rest` from `plugin-sdk` entirely, or at least move it to `devDependencies` to prevent it from being installed in projects using the SDK.
2. Modify the SDK to export `RestEndpointMethodTypes` from `@octokit/plugin-rest-endpoint-methods`, ensuring that projects can use the exact same types as `customOctokit`.

This change will eliminate the type mismatch issues and make testing more consistent.

I_kwDONLx-s87ZlWUD.title: SDK configuration fetch
I_kwDONLx-s87ZlWUD.body:
We will definitely need to add that feature in the SDK because it might be used by many different plugins

_Originally posted by @gentlementlegen in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/pull/185#discussion_r2548671154_
            

It would be great to have helper functions exposed to fetch the configuration of the plugin from its current repo and organization. It should now be possible since the configuration keys are ensured to be unique.

## Features
- helping function to retrieve the repo (and if missing) org configuration
- handle both dev and prod configuration
- decode and return the populated configuration

## Post resolution actions
- update `command-start-stop` https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/pull/185#discussion_r2563336726
- update `text-vector-embeddings`
- update `plugin-sdk`
- update the configuration files
<!-- @ubiquity-os/text-vector-embeddings update 2025-12-01T08:15:46.949Z -->

I_kwDONLx-s86_J1mF.body:
> Otherwise, when not crashing for improper JSON formatting, the rewriting was good.

_Originally posted by @whilefoo in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-spec-rewriter/issues/1#issuecomment-3036868615_

I_kwDONLx-s87SHshp.title: Handle Deno running on GitHub Action environment
I_kwDONLx-s87SHshp.body:
When we are running a Deno project on a GitHub environment, Deno environment variables are unavailable (since it's not hosted in Deno) but rather GitHub environment is set. This doesn't stop the plugin from running, however on comment posting we end up with the wrong metadata being posted. Example with `@ubiquity-os/daemon-disqualifier`:

```
<!-- UbiquityOS - removeAllAssignees - undefined - @ubiquity-os-development[bot] - https://dash.deno.com/projects/undefined/logs?filters=%7B%22query%22%3A%22%22%2C%22timeRangeOption%22%3A%22custom%22%2C%22recentValue%22%3A%221hour%22%2C%22customValues%22%3A%7B%22start%22%3A%222025-10-17T09%3A16%3A10.575Z%22%2C%22end%22%3A%222025-10-17T09%3A18%3A10.575Z%22%7D%2C%22logLevels%22%3A%7B%22debug%22%3Atrue%2C%22info%22%3Atrue%2C%22warning%22%3Atrue%2C%22error%22%3Atrue%7D%2C%22regions%22%3A%7B%22gcp-asia-southeast1%22%3Atrue%2C%22gcp-europe-west2%22%3Atrue%2C%22gcp-europe-west3%22%3Atrue%2C%22gcp-southamerica-east1%22%3Atrue%2C%22gcp-us-east4%22%3Atrue%2C%22gcp-us-west2%22%3Atrue%7D%7D
{
  "issue": "https://github.com/Meniole/daemon-xp/issues/3",
  "logins": [
    "ubiquity-ubiquibot"
  ],
  "caller": "removeAllAssignees"
}
-->
```

https://github.com/Meniole/daemon-xp/issues/3#issuecomment-3414575865

We can see the `undefined` values and the Deno URL instead of the GitHub url. Technically, it identifies the runtime correctly through Hono, but should actually understand that GitHub is the host.

I_kwDONLx-s87G66Ci.title: Remove `empty-string-checker` workflow
I_kwDONLx-s87G66Ci.body:
Remove the deprecated workflow, add the `eslint-rule` and eventually fix empty string errors.
<!-- @ubiquity-os/text-vector-embeddings update 2025-08-20T10:14:51.443Z -->

I_kwDONLx-s87BTjlB.title: Expose the `metadata` header function
I_kwDONLx-s87BTjlB.body:
Some plugins like https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-pull-review post a comment with the pull-review, which would benefit from having the metadata in the posted comment. Since the SDK does not provide a method to pull-review with a comment, it would be easier for that plugin to have the metadata function exposed so it can append it to the posted review.

Code reference:
https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-pull-review/blob/8add28e3cafd2b8af652c0370df27f3377ea3983/src/handlers/pull-reviewer.ts#L126-L132

<!-- daemon-merging update Sun Jul 20 2025 01:55:15 GMT+0000 (Coordinated Universal Time) -->

I_kwDONLx-s87GlQAm.title: Reduce or truncate payload size to avoid GitHub API denying the call on dispatch
I_kwDONLx-s87GlQAm.body:
Sometimes plugin runs get denied because the payload is too large for GitHub API to process, resulting in a rejected run.

Example of error:
```

An error occurred while processing the plugin chain, will skip plugin {"owner":"ubiquity-os-marketplace","repo":"daemon-merging","workflowId":"compute.yml","ref":"development"} HttpError: inputs are too large. - https://docs.github.com/rest/actions/workflows#create-a-workflow-dispatch-event
    at fetchWrapper (file:///node_modules/.deno/@octokit+request@9.2.4/node_modules/@octokit/request/dist-bundle/index.js:122:11)
    at eventLoopTick (ext:core/01_core.js:178:7)
    at async sendRequestWithRetries (file:///node_modules/.deno/@octokit+auth-app@7.1.5/node_modules/@octokit/auth-app/dist-node/index.js:388:12)
    at async requestWithGraphqlErrorHandling (file:///node_modules/.deno/@octokit+plugin-retry@7.2.1/node_modules/@octokit/plugin-retry/dist-bundle/index.js:36:20)
    at async Job.doExecute (file:///node_modules/.deno/bottleneck@2.19.5/node_modules/bottleneck/light.js:405:18) {
  name: "HttpError",
  status: 422,
  request: {
    method: "POST",
    url: "https://api.github.com/repos/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-merging/actions/workflows/compute.yml/dispatches",
```

Which corresponds to the plugin not being called here: https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/issues/402#issuecomment-3197860856 or even here https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/issues/402#issuecomment-3170643373

The payload should be trucated in such case, I think the limit was around 65k characters.
<!-- @ubiquity-os/text-vector-embeddings update 2025-08-18T18:30:06.396Z -->

I_kwDONLx-s87BgEdu.title: Do not escape the callstack, only the attached content
I_kwDONLx-s87BgEdu.body:
I can try not to escape it but we had many times the case where backticks and other characters we embed in the json body broke the comments entirely. In this we embed the call stack as well and the logs and messages which are a potential hazard for the display, which is why it is entirely escaped

_Originally posted by @gentlementlegen in https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugin-sdk/pull/102#discussion_r2217835138_

I_kwDONLx-s86uN9u5.title: Octokit helper methods
I_kwDONLx-s86uN9u5.body:
We are using two GraphQL queries for fetching linked issues and fetching linked PRs so the code gets duplicated in each plugin that needs it. 

I think this is helpful for a couple of reasons:
- No duplication of code
- Less bugs because currently each repo have its own implementation (daemon-pull-reviewer's implementation was typed wrongly causing a bug)
- New contributors can use these helper methods without having to worry about implementation

Octokit supports plugins which essentially add methods to the Octokit object. 
Example usage:
```js
octokit.helpers.getLinkedIssues({
  owner,
  repo,
  prNumber
})
octokit.helpers.getLinkedPullRequests({
  owner,
  repo,
  issueNumber
})
```

I_kwDONLx-s86oPwQe.title: Handle error arrays like `AggregateError` for proper display
I_kwDONLx-s86oPwQe.body:
maybe we should also handle `AggregateError` inside the SDK to have this functionality everywhere

_Originally posted by @whilefoo in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/pull/133#discussion_r1936395440_
            

Multiple aggregated errors do not get displayed properly by the SDK using `postComment`, because it is an array of errors instead of a single error with a single message. Error messages should get concatenated altogether.

I_kwDONLx-s86gUok4.title: Improve Cloudflare Worker `postComment` metadata

I_kwDONLx-s86n6Rai.title: Add posting to pull-request review comment capability
I_kwDONLx-s86n6Rai.body:
The SDK should be able to post to pull-request threads. The API is a bit different for these as it should directly use `pull` from the REST API.

What happens right now:
- if "pull_request" is present in the payload, try to post to the pull-request itself
- if we wanted to post to the thread, we would need to use `octokit.rest.pulls.createReplyForReviewComment` or `createReviewComment`
- same thing goes for updating the comment that needs a different API
- maybe we could consider having an extra argument in the options to specify a pull-request comment ID

I_kwDONLx-s86lPsd8.title: Post comment string
I_kwDONLx-s86lPsd8.body:
Currently if you want to post a comment with no formatting, you need to do this: 
```js
await postComment(context, context.logger.info("Somthing"), { raw: true });
```
it seems redundant to me that you need to call `logger.info` so instead the function could also accept a string

I_kwDONLx-s86lF-vL.title: Change metadata order
I_kwDONLx-s86lF-vL.body:
https://github.com/Meniole/text-conversation-rewards/issues/46#issuecomment-2572236162

> Actually, the more unique the field's value is, the further to the right it should be. Again for search purposes
> 1. UbiquityOS 
> 2. GithubCommentModule.transform
> 3. aaeed77eba29c75cd9fd4771a6a192acf88b4e32 
> 4. @gentlementlegen 
> 5. https://github.com/Meniole/text-conversation-rewards/actions/runs/12626080982 
> This order seems to make sense to me for search purposes.

I_kwDONLx-s86dx5Jt.title: Allow for reuse of previously posted comments during a run
I_kwDONLx-s86dx5Jt.body: The SDK should allow to reuse the same comment during a run to avoid posting multiple comments about a result, like for example with `text-conversation-rewards` that posts a waiting comment during its run. This capability should be implemented within the SDK.

I_kwDONLx-s86j5DZK.title: Properly set the `metadata` value of `LogReturn`
I_kwDONLx-s86j5DZK.body: Revision should point to the current version of the plugin. Within actions this is easy to get, within workers I don't think this is possible, instead we could probably use the version id? _Originally posted by @gentlementlegen in https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-kernel/pull/169#discussion_r1818077183_

I_kwDONLx-s86hxP1q.title: Runs triggered with `KERNEL_PUBLIC_KEY` empty fail
I_kwDONLx-s86hxP1q.body:
When no `KERNEL_PUBLIC_KEY` is supplied, the runs fail saying the given key is too short. My suspicion is that the default key is not used but `""` is used instead. See this run for reference: https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-pricing/actions/runs/12128570532/job/33815214041

SDKs will need to get updated across plugins as well.

I_kwDOIbdbd85WsAMX.title: Graph Compounding
I_kwDOIbdbd85WsAMX.body:
I'm focusing on the "breakeven line" idea and its ability to accurately express when a position is compounded (yield is withdrawn, and redeposited.) Instead of graphing it as a ray (a line with one "extended" point) we can instead graph it as a line segment (no extended points) from the position deposit time, to when a position's size is modified. 

If we add to the position size, then the breakeven line should be steeper (more fees should be accrued due to a larger position size.) I made a quick mockup using TradingView to illustrate this idea.

![ETHUSDT_2022-11-18_11-36-13](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4975670/202611303-0420fcc3-b379-46c0-a8d8-fba435562108.png)

I_kwDOIbdbd85WRMtT.title: Dynamic Data Input
I_kwDOIbdbd85WRMtT.body:
# Dynamic Data Input on TradingView Indicator

The first major engineering challenge in order for this to be useful in realtime decision making is to allow for the script to have the capability to dynamically read position data from external data sources. I did quite a bit of research on this, and it appears that TradingView's PineScript does not currently have this capability. 

Currently the variables must be manually input on the indicator. This can be done by double clicking the indicator on TradingView.

![tv](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4975670/201572439-f6443b3e-5f1c-4abc-bc15-e8a0be86a129.jpg)

Those variables are manually imported off of the data presented by Revert's UI

https://revert.finance/#/uniswap-position/mainnet/312741

The founder of Revert passed me along this draft documentation of their private API. This was current as of 12 July 2022, as such there may have been some changes introduced since. This will allow you to access the information in JSON format. 

[apidocs.md](https://github.com/ubiquity/uniswap-v3-liquidity-provider-indicator/files/9999366/apidocs.md)

---

# Research

I would encourage you to explore workarounds.

### Workaround Strategy A - API Reverse Engineering

I would highly recommend API reverse engineering so that there is FAR LESS infrastructure to support for this capability. Then we could have a super simple node program that simply pings Revert (GET) and TradingView (POST) APIs every minute or so.

### Workaround Strategy B - Scraper

Have a remote invoked [scraper](http://github.com/ubiquity/scraper) to manually log in to your TradingView account, and

1. pass in the updated parameters using the settings panel (this might be possible by directly making the API request to update the user's settings vs having to navigate through the UI) or 
2. to hardcode the parameters and update the PineScript code.

I_kwDOORydAM6973qC.title: Multicall3 batch contract reads
I_kwDOORydAM6973qC.body:
Multicall3 contract allows us to make batch contract reads with one RPC call.
Instead of using Multicall3 contract on the project level, we can implement it in the rpc-manager server so it's completely invisible to the end user who is using the RPC.

Benefits:
- More efficient - less network requests
- Faster - not by a lot but theoretically it should be because there is less overhead
- Less likely to hit rate limits - this is the biggest benefit of this because a lot of public RPCs have rate limits

The proposed flow:

1. The client sends an array of RPC calls to the server
2. The server transforms those RPC calls into one RPC call to the Multicall3 contract and sends it to the upstream RPC provider
3. The server receives the response and transform all results to the original format
4. The server sends the response back to the client which can process the results normally - no modification needed on the client part

This only applies to contract reads so only if the RPC method is `eth_call` and contract writes (`eth_sendTransaction` method) is not recommended for Multicall3 because it has some caveats
<!-- @ubiquity-os/daemon-merging update 2025-09-06T01:21:12.707Z -->
<!-- @ubiquity-os/daemon-merging update 2025-09-07T01:35:25.148Z -->

Extend `https://rpc.ubq.fi/{networkId}` to be fully MCP compliant. Then we can add it to our LLM clients and naturally interact with any Ethereum JSON RPC spec functions!

I_kwDOORydAM69VKwN.title: Failing RPC requests
I_kwDOORydAM69VKwN.body:
## Actual behavior

There were a few instances of failing RPC requests:
1. https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/issues/215#issuecomment-3004220948
2. https://github.com/ubiquity/uusd.ubq.fi/issues/22#issuecomment-2980183793

## Expected behavior

The RPC manager should retry the request with other RPCs and eventually succeed.

## Steps to reproduce

It's still unknown how it happens but both times it happened with `nonceBitmap` function of the `permit2` contract.
This is a read-only function so there's no reason for it to fail other than RPC failing.

1. request:

```
{
    "method": "eth_call",
    "params": [
        {
            "to": "0xd635918a75356d133d5840ee5c9ed070302c9c60",
            "data": "0x4fe02b440000000000000000000000009051eda96db419c967189f4ac303a290f332768000ee2ef1f2955d6f329451c34a743ef3c99968d503773c386001baff3f247251"
        },
        "latest"
    ],
    "id": 44,
    "jsonrpc": "2.0"
}
```

2. request:

```
{
    "method": "eth_call",
    "params": [
        {
            "to": "0x000000000022d473030f116ddee9f6b43ac78ba3",
            "data": "0x4fe02b440000000000000000000000009051eda96db419c967189f4ac303a290f3327680002f6228984c6b84cc13d581bfcd3b33711271745513d7522751701eecc00b34"
        },
        "latest"
    ],
    "id": 44,
    "jsonrpc": "2.0"
}
```


## Logs

Attaching logs because they will get deleted:

### 12:17:12 PM
gcp-us-east4 — Received single request for chain 100: eth_chainId  
gcp-us-east4 — Received single request for chain 100: eth_chainId  
gcp-us-east4 — Received single request for chain 100: eth_call  
gcp-us-east4 — [Permit2RPC:error] HTTP error from https://gnosis.drpc.org: 500 Internal Server Error  
gcp-us-east4 — Error processing single request (id: 44, method: eth_call) for chain 100: JsonRpcError: Unable to perform request  
```ts
  at Permit2RpcManager.executeRpcCall (file:///src/packages/permit2-rpc-server/src/permit2-rpc-manager.ts:466:17)
  at eventLoopTick (ext:core/01_core.js:178:7)
  at async Permit2RpcManager.send (file:///src/packages/permit2-rpc-server/src/permit2-rpc-manager.ts:270:24)
  at async handler (file:///src/packages/permit2-rpc-server/src/deno-server.ts:252:22)
  at async mapped (ext:deno_http/00_serve.ts:407:18) {
    code: 19,
    data: undefined,
    httpStatus: undefined,
    name: "JsonRpcError"
  }

I_kwDOORydAM69GGoh.title: Self Healing Failure
I_kwDOORydAM69GGoh.body:
https://github.com/ubiquity/permit2-rpc-manager/actions/runs/15800896388/job/44538920266
```
Run mkdir -p out
  mkdir -p out
  bun generate-json.js
  shell: /usr/bin/bash -e {0}
error: Cannot find module '../constants/additionalChainRegistry/list.js' from '/home/runner/work/permit[2](https://github.com/ubiquity/permit2-rpc-manager/actions/runs/15800896388/job/44538920266#step:6:2)-rpc-manager/permit2-rpc-manager/lib/chainlist/utils/fetch.js'

Bun v1.2.17 (Linux x6[4](https://github.com/ubiquity/permit2-rpc-manager/actions/runs/15800896388/job/44538920266#step:6:4) baseline)
Error: Process completed with exit code 1.
```

> The job failed because it could not find the module '../constants/additionalChainRegistry/list.js' when running lib/chainlist/utils/fetch.js. This means that list.js is either missing, misnamed, or not properly included in the repository.
> 
> Solution:
> 1. Ensure that the file constants/additionalChainRegistry/list.js exists in your repository.
> 2. If you recently renamed, moved, or deleted this file, update any imports or references accordingly.
> 3. If the file should be generated or downloaded, make sure this step happens before any code attempts to import it.
> 4. Commit and push the missing or corrected file to the repository.
> 
> Example directory structure:
> 
> constants/
> └── additionalChainRegistry/
>     └── list.js  ← Make sure this exists
> 
> Code suggestion for fetch.js (import statement):
> ```js
> // lib/chainlist/utils/fetch.js
> const list = require('../../constants/additionalChainRegistry/list.js');
> ```
> If list.js should be generated, add a step in your workflow to create it before running bun generate-json.js.
> 
> Commit the missing file or fix the path, then re-run the workflow. This should resolve the error.
>

I_kwDOP8q5Hc7YjdMW.title: Handle LLM commands
I_kwDOP8q5Hc7YjdMW.body:
It seems that currently LLM commands yield no result when called through the kernel. The plugin should be capable to answer LLM calls properly.

## Features
- make the plugin LLM callable
- handle scenarios where the command or payload is wrong
- add related tests & QA

I_kwDOMQ8E8c6_cpmG.body:
> [!CAUTION]
> Resource not accessible by integration - https://docs.github.com/rest/actions/workflows#enable-a-workflow
> 
> <!-- UbiquityOS - anonymous - 3a982a83b362b5ca539f786516b2a4c56c8ef990 - @ubiquity-os[bot] - https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-merging/actions/runs/16131882524
> {
>   "message": "Resource not accessible by integration - https://docs.github.com/rest/actions/workflows#enable-a-workflow",
>   "name": "HttpError",
>   "stack": "HttpError: Resource not accessible by integration - https://docs.github.com/rest/actions/workflows#enable-a-workflow\n    at fetchWrapper (file:///home/runner/work/daemon-merging/daemon-merging/dist/plugin/index.js:3:166093)\n    at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)\n    at async Job.doExecute (file:///home/runner/work/daemon-merging/daemon-merging/dist/plugin/index.js:1:171530)"
> }
> -->
>  

 _Originally posted by @ubiquity-os-beta in [#87](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugin-sdk/issues/87#issuecomment-3047074006)_

## What happened

The workflow posts a comment when it fails to enable / disable the CRON script, which is unwanted.

## What was expected

If the script fails, the error should only be visible in the workflow runs.

I_kwDOMQ8E8c7BkSN-.title: Change storage to use KV
I_kwDOMQ8E8c7BkSN-.body:
We can see from the runs that sometimes the db fails to be pushed, due to merge conflicts. Also sometimes due to pull request merging data is lost on the way.

We could leverage KV to avoid using the json db storage and these issues.

I_kwDOMQ8E8c67s2K3.title: Make the merging run once a day
I_kwDOMQ8E8c67s2K3.body:
Currently the merging runs on `issues.assigned` event, which is not reliable since sometimes it can happen multiple times a day or sometimes never.

We should implement something similar to https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-disqualifier/issues/68 so that it runs once a day.

I_kwDOMQ8E8c6m2bGJ.title: Fix plugin runs
I_kwDOMQ8E8c6m2bGJ.body:
https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-merging/actions/runs/12859777499/job/35850693896

Currently, this plugin is not running because the configuration is wrong, and should be fixed.

I_kwDOMQ8E8c6Y9wDt.title: Contributor Approvals Should Not Be Considered
I_kwDOMQ8E8c6Y9wDt.body:
This doesn't make any sense to consider outsider approvals to automatically merge into our code, let alone default to 2. This logic should be removed. 

In theory it could be possible to run an org with a single member using this system but seems very unusual. I would remove this logic related to considering contributor approvals.  

https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-merging/blob/b9e0cf38c4bc11749f172b6212a35f95d26ab7ad/src/types/plugin-inputs.ts#L25

I_kwDOMQ8E8c6VnxKI.title: Exclude `contributor` approvals
I_kwDOMQ8E8c6VnxKI.body:
Related to https://github.com/ubiquibot/command-start-stop/issues/36

Any `contributor` role based approvals are invalid and should be excluded from any consideration in auto-merging. The only valid roles would be:

- `collaborator`
- `member`

_original [context](https://github.com/ubiquity/pay.ubq.fi/pull/290#issuecomment-2333291028)_

I_kwDOMQ8E8c6RECzQ.title: Change database pull-request tracking by organization configuration
I_kwDOMQ8E8c6RECzQ.body:
The watcher should use GitHub API to go through all opened pull-requests instead of relying on a database. The main advantage is that we could get rid of the database which sole purpose is to keep track of the pull-requests to watch.

              It keeps track of the pull request to check updates for. Eventually, we could replace that with a list of organizations inside the configuration, which would allow us to totally remove the need for a database.

_Originally posted by @gentlementlegen in https://github.com/ubiquibot/automated-merging/pull/1#discussion_r1694058679_

I_kwDOMhAZP87FNHmS.title: CowSwap Integration
I_kwDOMhAZP87FNHmS.body:
The users should be able to "deposit anything" and "withdraw anything" basically abstract the assets by natively incorporating CowSwap into the deposit and withdraw transactions on the UI. [^01^] 

Deposit flow should allow them to select any asset, and then it should swap and send to our deposit contract. The deposit contract should then return UUSD tokens. This may have a minor conflict with #25 as it has an integration with a direct curve swap (the primary UUSD market.)

The reverse flow should also work, where the user can withdraw into any asset. Again, careful of the existing curve swap code. 

---

I assume the best UX is to "lock" or disable the UI elements mid transaction. 
1. the user invokes the flow by "depositing ETH" 
2. the UI has a notice explaining the current step in the flow, that the user is swapping ETH for LUSD and locks the UI elements so they don't stop mid flow 
3. [via a post hook](https://docs.cow.fi/cow-protocol/concepts/order-types/cow-hooks) the swap completes and then the user receives their UUSD.

---

This project requires changes to the UI and creating a CowSwap post hook smart contract.

[^01^]: ⚠ 78% possible duplicate - [Handle Swap Orders in On-Ramp Experience](https://www.github.com/ubiquity/uusd.ubq.fi/issues/7#7)

I_kwDOMhAZP87COspK.title: Integrate Wallet Connect via Reown AppKit
I_kwDOMhAZP87COspK.body:
## Overview  
Enable seamless wallet connectivity in the dApp by integrating the Reown platform's AppKit toolkit, supporting wallet login, transaction approvals, and cross-chain interactions. This spec is focused on Ethereum-compatible chains using `WagmiAdapter`. The solution uses React/Next.js as the UI framework.

## Goals  
- Support secure wallet connections without exposing private keys  
- Provide users wallet management (login/logout, transaction signing)  
- Utilize Reown's AppKit for enhanced UX, security, and multi-chain support  

## Tools and Libraries  
- `@reown/appkit/react` - Core UI and functionality kit  
- `@reown/appkit-adapter-wagmi` - Adapter for Ethereum EVM chains  
- `wagmi` - Ethereum hooks for React wallet management  
- `@tanstack/react-query` - Data caching and syncing (used by AppKit)  

## Setup Steps

### 1. Project Initialization  
- Use Reown’s CLI to bootstrap your AppKit integration:  
```bash
npx @reown/appkit-cli
```
- Select your project name, framework (React or Next.js), and network library (`wagmi` recommended for EVM).  
- Enter project folder and install dependencies:  
```bash
cd your-project-name
npm install
```

### 2. Register Your Project on Reown Cloud  
- Create a free account at [[cloud.reown.com](https://cloud.reown.com/)](https://cloud.reown.com)  
- Create a new AppKit project; obtain the **Project ID** for configuration  

### 3. Environment Configuration  
- Create a `.env` file at the root of your repo, add:  
```env
NEXT_PUBLIC_PROJECT_ID=your-reown-project-id
```

### 4. AppKit Setup in Code  
- Create a config file (e.g., `config/index.tsx`) to set up AppKit with WagmiAdapter and chains:  
```tsx
'use client';

import { createAppKit } from '@reown/appkit/react';
import { WagmiAdapter } from '@reown/appkit-adapter-wagmi';
import { mainnet, arbitrum } from '@reown/appkit/networks';

const wagmiAdapter = new WagmiAdapter({
  projectId: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PROJECT_ID!,
  networks: [mainnet, arbitrum],
});

export const appKit = createAppKit({
  adapters: [wagmiAdapter],
  networks: [mainnet, arbitrum],
  projectId: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PROJECT_ID!,
  metadata: {
    name: 'Your DApp Name',
    description: 'Description of your dApp',
    url: 'https://your-dapp-url.com',
    icons: ['https://your-dapp-url.com/icon.png'],
  },
  features: {
    analytics: true, // optional
  },
});
```

### 5. Wrap Your Application with AppKit Provider  
- In your React root component (e.g., `_app.tsx` for Next.js), wrap your app with AppKit’s provider for context management:  
```tsx
import { AppKitProvider } from '@reown/appkit/react';

function MyApp({ Component, pageProps }) {
  return (
    
      
    
  );
}
export default MyApp;
```

### 6. UI Integration and Wallet Connect Button  
- Use hooks like `useAppKitAccount()` to query wallet connection state and provide connection UI  
- Build wallet connect/logout buttons in your UI, styled with Chakra UI components for consistency  
- Example minimal connect button snippet:  
```tsx
import { useAppKitAccount, useAppKitModal } from '@reown/appkit/react';
import { Button } from '@chakra-ui/react';

function WalletConnectButton() {
  const { isConnected, disconnect } = useAppKitAccount();
  const modal = useAppKitModal();

  if (isConnected) {
    return Disconnect Wallet;
  }
  return  modal.open()}>Connect Wallet;
}
```

### 7. Handling Authentication & Transactions  
- Utilize AppKit hooks for transaction requests and approvals  
- Monitor wallet status and connect errors for UX feedback with UI toast notifications or modals  

### 8. Testing & Deployment  
- Test wallet connections on supported networks (e.g., Ethereum mainnet, Gnosis)  
- Test user flows: connect, disconnect, request signatures, transaction executions  
- Deploy with environment variables securely configured  

## References and Resources  
- [[Reown AppKit Documentation](https://docs.reown.com/appkit)](https://docs.reown.com/appkit)  
- [[Reown GitHub AppKit Repo](https://github.com/reown-com/appkit)](https://github.com/reown-com/appkit)  
- [[Reown CLI for AppKit Setup](https://docs.monad.xyz/guides/reown-guide)](https://docs.monad.xyz/guides/reown-guide)  
- Sample integrations with wallet connection buttons and React hooks  
- Chakra UI for React components and styling  

[1] https://docs.monad.xyz/guides/reown-guide
[2] https://docs.reown.com/appkit/next/core/installation
[3] https://docs.getpara.com/v2/react/guides/reown-appkit
[4] https://blog.toktokhan.dev/counter-example-using-reown-appkit-with-anchor-framework-e4845d08d175
[5] https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-kApLK41MrA
[6] https://github.com/reown-com/appkit
[7] https://github.com/reown-com/appkit-web-examples
[8] https://docs.tenderly.co/virtual-testnets/dapp-ui/reown-app-kit
[9] https://www.npmjs.com/package/@reown/appkit

I_kwDOMhAZP87RBkvu.title: "Savings Account"
I_kwDOMhAZP87RBkvu.body: A thought that occurred to me is if we make a "savings account" deposit button, we can simulate a banking experience. Proposed yield mechanic: Uniswap v4 concentrated liquidity at `0.9999 - 1.0001` I'm unsure how accurate the projection is, but anything above 5% is marketable as a "savings account" I think! > > > Given your screenshot data: > • Curve pool daily volume ≈ $27.9 k > • TVL ≈ $93.5 k > • Fee ≈ 0.04 % > → Fee yield ≈ 0.012 % per day ≈ 4.4 % APR before CRV boosts (close to the shown 2–3 %). > > Now suppose you moved that volume onto a Uniswap-style narrow-range LP: > 1. Range width = 0.02 % (0.9999–1.0001) > → ≈ 5 000 × capital efficiency vs full-range. > 2. Only a fraction of swaps cross your ticks — say 20–40 % in range. > 3. Fee tier = 0.01 %. > > Approximate effective boost: > > 4.4\% \times \frac{0.01}{0.04} \times 5000 \times 0.3 \approx 165\% \text{ APR} > > If the peg drifts slightly less, uptime rises, so ~100–300 % APR is plausible; if it drifts more, you fall out of range and it collapses back to single-digit yield. > > So: > > Expect maybe ~50 ×–70 × yield boost vs Curve, only while in-range. > Out-of-range = 0 fees; you’ll need automation or active rebalancing to realize the upper bound. Of course, it is implied that we would abstract all the operations away from the user i.e. swapping half the value into the other asset, depositing the liquidity etc.

I_kwDOMhAZP87GAn3A.title: feat: Enhanced wallet connection with auto-reconnect and account switching
I_kwDOMhAZP87GAn3A.body:
## Problem
Current wallet connection lacks modern UX features:
- No automatic reconnection on page refresh
- Manual account switching not detected
- Lost connection state between sessions
- Poor handling of wallet lock/unlock events

## Solution
Implement comprehensive wallet connection improvements including persistent sessions and automatic account switching detection.

## Features Implemented
1. **Persistent Wallet Sessions**:
   - Store connected wallet address in localStorage
   - Auto-reconnect on page load if wallet still has permissions
   - Graceful fallback if permissions revoked

2. **MetaMask Account Switching**:
   - Listen for accountsChanged events
   - Automatically update UI when user switches accounts
   - Refresh balances and transaction states

3. **Enhanced Event Handling**:
   - Detect wallet lock/unlock
   - Handle chain switching
   - Respond to permission changes

4. **Improved Connection Flow**:
   - Show 'Disconnecting...' state during disconnect
   - Force wallet selection on explicit connect
   - Better error messages for connection failures

## Technical Implementation
- Event-driven architecture with WalletService
- WALLET_EVENTS enum for type-safe event handling
- Robust error handling and retry logic
- Clean separation of concerns

## Benefits
- Seamless user experience across sessions
- No need to reconnect wallet on every visit
- Instant response to account changes
- Better integration with wallet features

I_kwDOMhAZP87E0PMi.title: Abstract away mints/redeems and swaps
I_kwDOMhAZP87E0PMi.body:
We should provide the user with the best deal, whether its to mint/redeem or swap off of curve. This UX implies that the user does not need to be knowledgeable about the mint/redeem mechanics. Instead, they can just reliably come to the interface and always get the best deal. 

It might make sense to change the terminology to be more like "deposit" and "withdraw", as if its a Ubiquity bank account. It is not clear to me what the best terminology is at the moment. 

Examples:

UUSD is trading at $0.99, and user wants LUSD:
- UI should redeem LUSD (1 USD = 1 LUSD)

UUSD is trading at $1.01, and user wants LUSD:
- UI should swap (1 UUSD = 1.01 LUSD)

UUSD is trading at $0.99, and user wants UUSD:
- UI should swap (1 UUSD = 0.99 LUSD)

UUSD is trading at $1.01, and user wants UUSD:
- UI should mint (1 UUSD = 1 LUSD)

I_kwDOMhAZP87GAnjX.title: feat: Implement centralized refresh service for periodic data updates
I_kwDOMhAZP87GAnjX.body:
## Problem
Multiple components independently fetch and refresh data (prices, balances, protocol settings) leading to:
- Redundant API calls
- Inconsistent data across components
- Uncoordinated refresh cycles
- Potential rate limiting issues

## Solution
Implement a centralized refresh service that coordinates all periodic data updates across the application.

## Implementation Details
1. **CentralizedRefreshService**:
   - Single source of truth for periodic updates
   - Configurable refresh intervals
   - Publish/subscribe pattern for data distribution
   - Intelligent batching of RPC calls

2. **Features**:
   - 15-second refresh cycle for dynamic data
   - Deduplication of requests
   - Error handling with exponential backoff
   - Performance monitoring

3. **Data Types Managed**:
   - Token balances
   - UUSD price
   - Protocol settings
   - Collateral ratios
   - Oracle prices

## Benefits
- Reduced RPC calls through intelligent batching
- Consistent data across all UI components
- Better performance and user experience
- Easier to monitor and debug data flow
- Prevents rate limiting issues

## Technical Approach
- Observer pattern for component subscriptions
- Centralized error handling
- Caching layer for frequently accessed data
- Graceful degradation on service failure

I_kwDOMhAZP87GAnJx.title: feat: Implement immediate balance refresh after transactions
I_kwDOMhAZP87GAnJx.body:
## Problem
Currently, when users complete transactions (buy/sell UUSD), their token balances in the UI don't update immediately. Users see a success message but the balance display still shows old values, creating confusion.

The UI relies on a 15-second automatic refresh cycle, which means users might wait up to 15 seconds to see their updated balances.

## Solution
Implement immediate balance refresh that triggers as soon as a transaction is confirmed on-chain.

## Implementation Details
1. Add balance refresh call to transaction success handlers
2. Update SimplifiedExchangeComponent and app.ts
3. Use `void this._services.inventoryBar.refreshBalances();`

## Benefits
- Immediate visual confirmation of transaction results
- Better user experience with real-time balance updates
- Reduced user confusion about transaction status

## Test Cases
1. Buy UUSD → Balance updates immediately after confirmation
2. Sell UUSD → Balance updates immediately after confirmation
3. Multiple rapid transactions → Each updates balances correctly
4. Failed transactions → Balances remain unchanged

I_kwDOMhAZP87F1uXk.body:
- If the user has UUSD + LUSD, show buy and sell side of UI. 
- UUSD only: sell side only
- LUSD only: buy side only. 

The idea is to make the UI as simple as possible for the user to navigate. Only show what they can actually make use of.

I_kwDOMhAZP87CHbdy.title: Display Accurate Redemption Oracle Price
I_kwDOMhAZP87CHbdy.body:
The user interface at `uusd.ubq.fi` is currently displaying a misleading UUSD price, which is causing confusion for users attempting to redeem their tokens. The UI shows the spot price from the Curve pool, while the smart contract uses a different calculated Time-Weighted Average Price (TWAP) to determine redemption eligibility. This discrepancy leads users to believe they can redeem when the functionality is actually blocked by the contract's security mechanisms.

## Technical Analysis

The frontend currently displays two prices:

1.  **Spot Price**: Fetched directly from the Curve pool. This is what users see as the main price.
2.  **TWAP Price**: This is the value returned by `getDollarPriceUsd` from the `UbiquityPoolFacet`, which is the actual value used by the contract for redemption checks.

The issue is that the UI does not clearly indicate that the "TWAP" price is the one that governs redemptions, and it is not prominently displayed.

## Proposed Solution

To address this, we will modify the frontend to provide users with clear and accurate information about the redemption process.

1.  **Update Price Display**:
    *   The main price displayed on the redemption page should be the value returned by `getDollarPriceUsd`.
    *   This price should be clearly labeled as "**Redemption Oracle Price (TWAP)**" to indicate its role in the redemption process and its time-averaged nature.
    *   The spot price can be retained as a secondary reference but should be clearly distinguished from the oracle price.

2.  **Add Explanatory Tooltip**:
    *   A tooltip should be added next to the "Redemption Oracle Price (TWAP)" that explains why this price is used and how it is calculated (i.e., "This is a time-weighted average price used to prevent flash loan attacks. It is calculated based on the LUSD/USD price and the UUSD/LUSD price from the Curve pool.").
    *   This will provide transparency and help users understand why redemptions may be paused.

3.  **Dynamic Redemption Message**:
    *   The UI should display a clear, dynamic message that indicates whether redemptions are currently possible based on the "Redemption Oracle Price (TWAP)."
    *   For example, if the price is above the threshold, a message like "**Redemptions are temporarily paused because the oracle price is above the $0.99 threshold**" should be displayed prominently.

## Acceptance Criteria

*   The `uusd.ubq.fi` redemption page must display the price from `getDollarPriceUsd` as the primary price for redemptions.
*   The UI must clearly label this price as "Redemption Oracle Price (TWAP)".
*   A tooltip explaining the price calculation and its purpose must be present.
*   The UI must display a clear message indicating the current status of the redemption functionality based on the TWAP.

I_kwDOMhAZP86SZWbh.body: There is the new [UbiquityPool](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/blob/0a230f977ad91bbfb01dd28bc1d2ea77faed5237/packages/contracts/src/dollar/facets/UbiquityPoolFacet.sol) contract (facet) where users can mint and redeem `Dollar` tokens for collateral tokens. Minting example: 1. User calls [mintDollar()](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/blob/0a230f977ad91bbfb01dd28bc1d2ea77faed5237/packages/contracts/src/dollar/facets/UbiquityPoolFacet.sol#L133) and sends 95 `LUSD` + 5 Governance (`UBQ`) tokens worth 5 USD 2. User gets 100 `Dollar` tokens Redeeming example: 1. User calls [redeemDollar()](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/blob/0a230f977ad91bbfb01dd28bc1d2ea77faed5237/packages/contracts/src/dollar/facets/UbiquityPoolFacet.sol#L161) and sends 100 `Dollar` tokens 2. User waits for 2 blocks to be mined 3. User calls [collectRedemption()](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/blob/0a230f977ad91bbfb01dd28bc1d2ea77faed5237/packages/contracts/src/dollar/facets/UbiquityPoolFacet.sol#L181) and gets 95 `LUSD` + 5 Governance tokens worth 5 USD back We should create a new frontend (using https://github.com/ubiquity/ts-template) for the [UbiquityPool](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/blob/0a230f977ad91bbfb01dd28bc1d2ea77faed5237/packages/contracts/src/dollar/facets/UbiquityPoolFacet.sol) contract and make sure it works with already deployed [mainnet contracts](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/blob/0a230f977ad91bbfb01dd28bc1d2ea77faed5237/packages/contracts/broadcast/Deploy001_Diamond_Dollar_Governance.s.sol/1/run-latest.json). Notice: - The old UI may be found at https://uad.ubq.fi/ + its sources [here](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/tree/development/packages/dapp) - Try to use CSS styles from the old UI defined [here](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/tree/0a230f977ad91bbfb01dd28bc1d2ea77faed5237/packages/dapp/pages/styles) - Make sure input validation and error handling are implemented Since the [UbiquityPool](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/blob/0a230f977ad91bbfb01dd28bc1d2ea77faed5237/packages/contracts/src/dollar/facets/UbiquityPoolFacet.sol) contract is forked from the [FraxPoolV3](https://github.com/FraxFinance/frax-solidity/blob/967002fc7f2c5ee9e175cabc763a776a8ed03e01/src/hardhat/contracts/Frax/Pools/FraxPoolV3.sol) contract we can take the Frax UI as an example: - mint page: https://old.app.frax.finance/mint - redeem page: https://old.app.frax.finance/redeem Mint: Redeem:

I_kwDON4-SUc6p8AJJ.title: Add @ubiquity-os-simulant as Collaborator
I_kwDON4-SUc6p8AJJ.body:
## Overview

- If we add @ubiquity-os-simulant as a collaborator then they can really pretend to be like a normal team member. 
- My vision is that the human user watching the demo could act as "the manager"
- What this means is that @ubiquity-os-simulant can file the issue, and set a time label as we do in our culture at Ubiquity.
- Then we can write a comment as @ubiquity-os-simulant saying "Can you please set a priority label?" 
- Once the priority label is set, they can self assign `/start` and proceed with the script. 

## Requirements

This would require the demo.ubq.fi client to be able to 
1. invite @ubiquity-os-simulant and 
2. the @ubiquity-os-simulant immediately accepting invitations (listen to invite webhook?)

Based on the research below, we may need a simple Cloudflare Worker that has the PAT, and we can hit its endpoint from demo.ubq.fi to let it know that a new repository was just created and the configuration was pushed by the user. 

Then the simulant can open the issue, and send back its completion status to the demo.ubq.fi UI to render the button to start the demo. 

## Deliverables

- Cloudflare Worker backend that protects PAT exposure from dao.ubq.fi user. 
   - We send the owner/repository that was created and the worker should be able to accept the invite with the PAT, file the issue, and set the time label. 
- Changes to this UI to send an invite 
- Changes to `/demo` to listen to the `Price: ` label being set so it can `/start`
   - If another label other than `Price: ` or `Priority: ` set, it can complain and say "No, you should assign a Priority level so the pricing is set!"

## ChatGPT on automating invites
> However, if the invited user controls their own bot or script, they can automate the acceptance process using the GitHub API. Here’s how they can do it:
> 
> Automating Invitation Acceptance via GitHub API
> 	1.	Get pending invitations:
> 
> ```shell
> curl -H "Authorization: token YOUR_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN" \
>      -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json" \
>      https://api.github.com/user/memberships/orgs
> ```
> 
> 	2.	Accept an invitation (if pending):
> 
> ```shell
> curl -X PATCH -H "Authorization: token YOUR_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN" \
>      -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json" \
>      https://api.github.com/user/memberships/orgs/ORG_NAME \
>      -d '{"state":"active"}'
> ```

I_kwDOLAGaBs6MCJ5A.title: Detect events for acquisition and for conversion
I_kwDOLAGaBs6MCJ5A.body:
## Definitions
* Acquisition: When a user has signed up by logging in via metamask or similar web3 tools or via web2 username/password to client's system
* Conversion: End customer (the customer of a client) makes a meaningful purchase

## Questions

* How can we query all acquired customers
* How can we query their history log of their customer journey?
* What is the defined domain of a qualifying conversion event? E.g. at a minimum the subscription must cost at least $5 or they must make a web3 transfer of over $10 on the DeFi platform


## Needs additional specification

* For L1's, how do we track conversion events?

I_kwDOLAGaBs6MCFwk.title: Customer journey
I_kwDOLAGaBs6MCFwk.body:
Reference: https://www.notion.so/lucia-protocol/Customer-Acquisition-Cost-Financial-Metrics-6348d1bf6cd24358b175dc661bdbe871

## UI QOL and Customer Journey

* What happens when a client doesn't input cost to a marketing campaign? The UI needs to track their state and push them to do that
* What happens when a client has different costs and  has questions on how to use our product. E.g. they pay KOLs and do organic marketing on Medium and sometimes creates videos on YT. How does Lucia Attribution act as an authority figure for planning their GTM. How is this reflected in our interfaces?

I_kwDOLAGaBs6MB9iB.body:
Collects and aggregates financial information together

## Convert this to User Story Syntax
* For any client (a b2b customer or client of Lucia protocol), be able to count the number of total customers (end users of that client)


## Questions
* What needs to be done for our Machine learning system to query how many unique customers a client has? 
* Are there queries for this type of action implemented in the API?

I_kwDOI-EJSM7Q8VIR.title: Migrate backend to Deno-native + harden Deno Deploy
I_kwDOI-EJSM7Q8VIR.body:
Context
- Current backend uses Node-specific server (`@hono/node-server`) and `process.env` while deploying on Deno Deploy. This couples Node APIs to a Deno runtime and increases risk of incompatibilities and subtle failures.
- We already deploy via GitHub Actions OIDC to Deno Deploy (project: `pay-ubq-fi`). Frontend is built to `frontend/dist` and served by `backend/server.ts`.

Goals
- Run fully Deno-native on Deploy (no Node server adapter).
- Keep GitHub Actions deploy unchanged except for necessary tweaks.
- Preserve API behavior and static asset serving.

Scope of Work
- Replace server bootstrap with Deno-native:
  - Swap `@hono/node-server` + `serveStatic` for `Deno.serve` + `hono` fetch handler.
  - Use `Deno.env.get()` for configuration.
  - Serve SPA assets from `./frontend/dist` (index.html fallback).
- Supabase client
  - Import via `npm:@supabase/supabase-js` under Deno.
  - Validate ESM usage works on Deploy.
- Add `/health` endpoint returning 200 with basic info (uptime/version).
- Add `deno.jsonc` (server side) with:
  - `tasks` (dev/start/test)
  - Optional import map for `npm:` spec
  - Lint/fmt config
- CI/CD (pay.ubq.fi/.github/workflows/deploy.yml)
  - Keep OIDC deployctl step; ensure `entrypoint: backend/server.ts` still correct after refactor.
  - Ensure frontend build step remains and artifacts exist.
- CORS:
  - Keep current permissive CORS via `hono/cors` or stricter allowlist; document decision.
- Logging:
  - Ensure console logs appear in Deno Deploy logs; standardize error handling.

Acceptance Criteria
- No usage of `@hono/node-server` or `process.env` in runtime code.
- Server starts with `Deno.serve` and exports `app.fetch` handler.
- `GET /health` returns 200 JSON `{ status: "ok" }`.
- Static assets served from `frontend/dist`, SPA fallback serves `index.html`.
- Supabase calls succeed using `Deno.env.get` variables: `SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`.
- CI deploy completes via `denoland/deployctl@v1` to project `pay-ubq-fi`.
- Docs updated: .rules and docs/additional/deno-deploy-guide.md reference Deno-native server and env names.

Non-Goals
- Frontend functional changes.
- Schema or Supabase policy changes.

References
- Server: `backend/server.ts`
- CI: `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`
- Project rules: `.rules`
- Deploy guide: `docs/additional/deno-deploy-guide.md`

Test Plan
- Local: `deno run --allow-net --allow-read --allow-env backend/server.ts` and open site; verify `/api/permits/record-claim` and `/health`.
- Deploy: confirm Deploy logs, static assets, and API endpoints.

Owner/Timebox
- Est: 0.5–1 day. Assign to backend engineer familiar with Deno/Hono.

I_kwDOI-EJSM7IXUNe.title: Importing Nonces
I_kwDOI-EJSM7IXUNe.body:
We get rewards again on already completed and paid out tasks because we upgraded the permit smart contract (to support batch claims) which has a fresh set of nonces. 

### Problems

1. We should "import" the old nonces some how, because we shouldn't double pay on already completed tasks. 
2. This also means it's network dependent, which can be problematic if we switch between mainnet, optimism, and gnosis for example. 

### Solution 

https://github.com/ubiquity/pay.ubq.fi/issues/413#issuecomment-3238957881

### Remarks

1. This potentially may involve us deploying a new version of the smart contract with an import function, so that we can do it all in one transaction. 
2. We may also need to add cross-network protection on the permit rendering function (off chain)

 _Originally posted by @0x4007 in [#169](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/issues/169#issuecomment-3230856317)_

I_kwDOI-EJSM7Ia4lv.title: Fix Console Warnings: Invalid Permit Data & Validation Errors
I_kwDOI-EJSM7Ia4lv.body:
## Problem Description
The application generates 300+ console warnings when loading, primarily related to invalid permit data and validation errors. This creates noise in the console and makes debugging difficult.

## Categories of Warnings

### 1. Worker Warnings (667 instances)
```
Worker: Permit [X] with nonce Y has no owner address
Worker: Permit [X] with nonce Y has no token address  
Worker: Permit [X] with nonce Y has invalid network ID
Worker: Permit [X] with nonce Y has no deadline
Worker: Permit [X] with nonce Y has invalid signature format
```

### 2. Client-Side Validation Warnings
```
Permit X: Missing permit information
Permit X: Full Permit data: [object]
```

### 3. External Resource Warnings (2 instances)
```
Failed to load resource: net::ERR_BLOCKED_BY_CLIENT (Google Play scripts)
```

## Root Causes

1. **Data Integrity Issues**: Database contains permits with missing or invalid fields
2. **Overly Verbose Validation**: Validation functions log warnings for every invalid permit
3. **No Early Filtering**: Invalid permits propagate through the entire data pipeline

## Time Estimate
30 minutes
- **Total Implementation Time**: 30-45 minutes
- Phase 1 (Database): 12-15 minutes
- Phase 2 (Worker): 8-12 minutes  
- Phase 3 (Client): 8-12 minutes
- Phase 4 (Testing): 4-8 minutes

## Implementation Plan
See detailed implementation guide in PR description when fix is submitted.

## Expected Outcome
- Clean console output in production
- Improved application performance
- Better debugging experience
- Data integrity enforcement

## Priority
Medium - This affects developer experience and debugging capability but doesn't block functionality.

I_kwDOI-EJSM6sv2IK.title: Cow Swap Cash Out
I_kwDOI-EJSM6sv2IK.body:
I was doing some research on [implementing automated cowswap orders](https://github.com/0x4007/travel-stipend/blob/feat/auto-swap/src/cowswap-swap.ts) (they have an SDK) and I realize that we can do something pretty cool:

1. Only support settlements using UUSD. 
2. Allow the user to configure what asset they want to be paid in, it should be saved in localStorage. 
3. The cowswap quote can appear in place of the reward amount. For example, by default, loading the app for `100 UUSD` permit would show that. But if the user configured to get paid in USDT, then it might come out to something like `99.5 USDT`. The app will simply display `99.5 USDT` instead if their settings are set as such. 
4. The claim permit button would actually 1. invalidate their permit (if the wallet can afford the pay out) 2. initiate a cowswap order with the beneficiary address set to the permit beneficiary

Then now we can enforce people using UUSD in the backend but user facing they can receive rewards in any asset they want! 

I think this provides best of all worlds for users. For partners, we could charge a premium for them to use other tokens natively in the background etc.

I_kwDOI-EJSM7J87cF.title: When the network is too slow or fails, no error faces the user
I_kwDOI-EJSM7J87cF.body:
When using a slow or unresponsive network, no error is displayed to the user indicating that something went wrong. The errors only show in the console. User side, it just sees "0 permits to claim".

The error should be notified to the user,and eventually a retry should happen.

I_kwDOI-EJSM7HX8o-.title: Permit invalidation
I_kwDOI-EJSM7HX8o-.body:
We had the ability where when the funding wallet was signed in on the UI, that we would be able to invalidate any permits. 

Since we rolled out version 2, I forgot to implement this functionality. 

You can review the old code for the implementation, but basically it would invalidate the bit on the bitmap that corresponds to that permit, making it unclaimable. [^03^] 

- https://github.com/ubiquity/pay.ubq.fi/issues/73

The slightly tricky part is that we need to make sure the UI displays all of the permits for everybody that aren't claimed already. Unlike before, where it would only display the single permit and its claimed status. [^02^]

> 
>  

 _Originally posted by @gentlementlegen in [#207](https://github.com/ubiquity/work.ubq.fi/issues/207#issuecomment-3213827501)_ [^01^]

I_kwDOI-EJSM6maBLt.title: Automatic Population of Permits
I_kwDOI-EJSM6maBLt.body:
### February 2025 Addendum - Use Supabase Instead!

I just realized that we actually capture all the generated permits in our database. 

Be sure to check for doubles that have the same nonce. This means that the permits were generated multiple times for the same issue. Pick the higher value one if there are two of the same nonce. 

### Original Spec

- Using our metadata system we can easily GraphQL query for permits. 
- import the "login with github" button from work.ubq.fi 
- GraphQL query with authentication for private repos. 
- limit to previous 100 results or whatever is the max that GraphQL can return in a single shot 
- check if they have already been claimed, if so, discard 
- if unclaimed permits are found, import into the UI (we should already support multiple permits to be loaded in the UI)

```html

I_kwDOI-EJSM6tGzxH.body:
Take a look at this log: https://github.com/ubiquity/pay.ubq.fi/actions/runs/13731352779/job/38410431355

```bash
Run echo "Runner name: $RUNNER_NAME"
  echo "Runner name: $RUNNER_NAME"
  if [[ "$RUNNER_NAME" != *"GitHub Actions"* ]]; then
    echo "This workflow must run on a GitHub Actions runner. (github-actions[bot])"
    exit 1
  fi
  shell: /usr/bin/bash -e {0}
Runner name: GitHub-Actions-1000000[4](https://github.com/ubiquity/pay.ubq.fi/actions/runs/13731352779/job/38410431355#step:2:4)55

The workflow expects the runner to have "GitHub Actions" as a prefix, but apparently "GitHub-Actions" should also be allowed.

I_kwDOI-EJSM6p-QFG.title: Only Allow Card Minting With UUSD
I_kwDOI-EJSM6p-QFG.body:
Our core strategy to boost adoption of our dollar is to offer perks like being able to mint payment cards. 

Disable mints for any token except for UUSD. 

Side note: it would be really interesting if we could also simulate credit with UBQ. Imagine holding UBQ and borrowing off of it! It may require an aave fork and some more integration but that can be discussed in the future. 

> I see this problem can occur again as the UbiquityOS is issuing WXDAI permits and card feature is still expecting UUSD permits. Is @zugdev working on anything related to this? 
> We have to pick 1 of these options, where first one was suggested earlier.
> - card feature uses only uusd, disabled for wxdai
> - card feature uses only wxdai, disabled for uusd
> - support both uusd & wxdai
>  

 _Originally posted by @EresDev in [#369](https://github.com/ubiquity/pay.ubq.fi/issues/369#issuecomment-2650169554)_
