I_kwDOOyHL-s69t4qC.title: Handle Web App deploys
I_kwDOOyHL-s69t4qC.body:
> So one observation that makes things tricky for web apps deploy scripts at the current stage: apparently we need to make a deno server file and then "include" that during the `deployctl` invocation, which implies we need a new web app template that always has a server file in the same location with the same name for our deploy template to "just work" 
> 
> I spent so much time debugging deployments over at uusd.ubq.fi and health.ubq.fi lately. 
> 
> ---
> 
> Our ubq.fi-router, which automatically detects and routes subdomains to the correct deployment (deno first, then cloudflare pages) currently only supports preview branches for plugins. This is for the beta instance of the bot to pick up on. We haven't had the need for "previews" to be hosted for apps so I didn't add that support. 

 _Originally posted by @0x4007 in [#5](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/deno-deploy/issues/5#issuecomment-3010246441)_

We should be able to deploy Web Apps to Deno with an Action. We should figure out what this script is missing to do so properly, or eventually create a new script if the changes are too complex.

I_kwDOOyHL-s67N7Rj.title: Split the script into two distinct Actions
I_kwDOOyHL-s67N7Rj.body:
It would be nice to split the deploy into two different scripts, which would make more sense in terms on reusability for other projects.

Example:
<details>

<summary>new deno deploy template</summary>

https://github.com/0x4007/pi-vpn/blob/main/.github/actions/deno-deploy/action.yml

```yaml
name: "Deno Deploy Reusable Template"
description: "Checks out the repository and publishes or deletes the project on Deno Deploy. The project name must be provided by the workflow."
inputs:
  action:
    description: "Action to perform: 'deploy' or 'delete' the project."
    required: true
    default: "deploy"
  token:
    description: "Deno Deploy access token."
    required: true
  entrypoint:
    description: "The entrypoint to the script that will be deployed."
    required: false
    default: "worker/main.ts"
  root:
    description: "The root directory of the script that will be deployed."
    required: false
    default: "."
  project_name:
    description: "The Deno Deploy project name. Must be provided by the workflow."
    required: true
  organization:
    description: "The organization to target when deploying the project. If omitted, will default to the user account."
    required: false
  env_file:
    description: "Path to .env file to use during deployment"
    required: false
    default: ""
  production:
    description: "Deploy to production (uses --prod flag)"
    required: false
    default: "true"
outputs:
  deployment_url:
    description: "The deployment URL"
    value: ${{ steps.get_deployment_url.outputs.url }}
runs:
  using: "composite"
  steps:
    - name: Checkout
      uses: actions/checkout@v4.2.2

    - name: Setup Deno
      uses: denoland/setup-deno@v2
      with:
        deno-version: v2.x

    - name: Install deployctl
      shell: bash
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail
        deno install --global --allow-read --allow-write --allow-env --allow-net --allow-run --allow-sys --no-check -r -f https://deno.land/x/deploy@1.12.0/deployctl.ts
        echo "DENO_PROJECT_NAME=${{ inputs.project_name }}" >> $GITHUB_ENV

        # Verify installation
        if ! command -v deployctl &> /dev/null; then
          echo "❌ ERROR: deployctl installation failed"
          exit 1
        fi
        echo "✅ deployctl installed successfully"

    - name: Manage Deno Deploy Project (Deploy)
      if: inputs.action == 'deploy'
      shell: bash
      env:
        DENO_DEPLOY_TOKEN: ${{ inputs.token }}
        DENO_PROJECT_NAME: ${{ inputs.project_name }}
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail
        echo "Deploying project: $DENO_PROJECT_NAME"

        # Validate entrypoint exists
        if [ ! -f "${{ inputs.root }}/${{ inputs.entrypoint }}" ]; then
          echo "❌ ERROR: Entrypoint file not found: ${{ inputs.root }}/${{ inputs.entrypoint }}"
          exit 1
        fi

        # Function to try creating project
        create_project() {
          local org_flag="$1"
          deployctl projects create \
            --token=$DENO_DEPLOY_TOKEN \
            --project=$DENO_PROJECT_NAME \
            $org_flag \
            --color=never 2>&1
        }

        # Function to check if project exists
        project_exists() {
          deployctl projects show \
            --token=$DENO_DEPLOY_TOKEN \
            --project=$DENO_PROJECT_NAME \
            --color=never > /dev/null 2>&1
        }

        # Check if project already exists
        if project_exists; then
          echo "Project already exists, skipping creation"
        elif [ -n "${{ inputs.organization }}" ]; then
          echo "Attempting to create project in organization: ${{ inputs.organization }}"

          # Try to create with organization
          output=$(create_project "--org=${{ inputs.organization }}")
          exit_code=$?

          if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
            echo "$output"

            # Check if error is due to missing organization
            if echo "$output" | grep -q -E "(organization|org|team) .* (not found|does not exist|doesn't exist)"; then
              echo ""
              echo "⚠️  WARNING: Organization '${{ inputs.organization }}' not found in Deno Deploy"
              echo ""
              echo "Attempting to create project under personal account instead..."

              # Try without organization (personal account)
              if ! create_project ""; then
                echo ""
                echo "❌ ERROR: Failed to create project in both organization and personal account"
                echo ""
                echo "To fix this issue, you have two options:"
                echo ""
                echo "1. Create a Deno Deploy organization/team named '${{ inputs.organization }}' at:"
                echo "   https://dash.deno.com/account#organizations"
                echo ""
                echo "2. Remove the organization parameter from your workflow to deploy to your personal account"
                echo ""
                echo "For more information, see: https://docs.deno.com/deploy/manual/organizations"
                exit 1
              fi

              echo "✅ Successfully created project under personal account"
              echo ""
              echo "ℹ️  Note: To deploy to an organization in the future, create a team named"
              echo "   '${{ inputs.organization }}' at https://dash.deno.com/account#organizations"
            else
              # Other error, exit
              echo "❌ ERROR: Failed to create project"
              exit 1
            fi
          else
            echo "✅ Successfully created project in organization: ${{ inputs.organization }}"
          fi
        else
          # No organization specified, create in personal account
          echo "Creating project in personal account (no organization specified)"
          if ! create_project ""; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: Failed to create project"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "✅ Successfully created project"
        fi

        # Build deployment command
        DEPLOY_CMD="deployctl deploy \
          --token=$DENO_DEPLOY_TOKEN \
          --project=$DENO_PROJECT_NAME \
          --entrypoint=${{ inputs.entrypoint }} \
          --root=${{ inputs.root }} \
          --color=never"

        # Add production flag if enabled
        if [ "${{ inputs.production }}" = "true" ]; then
          DEPLOY_CMD="$DEPLOY_CMD --prod"
        fi

        # Add env file if provided
        if [ -n "${{ inputs.env_file }}" ] && [ -f "${{ inputs.env_file }}" ]; then
          DEPLOY_CMD="$DEPLOY_CMD --env-file=${{ inputs.env_file }}"
        fi

        # Execute deployment
        echo ""
        echo "Executing deployment..."
        eval $DEPLOY_CMD

    - name: Get Deployment URL
      if: inputs.action == 'deploy'
      id: get_deployment_url
      shell: bash
      env:
        DENO_DEPLOY_TOKEN: ${{ inputs.token }}
        DENO_PROJECT_NAME: ${{ inputs.project_name }}
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail

        # Get the deployment URL from Deno
        echo "Fetching deployment URL..."
        PROJECT_INFO=$(deployctl projects show --token=$DENO_DEPLOY_TOKEN --project=$DENO_PROJECT_NAME --color=never || true)

        # Try multiple patterns to extract URL
        PROJECT_URL=""
        if [ -n "$PROJECT_INFO" ]; then
          # Try to extract from Domain(s): line
          PROJECT_URL=$(echo "$PROJECT_INFO" | grep -i "domain" | sed -E 's/.*Domain\(s\):[[:space:]]+([^[:space:]]+).*/\1/' || true)

          # If not found, try to find any .deno.dev URL
          if [ -z "$PROJECT_URL" ]; then
            PROJECT_URL=$(echo "$PROJECT_INFO" | grep -o "[a-zA-Z0-9-]*\.deno\.dev" | head -n1 || true)
          fi
        fi

        # Fallback to constructed URL if extraction failed
        if [ -z "$PROJECT_URL" ]; then
          PROJECT_URL="https://${DENO_PROJECT_NAME}.deno.dev"
          echo "⚠️  WARNING: Could not extract URL from deployctl output, using constructed URL: $PROJECT_URL"
        fi

        echo "url=$PROJECT_URL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        echo "Deployment URL: $PROJECT_URL"

        # Write to step summary
        echo "### 🚀 Deployment Complete" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
        echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
        echo "**Project:** $DENO_PROJECT_NAME" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
        echo "**URL:** $PROJECT_URL" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
        if [ "${{ inputs.production }}" = "true" ]; then
          echo "**Environment:** Production" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
        else
          echo "**Environment:** Preview" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
        fi

    - name: Manage Deno Deploy Project (Delete)
      if: inputs.action == 'delete'
      shell: bash
      env:
        DENO_DEPLOY_TOKEN: ${{ inputs.token }}
        DENO_PROJECT_NAME: ${{ inputs.project_name }}
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail
        echo "Deleting project: $DENO_PROJECT_NAME"

        # Attempt to delete the project
        if deployctl projects delete \
          --token=$DENO_DEPLOY_TOKEN \
          --project=$DENO_PROJECT_NAME \
          --force \
          --color=never; then
          echo "✅ Successfully deleted project: $DENO_PROJECT_NAME"

          # Write to step summary
          echo "### 🗑️ Project Deleted" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "**Project:** $DENO_PROJECT_NAME" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "**Status:** Successfully deleted" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
        else
          echo "❌ ERROR: Failed to delete project: $DENO_PROJECT_NAME"

          # Write error to step summary
          echo "### ❌ Deletion Failed" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "**Project:** $DENO_PROJECT_NAME" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "**Status:** Failed to delete" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          exit 1
        fi
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>new plugin adapter template</summary>

https://github.com/0x4007/pi-vpn/blob/main/.github/actions/plugin-adapter/action.yml

```yaml
name: "Deno Deploy Plugin Adapter"
description: "Converts a plugin into a Deno-compatible deployment by creating entry points and handling manifest updates."
inputs:
  pluginEntry:
    description: "The path to the plugin entry file. It is expected to export 'fetch' as a default."
    required: false
    default: "./worker"
outputs:
  project_name:
    description: "The generated Deno project name"
    value: ${{ steps.prepare_env.outputs.project_name }}
  deployment_url:
    description: "The deployment URL (only available after deployment)"
    value: ${{ steps.prepare_env.outputs.deployment_url }}
  entrypoint:
    description: "The generated Deno entry point path"
    value: "src/deno.ts"
  manifest_exists:
    description: "Whether manifest.json exists in the repository"
    value: ${{ steps.check_manifest.outputs.exists }}
  env_file_path:
    description: "Path to the temporary .env file (in CI temp directory)"
    value: ${{ steps.generate_env.outputs.env_file_path }}
runs:
  using: "composite"
  steps:
    - name: Prepare Environment Variables
      id: prepare_env
      shell: bash
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail

        # Extract branch name and sanitize it
        branch_name=$(echo '${{ github.event.ref || github.event.workflow_run.head_branch || github.ref }}' | sed 's#refs/heads/##')

        # Create a more unique sanitization to avoid collisions
        # Replace slashes with double dashes to distinguish from regular dashes
        sanitized_branch=$(echo "$branch_name" | sed 's#/#--#g' | sed 's#[^a-zA-Z0-9-]#-#g')

        project_name="${{ github.event.repository.name }}"
        new_name="${project_name}-${sanitized_branch}"

        # Deno project names cannot exceed 26 characters
        # If too long, use hash of the branch name to ensure uniqueness
        if [ ${#new_name} -gt 26 ]; then
          # Get first 10 chars of project name and 8 chars of branch hash
          project_prefix=$(echo "$project_name" | cut -c 1-10)
          branch_hash=$(echo "$branch_name" | sha256sum | cut -c 1-8)
          new_name="${project_prefix}-${branch_hash}"
        fi

        echo "DENO_PROJECT_NAME=$new_name" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        echo "project_name=$new_name" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        DENO_DEPLOYMENT_ID=${{ github.sha }}
        echo "DENO_DEPLOYMENT_ID=$DENO_DEPLOYMENT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV

        echo "Generated project name: $new_name"

    - name: Generate .env File in Temp Directory
      id: generate_env
      shell: bash
      run: |
        # Create a temporary directory for the .env file
        TEMP_ENV_DIR="${RUNNER_TEMP}/deno-deploy-env"
        mkdir -p "$TEMP_ENV_DIR"
        ENV_FILE_PATH="$TEMP_ENV_DIR/.env"

        cat > generate-env.ts <<'EOF'
        const envVars = Deno.env.toObject();
        const filtered = Object.entries(envVars)
          .filter(([k, v]) => !/^(GITHUB_|RUNNER_|CI|HOME|PATH|PWD|SHELL|USER|LANG|LC_|TZ|ACTIONS_)/.test(k))
          .filter(([k, v]) => v !== undefined && v !== null && v.trim() !== "")
          .reduce((acc, [k,v]) => ({...acc, [k]:v}), {});

        const envPath = Deno.args[0];
        await Deno.writeTextFile(envPath,
          Object.entries(filtered)
            .map(([k,v]) => `${k}="${v.replace(/"/g, '\\"')}"`)
            .join("\n")
        );
        EOF

        deno run --allow-env --allow-write="$TEMP_ENV_DIR" generate-env.ts "$ENV_FILE_PATH"
        rm generate-env.ts

        echo "env_file_path=$ENV_FILE_PATH" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        echo "Generated .env file at: $ENV_FILE_PATH"

    - name: Create Deno Entry Point
      shell: bash
      run: |
        mkdir -p src
        cat << 'EOF' > src/deno.ts
        import worker from "${{ inputs.pluginEntry }}";
        export default {
          async fetch(request: Request, env: Record<string, unknown>, executionCtx?: ExecutionContext) {
            Object.assign(env, Deno.env.toObject());
            return worker.fetch(request, env, executionCtx);
          },
        };
        EOF

    - name: Fix Import Extensions
      shell: bash
      run: |
        find src -name "*.ts" -type f -exec sh -c '
          sed -i.bak "
            s/from \"\(\.\+[^\"]*\)\.json\"/from \"\1.json\" with { type: \"json\" }/g;
            s/from '"'"'\(\.\+[^'"'"']*\)\.json'"'"'/from '"'"'\1.json'"'"' with { type: \"json\" }/g;
            s/from \"\(\.\+[^\"]*\)\"/from \"\1.ts\"/g;
            s/from '"'"'\(\.\+[^'"'"']*\)'"'"'/from '"'"'\1.ts'"'"'/g;
            s/\.json\.ts\" with { type: \"json\" }/.json\" with { type: \"json\" }/g;
            s/\.css\.ts/.css/g;
            s/\.svg\.ts/.svg/g;
            s/\.ts\.ts/.ts/g
          " "$1"
        ' _ {} \;
        find src -name "*.ts.bak" -delete

    - name: Check if manifest.json exists
      id: check_manifest
      shell: bash
      run: |
        if [ -f manifest.json ]; then
          echo "exists=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        else
          echo "exists=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        fi

    - name: Update manifest.json with deployment URL
      if: steps.check_manifest.outputs.exists == 'true'
      shell: bash
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail

        # Construct the deployment URL
        DEPLOYMENT_URL="https://${{ steps.prepare_env.outputs.project_name }}.deno.dev"
        echo "Updating manifest.json with deployment URL: $DEPLOYMENT_URL"

        # Create a Node.js script to update the manifest
        cat > update-manifest.js <<'EOF'
        const fs = require('fs');
        const path = require('path');

        const manifestPath = path.resolve(process.cwd(), './manifest.json');
        const deploymentUrl = process.argv[2];

        if (!fs.existsSync(manifestPath)) {
          console.error('manifest.json not found');
          process.exit(1);
        }

        try {
          const manifest = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(manifestPath, 'utf8'));
          manifest["homepage_url"] = deploymentUrl;

          const updatedManifest = JSON.stringify(manifest, null, 2);
          fs.writeFileSync(manifestPath, updatedManifest);
          console.log('Successfully updated manifest.json');
          console.log('Updated content:', updatedManifest);
        } catch (error) {
          console.error('Error updating manifest.json:', error);
          process.exit(1);
        }
        EOF

        # Run the Node.js script
        node update-manifest.js "$DEPLOYMENT_URL"

        # Clean up
        rm update-manifest.js

        # Format the manifest.json file with deno fmt if available
        if command -v deno &> /dev/null; then
          deno fmt --ext=json manifest.json || true
        fi

    - name: Store deployment URL
      id: store_url
      shell: bash
      run: |
        # This will be updated by the workflow after deployment
        echo "deployment_url=" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>example of how to use both</summary>

https://github.com/0x4007/pi-vpn/blob/main/.github/workflows/example.yml

```yaml
name: Deploy Plugin to Deno

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - develop
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      action:
        description: 'Action to perform'
        required: true
        default: 'deploy'
        type: choice
        options:
          - deploy
          - delete

env:
  # GitHub App credentials for manifest updates (optional)
  APP_ID: ${{ secrets.APP_ID }}
  APP_PRIVATE_KEY: ${{ secrets.APP_PRIVATE_KEY }}

jobs:
  deploy-plugin:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Determine if we're on the default branch for production deployments
      - name: Check if default branch
        id: check-branch
        shell: bash
        run: |
          # Get the default branch name from GitHub API
          DEFAULT_BRANCH=$(curl -s -H "Authorization: token ${{ github.token }}" \
            "https://api.github.com/repos/${{ github.repository }}" | \
            jq -r '.default_branch')

          # Get current branch name
          CURRENT_BRANCH="${{ github.ref_name }}"

          # Check if we're on the default branch
          if [ "$CURRENT_BRANCH" = "$DEFAULT_BRANCH" ]; then
            echo "is_default_branch=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "On default branch ($DEFAULT_BRANCH), will deploy to production"
          else
            echo "is_default_branch=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "Not on default branch (current: $CURRENT_BRANCH, default: $DEFAULT_BRANCH), will deploy to preview"
          fi

      # Step 1: Prepare the plugin for Deno deployment
      - name: Adapt Plugin for Deno
        id: plugin-adapter
        uses: ./.github/actions/plugin-adapter
        with:
          pluginEntry: "./worker"  # Path to your plugin entry file

      # Step 2: Deploy to Deno using the adapted plugin
      - name: Deploy to Deno
        id: deno-deploy
        if: github.event.inputs.action != 'delete'
        uses: ./.github/actions/deno-deploy
        with:
          action: deploy
          token: ${{ secrets.DENO_DEPLOY_TOKEN }}
          project_name: ${{ steps.plugin-adapter.outputs.project_name }}
          entrypoint: ${{ steps.plugin-adapter.outputs.entrypoint }}
          root: "."
          organization: ${{ github.repository_owner }}  # Dynamically use GitHub organization
          env_file: ${{ steps.plugin-adapter.outputs.env_file_path }}  # Use temp env file path
          production: ${{ steps.check-branch.outputs.is_default_branch }}  # Only production on default branch

      # Step 3: Post-deployment tasks

      - name: Get GitHub App token
        if: github.event.inputs.action != 'delete' && env.APP_ID != '' && env.APP_PRIVATE_KEY != ''
        uses: actions/create-github-app-token@v1
        id: app-token
        with:
          app-id: ${{ env.APP_ID }}
          private-key: ${{ env.APP_PRIVATE_KEY }}

      - name: Commit manifest changes
        if: github.event.inputs.action != 'delete' && steps.plugin-adapter.outputs.manifest_exists == 'true'
        uses: swinton/commit@v2.x
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token || env.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          files: |
            manifest.json
          commit-message: "chore: [skip ci] update manifest.json url"
          ref: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.head_branch || github.ref }}

      - name: Write Deployment URL to Summary
        if: github.event.inputs.action != 'delete'
        shell: bash
        run: |
          echo "### Deployment URL" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "${{ steps.deno-deploy.outputs.deployment_url }}" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          if [ "${{ steps.check-branch.outputs.is_default_branch }}" = "true" ]; then
            echo "**Environment:** Production" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          else
            echo "**Environment:** Preview" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          fi

      # Delete action
      - name: Delete Deno Project
        if: github.event.inputs.action == 'delete'
        uses: ./.github/actions/deno-deploy
        with:
          action: delete
          token: ${{ secrets.DENO_DEPLOY_TOKEN }}
          project_name: ${{ steps.plugin-adapter.outputs.project_name }}

      - name: Write Deletion to Summary
        if: github.event.inputs.action == 'delete'
        shell: bash
        run: |
          echo "### Deployment URL" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "Deployment \`${{ steps.plugin-adapter.outputs.project_name }}\` has been deleted." >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
```
</details>

I_kwDOLaCxqM6BBQ8W.title: Generate Default Organization Configuration App
I_kwDOLaCxqM6BBQ8W.body:
We should have a simple page that will help new partners generate a basic UbiquiBot organization config with our recommended default settings. 

They should be able to login with GitHub and then the bot can handle the rest, which would include creating the `ubiquibot-config` repository and adding a `ubiquibot-config/.github/ubiquibot-config.yml` file. 

As a side note, I think it makes sense for us to rename the repository to `.ubiquibot-config` to clearly indicate to our partners that it is meant to be a private repository. I'm not sure how far along we will be in updating all of our infrastructure to accomodate this change, but keep this in mind when you start this task. 

> > github's app installation token

> No this can be generated with Octokit. We do that right now in the kernel. We can store the "app private key" in our backend/secrets, as well as the "app id". The user just needs to pass in their installation ID for us to authenticate under their installation

> https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquibot-kernel/blob/af8fe436648aa6012ea885582f5e45c1e97f1bb0/src/github/github-event-handler.ts#L33-L39

> @whilefoo and I have been working with this code and the authentication definitely works.

> ---

> I just realized that we can probably handle this automatically for the user. We can make a "Login with GitHub" button so that we know what organizations they are associated with, then we can look up any overlap with all the organizations that the bot is installed to and then automatically identify the installation ID. 

> More information here: https://chat.openai.com/c/70e59735-9fde-4c97-814b-e56164a59913

_Originally posted by @pavlovcik in https://github.com/ubiquity/pay.ubq.fi/issues/65#issuecomment-1945702584_

I_kwDOM7ZNU86jO-K0.title: Arbitrage bot
I_kwDOM7ZNU86jO-K0.body:
In order to maintain [Dollar token](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/blob/0a230f977ad91bbfb01dd28bc1d2ea77faed5237/packages/contracts/src/dollar/core/UbiquityDollarToken.sol) USD peg [LibUbiquityPool](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/blob/0a230f977ad91bbfb01dd28bc1d2ea77faed5237/packages/contracts/src/dollar/libraries/LibUbiquityPool.sol) provides the following arbitrage mechanic:
1. If Dollar token USD price >1$ (on a secondary market) then arbitragers are incentivised to 
a) Mint 1 Dollar token for 1 USD of collateral in the [LibUbiquityPool](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/blob/0a230f977ad91bbfb01dd28bc1d2ea77faed5237/packages/contracts/src/dollar/libraries/LibUbiquityPool.sol)
b) Sell 1 Dollar token on a secondary market
2. On the contrary, If Dollar token USD price <1$ (on a secondary market) then arbitragers are incentivised to:
a) Buy cheap Dollar token on a secondary market
b) Redeem Dollar token for collateral worth 1 USD in the [LibUbiquityPool](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/blob/0a230f977ad91bbfb01dd28bc1d2ea77faed5237/packages/contracts/src/dollar/libraries/LibUbiquityPool.sol)

On early project stage any large swap may affect the Dollar token price greatly and the pool (where the swap occurred) may remain unbalanced for a long time which in turn depegs the Dollar token.

As a part of this issue we should create an arbitrage bot (i.e. `nodejs` app) which could be run on VPS or any suitable PAAS (heroku, cloudflare, etc...) to catch arbitrage opportunities in order to keep the Dollar token USD pegged on early project stage.

What should be done:
1. Create a new `nodejs` app that will catch arbitrage opportunities for the Dollar token
2. Add unit tests for basic functionality
3. Prepare a clear README of how to run the bot

Notes:
- It makes sense to create the arbitrage bot in a separate repository.
- Deployed contract addresses (mainnet): [here](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/blob/0a230f977ad91bbfb01dd28bc1d2ea77faed5237/packages/contracts/broadcast/Deploy001_Diamond_Dollar_Governance.s.sol/1/run-latest.json)

I_kwDOM7ZNU87CHzr2.title: Multi Chain Arbitrage
I_kwDOM7ZNU87CHzr2.body:
This bot must target the following markets for now:
1. Mainnet https://www.curve.finance/dex/ethereum/pools/factory-stable-ng-164/swap/
2. Gnosis https://www.curve.finance/dex/xdai/pools/factory-stable-ng-29/swap/

The cross chain mechanism arbitrage is more complex because it would move liquidity back to Mainnet. For example, because now we have UUSD trading at $0.97 on Gnosis:

1. DAI -> bridge -> xDAI -> WXDAI
2. buy UUSD at a 3% discount 
3. bridge UUSD to Mainnet
4. Sell UUSD for LUSD
5. Profit 3% in LUSD

The system would have to estimate all the gas fees involved in the bridging and do this profitably. This will allow UUSD to maintain peg across any chain.

I_kwDOM7ZNU87CHVDr.title: Triggering Arbitrage
I_kwDOM7ZNU87CHVDr.body: Expose a RESTful endpoint to trigger the bot. The vision is when we access uusd.ubq.fi, the UI should invoke the bot to make a trade. This will prevent excessive unnecessary use of CRON invocations, and RPC calls, and possibly even ETH.

I_kwDOO9rGXc68L6_d.title: Upgrade ubq.fi request router
I_kwDOO9rGXc68L6_d.body:
Since we are migrating to deno deploy, our dynamic subdomain router should do the following:

1. continue serving `*.ubq.fi` -> `*-ubq-fi.pages.dev`
2. also serve `*.ubq.fi` -> `*-ubq-fi.deno.dev`
3. prioritize deno deploys
4. intelligent caching (KV)

In addition it should also work for our plugins, similar to the logic above. 

The complication is understanding how to point to the correct branch of the plugin, so need to make it check the ubiquity-os-marketplace GitHub and cross reference the plugin names to understand where the plugin name stops and the branch name begins (from within the *.deno.dev or *.pages.dev URLs)

---

Addendum:

Simplify to Deno support only (remove KV)

I_kwDOLcmjKM7PQVxG.title: Could be cool to make the llm automatically set the time using /time with no parameter.
I_kwDOLcmjKM7PQVxG.body:
Shouldn't we move this to `ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-time`? 

> Could be cool to make the llm automatically set the time using /time with no parameter. 

_Originally posted by @0x4007 in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/issues/434#issuecomment-3360704312_

I also think that we should capture the issue created and issue edited events and automatically set time. These should be configurable/enabled via the config. 

The prompt should also be configurable in case teams are faster/slower at certain types of projects or if there is relevant context to pass in for the automatic estimates.

I_kwDOLcmjKM7L3SNA.title: Anybody Setting Time
I_kwDOLcmjKM7L3SNA.body:
If the time label isn't set, we should allow anybody to set it, and then respect a hierarchy:

1. Collaborator (although they can just use the UI, so seems kind of pointless.)
2. Author (only if unset or set by lower rank.)
3. Anybody (only if unset.)


> [!WARNING]
> Only admins, collaborators, or the issue author can set time estimates.

_Originally posted by @ubiquity-os in https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquity-dollar/issues/1001#issuecomment-3292067729_

I_kwDOLcmjKM6qBpvq.title: Scraper: Scrape Issue Threads with Time estimates.
I_kwDOLcmjKM6qBpvq.body:
Build a scraper that creates a JSONL compatible dataset, following OpenAI's spec for the fine-tune dataset[^1^], to be used with their framework.

We need close to 250 to 300 well annotated and clean examples, for the training set.    And a separate validation set which should have another 100 to 150 examples.


[^1^]: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/fine-tuning#example-format

I_kwDOLcmjKM6vmNvz.title: Migrate to Deno Deploy
I_kwDOLcmjKM6vmNvz.body:
I was doing research and I have a feeling this might be our best serverless provider. 

https://deno.com/deploy/pricing

50ms execution time on the free tier should prevent the consistent problems we have had with this plugin. 

Assuming this works well, we should consider migrating all the other projects slowly over to deno deploy.

I_kwDOLcmjKM7DHD1_.title: Bot warns about not being allowed to set labels but doesn't undo the action
I_kwDOLcmjKM7DHD1_.body:
## Bug Description

The daemon-pricing bot posts a warning message stating "You are not allowed to set labels" but doesn't actually undo the label setting action, making the warning ineffective.

## Expected Behavior

When a user is not allowed to set labels, the bot should:
1. Either prevent the label from being set in the first place, OR
2. Remove the label after detecting unauthorized access AND post the warning

## Actual Behavior

The bot only posts a warning message but leaves the unauthorized label in place, making the warning pointless.

## Evidence

This issue was observed in: https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/issues/392#issuecomment-3103286448

As noted by @0x4007: "It didn't undo setting the label so this is kind of pointless or a bug"

## Metadata from the incident
- Caller: `onLabelChangeSetPricing`
- User: @whilefoo
- Request ID: 7arbfe0477zr

The bot should either enforce the permission check before allowing the action or implement proper remediation by removing unauthorized labels.
<!-- daemon-merging update Thu Jul 31 2025 01:49:52 GMT+0000 (Coordinated Universal Time) -->
<!-- daemon-merging update Fri Aug 01 2025 01:59:56 GMT+0000 (Coordinated Universal Time) -->

I_kwDOLcmjKM6qBo0D.title: Suggest Time Estimates based on past issue threads
I_kwDOLcmjKM6qBo0D.body:
The current time estimate relies on the user/contributor to suggest accurate time estimates, which are often inflated. 

The pricing strategy should award natural time estimates and penalize inflated ones in the final reward calculation.

One such strategy to calculate would be to fine-tune a LLM with past issue threads and the corresponding time estimates to make time estimate predictions, and use this for calculation. Before we start fine-tuning, we would need a good corpus of issue threads, with at least 250 to 300 clean examples. 

-  #82 

Once this is done, we should be able to build the fine-tuned model.

I_kwDOLcmjKM6Y0b0d.title: Refactor `publicAccessControl`
I_kwDOLcmjKM6Y0b0d.body:
I realize that contributors cant add labels unless there is an issue template that allows them to create an issue and select labels when creating issues, but this is an unusual scenario that projects need to go out of their way for. 

- Remove all references to `publicAccessControl.setLabel`
- Refactor `publicAccessControl` to only be a boolean called: `fundContributorClosedIssue`, default is false.

https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/assistive-pricing/blob/9984131a66eb9167dd0f60147a99e41ae382c43b/src/types/plugin-input.ts#L23-L29

### Note

The reason why `fundContributorClosedIssue` exists is because if: 
1. an outside party files an issue
2. we fund it
3. self assign

Then they can mark the issue closed as completed using the GitHub UI (because they created the issue) which makes an easy attack vector. 
###### Similar [^01^]

## What happened

An error was displayed when only one label was set. I should not be shown, and be a warning most likely. [^03^]

## What was expected

No error should be shown.

## How to reproduce

- create an issue
- add only one label
- observe the error being posted

# Required changes

The plugin should be able to recognize a pattern in the priority labels, and check them accordingly. [^01^] Example:
- "(Low) p0"
- "(Medium) p1"
- "(High) p2"

Could be checked because they all respect the pattern "p{number}". In our organization, they could all be recognized by "Priority: {number}'. [^02^] From there, the order can also be deduced (lowest to highest or opposite).

This in turn would easily allow us to check for pricing attempts with outdated tags, like when we remove priorities for example.

[^01^]: ⚠ 77% possible duplicate - [Label creation should run within the Action not the Worker](https://www.github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-pricing/issues/79#79)

[^02^]: ⚠ 79% possible duplicate - [Updating Priority Levels Removes Pricing From Many Issues](https://www.github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-pricing/issues/64#64)

[^03^]: ⚠ 79% possible duplicate - [Parent Issues Should Allow Labels](https://www.github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-pricing/issues/91#91)

I_kwDOLcmjKM6rjmUq.title: Parent Issues Should Allow Labels
I_kwDOLcmjKM6rjmUq.body:
1. I should be able to set this as `Urgent`. But if I try to set a `Time` label, it should not apply a `Price` label. It shouldn't add `Price` labels but it should allow us to add any labels we want (except for `Price` labels.) 
2. It should throw a `Warning` because `Caution` should be reserved for internal problems that we need to triage. My vision is to automatically file issues when `Caution` is thrown, so any user input error should be `Warning`

> [!CAUTION]
> Pricing is disabled on parent issues.

I_kwDOLcmjKM6jGUGF.title: Updating Priority Levels Removes Pricing From Many Issues
I_kwDOLcmjKM6jGUGF.body: This is bad because I have to go back in and manually re-add all the pricing. Example: - https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/.ubiquity-os/commit/3635c013b31f35975dffa370369de72d52d2da0b - causes https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/issues/100#event-15637253843 This isn't the first time this happened either, with other "normal" priority levels. After a quick look at our https://github.com/orgs/ubiquity/projects/27 I noticed it only seems to do it inside of the @ubiquity-os-marketplace organization. The other two organizations were not affected, even after doing the same config changes. Due to exceeded rate limits, it is possible that this failed halfway through the process. ![telegram-cloud-photo-size-5-6258183812232101774-y](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9b699790-81bd-4a2f-9ade-f36e54fe62db) As of this moment I won't fix the price labels but in case they are left as is, these can be useful to review for reference for not being affected: ###### https://devpool.directory/ubiquity-os-marketplace ###### https://devpool.directory/ubiquity-os-marketplace?proposal=true

I_kwDOLcmjKM6p3m3M.title: Label creation should run within the Action not the Worker
I_kwDOLcmjKM6p3m3M.body: When creating a brand new repo that has no labels set whatsoever, this plugin tries to create the missing labels. [^05^] [^03^] Depending on the configuration, this can mean tens and tens of labels created, which can take quite some time. [^04^] [^02^] [^01^] The problem is that this is being run on the Worker side, which very often (if not always) times out, showing a "this script will never generate a response" in a CF environment. Instead, this should be run on the Action side, the same way the configuration updates runs. [^01^]: ⚠ 69% possible duplicate - [Transform assistive-pricing in a worker instead of a GitHub Action](https://www.github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-pricing/issues/5#5) [^02^]: ⚠ 70% possible duplicate - [Remove Base Rate Logic](https://www.github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-pricing/issues/69#69) [^03^]: ⚠ 70% possible duplicate - [Updating Priority Levels Removes Pricing From Many Issues](https://www.github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-pricing/issues/64#64) [^04^]: ⚠ 71% possible duplicate - [Set labels command](https://www.github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-pricing/issues/51#51) [^05^]: ⚠ 74% possible duplicate - [Init new repo, populate labels](https://www.github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-pricing/issues/45#45)

I_kwDOLcmjKM6olFTd.title: Incentives for lower estimates
I_kwDOLcmjKM6olFTd.body:
This is a draft/discussion and needs more research and ideas. 

As the DAO grows and trust naturally decreases, there should be an incentive for the author and possibly assignee for them to want to make time estimates for tasks as short as realistically possible (or just as close to accurate as possible)

Otherwise the natural incentive is to over estimate all tasks to make pricing larger than it should be. 

Currently the only soft incentive is the financier/admins/managers needing to approve a time estimate before "funding" tasks. 

#### time accuracy 

This is very tricky to do because even if it's a `<4 hour` task, in our system design it's perfectly acceptable to spread that project over a few days. Because of this, we can't simply check the timeline timestamps of when they started and when they pushed their last commit or comment to determine how long it actually took them. Finding accurate time estimates may be better suited for an LLM to be the judge but even still it might be tough to do with accuracy. RAG with all the comments in the repository may provide more clues/hints for accuracy but this still seems shaky. We could do an experiment with estimating time estimates based on RAG context and then we could consider a penalty/bonus depending on how aligned the estimate is for the LLM compared to who set the label?

It could even be revealed only when the project is closed as complete which may incentivize further the label setter to tell the truth. I.e. "accurate label set bonus reward" which can be a gradient based on how close in milliseconds the label value is to the LLM estimate.

I_kwDOLcmjKM6k6w-8.title: Remove Base Rate Logic
I_kwDOLcmjKM6k6w-8.body:
- Change this back to a cloudflare worker 
- Move the logic to change the price labels if base rate is changed to a separate GitHub action. It is the only reason why this is deployed as an action but it's too slow, especially for a feature used multiple times a day.

Code duplication is okay if necessary.

I_kwDOLcmjKM6jEBlX.title: Remove `/allow`
I_kwDOLcmjKM6jEBlX.body:
- remove all code related to `/allow` command 
- always undo `priority` and `price` class label changes (add or remove) from collaborators EXCEPT if they are an admin/billing manager. 
- perhaps label classes should be configurable, with ours being the default, so that projects can design their own label systems. 

This is a capability we need for some new hires, also this should really simplify this codebase. 

Full context: https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/issues/104#issuecomment-2538648933

I_kwDOLcmjKM6WSCps.title: Round decimal and eventually use a Decimal library
I_kwDOLcmjKM6WSCps.body:
When using certain duration settings, the pricing doesn't round up and ends with a lot of decimals. In this case the number should be properly calculated and eventually truncated if needed. This happens for example when setting a time like `Time: <1 Minute`.

_Originally posted by @gentlementlegen in https://github.com/ubiquibot/sandbox/issues/23#issuecomment-2343101400_

I_kwDOLcmjKM6cFHZx.title: Set labels command
I_kwDOLcmjKM6cFHZx.body:
In an effort to reduce the distinction between "contributors" and "collaborators" we should let anybody whitelisted to set labels. 

It may not be user friendly unless we do a mini "search" and select the highest match. For example:

/labels 15 min high priority 

Then we need to intelligently pick the correct labels (or create new ones in the case of the time estimates)  

We also need an admin control (maybe simpler to write in the config yml?) to let specific users set a class of labels: time, priority, price.

```yml
time:
   users:
      - 0x4007
      - whilefoo
priority: 
   users:
      - keyrxng 
price:
   users: 
      - rndquu 
```

This can be really nice with the natural mini LLM command router. Particularly in outside of GitHub contexts like telegram or a google docs comment: 

> @Ubiquityos set to 15 min high priority task 

### v2 vision

The reason why I want to blur the lines between collaborator/contributor is because I think the concept of a "core team" is not truly DAO like. Eventually we should allow for label classes to be editable based on XP. For example:

```yml
time:
   xp: 0
priority: 
   xp: 1000
price:
   xp: 2000
```

And ideally with a time decay element so that you need to be actively contributing to have the privilege to set these. And of course, collaborators would be able to set using the GitHub UI. 
   

> I can't seem to set labels. 15 minutes sounds about right

_Originally posted by @whilefoo in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-pricing/issues/47#issuecomment-2428389010_

I_kwDOLcmjKM6bJoIi.title: Use SDK
I_kwDOLcmjKM6bJoIi.body: Modify this plugin to use the SDK

I_kwDOLcmjKM6WGYzy.title: Create a base reward for users within this plugin
I_kwDOLcmjKM6WGYzy.body:
Yes I do agree that we should be able to chain these results. It's just that this plugin never had the capability to actually attribute rewards to a user, this was the responsibility of `conversation-rewards`. We could think of splitting this and have the logic here later on.

_Originally posted by @gentlementlegen in https://github.com/ubiquibot/assistive-pricing/issues/27#issuecomment-2338184070_

This plugin should output a `Reward` compatible output to be able to be chained with other plugins. The current shape of the rewards is as follows:
https://github.com/ubiquibot/conversation-rewards?tab=readme-ov-file#data-structure

What this plugin should output is the following section:
```json
"userName": {
  "task": {
    "reward": 37.5,
    "multiplier": 1
  }
}
```
which corresponds to the current reward within the label divided by the amount of users assigned to the task. Once in the output, chained plugins could pick this up and use it for further calculations.

I_kwDOLcmjKM6YjE9P.title: Always prefer lower dimension label
I_kwDOLcmjKM6YjE9P.body:
We do this already when rendering the price, but when we have two different time labels (or two different priority labels) the pricing algorithm will always take the lowest combination. 

We should remove the higher labels as well so that there is no confusion.

I_kwDOLcmjKM6bNbF7.title: Non Collaborator Labels
I_kwDOLcmjKM6bNbF7.body:
> @0x4007 Could you make me an owner of https://github.com/ubiquity-os? I want to put time estimates and perform reviews.

I am interested in phasing out the Boolean distinction of "collaborator" and "contributor" in favor of modeling status as a continuum. I think this is more "DAO like" for our general operations

It would be useful to think of ways to support this shift. Like some type of way to vote on a time estimate and have an admin (or billing_manger) approve funding it. 

Maybe a command 

/time value

Also /priority value

And financier gives it a thumbs up for the bot to make it official.  

It would be even cooler for the bot to understand the XP of everybody's vote to get an idea of if some task should be funded even without the admin direct approval but that can be a task for another time after more refinement.

I_kwDOLcmjKM6a522k.title: Init new repo, populate labels
I_kwDOLcmjKM6a522k.body:
I regularly create new repositories and try to price tasks, but we always need to first toggle some random label (the convention we follow is "good first task") and then the plug-in populates the rest. 

The problem with this is that it's not intuitive. We should listen for when a new issue is filed and check if the labels are populated already. 

If they aren't then we should populate. I think that this flow is more intuitive for new partners.  

Or ideally, this plug-in should change the organization repository's default labels so that when a new repository created, they are already pre-populated but I'm not sure if this is straightforward or worth extending our permissions for if we don't already have them.

I_kwDOLcmjKM6Wkf6R.title: Price Label Color Not Correct
I_kwDOLcmjKM6Wkf6R.body:
https://github.com/ubiquibot/assistive-pricing/blob/2fd11e285c61148c01eaa934faf59b247ffe6d34/src/shared/label.ts#L5

I_kwDOLcmjKM6L-b4Z.title: Crash on missing environment variable
I_kwDOLcmjKM6L-b4Z.body:
The check the payload signature, the plugin needs to read `UBIQUIBOT_PUBLIC_KEY` which should be fed through the environment. It currently crashes because the value is not set within the worker.

### Tasks to be carried out
- add the environment variable within the deployment step
- add checks for environment validity
- display errors and return them to the kernel

I_kwDOLcmjKM6JaaUg.title: Transform assistive-pricing in a worker instead of a GitHub Action
I_kwDOLcmjKM6JaaUg.body:
`assistive-pricing` does not run long tasks and requires to be quite reactive as it handles commands from user, that should preferably receive quick feedback. When running through Actions, it takes ~1 minutes per command to be handled, which is not ideal

Thanks to https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquibot-kernel/issues/39 we should be able to run plugins as workers, making them instantaneous.

Thus this plugin should be transformed from Action to Worker inside of Cloudflare.

I_kwDOLcmjKM6C0-oI.title: Generate the Supabase types instead of committing the generated file
I_kwDOLcmjKM6C0-oI.body:
Currently, the TypeScript types that are generated by Supabase are committed in the repository. It might be a better practice to generate them on a build step. This would give some benefits:
- less likely to merge conflict between contributors
- types always up to date on a build, which would fail compilation if they changed

The drawback would be that for contributors who do not have a Service Role Key, they would only be able to generate the schema locally.

The expected fix would be to have a `prebuild` step that generates the types properly.

Refers to https://github.com/ubiquibot/assistive-pricing/pull/1#discussion_r1529509069

I_kwDOMBcers6u_TRR.title: Cold Start Time Optimization
I_kwDOMBcers6u_TRR.body:
### Following GitHub Example

I was checking GitHub's [example of a TypeScript action](https://github.com/actions/typescript-action/blob/main/action.ym) and they don't do any setup before execution. We should do the same.

### Web Assembly

Looking into this further, I realize there are other out-of-pocket optimizations we can incorporate, like Web Assembly. 

Example: https://github.com/0x4007/action-cold-start-optimization/pull/1

I_kwDOMBcers6ioLel.title: Update the template
I_kwDOMBcers6ioLel.body:
This plugin template has become out of date:
- All plugins now use SDK so this template should also use it
- Most if not all plugins use `bun` so this template should also switch to it
- Out of date dependencies
- It's still using old workflows, for example `update-configuration.yml` doesn't use our [reusable workflow](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/action-deploy-plugin) 
- It doesn't use `ncc` to build Action plugins 
- Still using CommonJS modules instead of ESM (also for tests)
- It should use Typescript for eslint, jest and other configs

I'm sure I'm missing some other things but you get the gist, the main task is to update the template so it's more aligned with our current plugin structure

I_kwDOMBcers6mPubS.title: Change Worker deploy to target the proper branch
I_kwDOMBcers6mPubS.body:
The worker is always deployed to the `default` branch regardless of the branch where the build happened.
In https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugin-template/blob/development/.github/workflows/worker-deploy.yml `github.ref` has to be changed to `github.event.workflow_run.head_branch || github.ref` to handle both manual and build triggered deployments.

Related: https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop/issues/124

I_kwDOMBcers6gkVIT.title: Add `url` property to `manifest.json`
I_kwDOMBcers6gkVIT.body:
We need to know an exact plugin URL (in case it works as a cloudflare worker) in order to set it in the partner [onboarding](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-plugin-installer) UI.

What should be done:
1. Add a new optional `url` property to [manifest.json](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugin-template/blob/8d8f06958ca1273f299ae0bc2a51a6c512411af9/manifest.json)
2. Update the [worker-deploy.yml](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugin-template/blob/8d8f06958ca1273f299ae0bc2a51a6c512411af9/.github/workflows/worker-deploy.yml) workflow to set the `url` property in `manifest.json` on each deployment of the `development` branch. I think it's enough for now to use the `development` plugin instances + https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-plugin-installer will simply have to fetch a default plugin's github branch in order to get a plugin URL.
3. Make sure all plugins from https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace have the `url` property set in the `manifest.json` file and updated [worker-deploy.yml](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugin-template/blob/8d8f06958ca1273f299ae0bc2a51a6c512411af9/.github/workflows/worker-deploy.yml) workflow

Original [comment](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-plugin-installer/issues/24#issuecomment-2493899154)

I_kwDOMBcers6Y0JGW.title: Production Builds & Distribution
I_kwDOMBcers6Y0JGW.body:
- Semi related to https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugin-template/issues/24 

I was working on [this pull](https://github.com/m1guelpf/plz-cli/pull/40/files) and appreciate how they automated releases and distributions (based on label) 

We should consider "distributing" as well with a special build script using `ncc` that will compile all the code and used dependencies into a single javascript string. This will make our plugins read for production as these should run almost immediately.  

This essentially adds the `plz-cli` distribution logic on top of #24 when its implemented. 
###### Similar [^01^]

[^01^]: [Optimize Update Manifest Configuration Speed](https://www.github.com/ubiquity-os/plugin-template/issues/25) 89%

I_kwDOMBcers6jAlek.title: Add the CHANGELOG to the ignored prettier files
I_kwDOMBcers6jAlek.body: The changelog is auto-generated and doesn't seem to comply with prettier settings, which always lead to a failed formatting action run. We should either ignore the file, or find a way to run prettier on the file before it is created to avoid having to manually fix it every time a new version is released.

I_kwDOMBcers6bLtIi.title: Update configuration action
I_kwDOMBcers6bLtIi.body:
The `update-configuration.yml` doesn't apply formatting according to the prettier configuration

Also I think it would be better to make this a [custom action](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/sharing-automations/creating-actions/about-custom-actions) which would prevent duplication, we can use Typescript instead of writing code in the YML and allow us to change it without having to apply changes to every repo that forks plugin-template.

The only question is how to specify where the schema is located in the code, it could be something like this:
```yml
- name: update configuration
  uses: ubiquity-os/update-configuration
  with:
    filepath: './src/types'
    typeboxSchemaName: 'pluginSettingsSchema'
```
and then we can dynamically import it!

In the future we could also support different schema libraries (zod, joi...)

@0x4007 rfc

I_kwDOMBcers6UkkVn.title: Make sure to pick up the top level name to generate the worker url
I_kwDOMBcers6UkkVn.body:
Good point, I didn't think of this scenario. I guess we should always base it on the top level `name` to generate the `base_name`. To be sure it's the top level I guess I should read the `.toml` and store the values in an object, which complexifies this script quite a lot. Mind if I take care of it in another pull-request? I think this is quite helpful to get in first for the dev experience and we did not encounter this scenario yet.

_Originally posted by @gentlementlegen in https://github.com/ubiquibot/plugin-template/pull/16#discussion_r1734973213_
            
The `name` is an overridable key within the `wrangler.toml` configuration, meaning the key can be set in different places in the file, for different environments. We rely on that value to set the generated worker name. Currently it is extracted through a `sed` command which becomes unreliable if multiple `name` are present within the file. We should probably extract the whole `toml` content and parse the keys, to only extract the top level value.

I_kwDOMBcers6UtmI6.title: Bot strings language localization
I_kwDOMBcers6UtmI6.body:
Original comment: https://github.com/ubiquibot/command-start-stop/pull/25#discussion_r1736233348

There may be [cases](https://github.com/ubiquibot/command-start-stop/issues/22) when partner would need to customize the plugin's text response to some commands. 

Possible use cases:
- different text messages for different business logic flows
- translation to other languages

It could be useful to keep a separate `json` file of used strings in the plugin repository. This way plugin developers would host `json` file in the plugin's repo while partners would be able to customize the whole "text strings" file without cluttering the config too much.

Example config with a new param `customStringsUrl`:
```
- plugin: http://localhost:4000
  name: start-stop
  id: start-stop-command
  description: "Allows a user to start/stop a task without negative XP impact"
  command: "\/start|\/stop"
  example: "/start" # or "/stop"
  with:
    reviewDelayTolerance: "3 Days"
    taskStaleTimeoutDuration: "30 Days"
    maxConcurrentTasks: 3
    startRequiresWallet: true
    ## ...
    ## ...
    ## NEWLY ADDED CONFIG PARAM
    customStringsUrl: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dev/new-pugin/development/strings.json
```

So as a part of this issue we should add a basic plugin config and an example usage of the `customStringsUrl` param so that future plugin developers could fork this repo and use configurable text messages instead of hardcoding them in the code.

I_kwDOMNa7fM6qAFBo.title: Unexpected issues
I_kwDOMNa7fM6qAFBo.body:
## 1. issue: 

We were trying to set up a wallet for @ubiquity-os-simulant for the demo. [^05^] [^02^] Each time, the response comment indicated that the wallet was set successfully. [^04^] However, when generating permits, it couldn't fetch the wallet:
```
	›› Updating result for user ubiquity-os-simulant
	›› {
	     "caller": "PermitGenerationModule.transform"
	   }
Failed to get wallet {
  userId: 198770750,
  error: {
    code: 'PGRST116',
    details: 'The result contains 0 rows',
    hint: null,
    message: 'JSON object requested, multiple (or no) rows returned'
  }
}
```
I checked the database and confirmed that there is no user record with ID `198770750`, meaning the wallet is not actually being set. I also reviewed the Cloudflare logs for the plugin but found nothing unusual.

## 2. issue:

While coding the demo, I changed my wallet to a random address because permits were not being generated for me (which turned out to be a separate issue with the EVM private key being all zeroes).

When I tried to change my wallet back to the original one, I got an error stating that the wallet was already used by another user. [^01^] I checked the database and found that my wallet was assigned to user ID `124931263` (@devtestubq).

I'm not sure how this happened. [^03^] Either someone manually modified the database and made a mistake, or the plugin did this when I changed my wallet. However, I doubt that someone manually changed the database, as this issue occurred today while coding the demo.

[^01^]: ⚠ 67% possible duplicate - [no docs for populating supabase](https://www.github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-wallet/issues/20#20)

[^02^]: ⚠ 67% possible duplicate - [Use organization package `rpc-handler` to get the fastest provider available](https://www.github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-wallet/issues/3#3)

[^03^]: ⚠ 68% possible duplicate - [Incorrect `homepage_url`](https://www.github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-wallet/issues/38#38)

[^04^]: ⚠ 69% possible duplicate - [Specify EVM](https://www.github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-wallet/issues/33#33)

[^05^]: ⚠ 76% possible duplicate - [December 2024 Fixes](https://www.github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-wallet/issues/28#28)

I_kwDOM2UEbM6-tiMD.title: Remove extra configurations
I_kwDOM2UEbM6-tiMD.body:
*I was not sure which repository to file this in*

We want to simplify the configuration and avoid having to modify them in many places every time. The problem is that currently our configuration CLI tool does not crawl for all the repositories in the organization but only the targeted repo and the parent org.

Known locations for configurations so far (❌ marked ones should get deleted):

I_kwDOM2UEbM7cRAxh.title: Copilot assignment via the Github API
I_kwDOM2UEbM7cRAxh.body:
**Putting myself out of a job here, but here we go 😅**

[As of yesterday](https://github.blog/changelog/2025-12-03-assign-issues-to-copilot-using-the-api/), it’s now possible to assign **Copilot** to issues via the REST and GraphQL APIs.

This opens up some interesting opportunities we could explore:

- A plugin or kernel extension that directly bridges the kernel with Copilot.
- Automated weekly or monthly **refactoring** tasks planned and executed by Copilot — either within a parent issue with a few sub-issues, or as a single “spec → plan → execute” workflow.
- Automated **documentation updates** across all plugins and projects on a set schedule.

If some of these don’t fit as new plugins, they could still be packaged as reusable workflows. I’m sure there are more ideas along these lines, but these are a few clear starting points.

rfc @0x4007

I_kwDOM2UEbM7TATiW.title: Automate beta → prod merge after inactivity timeout
I_kwDOM2UEbM7TATiW.body:
Automate merging of the `development` branch into `main` after a period of inactivity (e.g. 3 months) with no new commits to `development`.

Purpose: ensure tested features in `development` eventually promote to `main`.

Scope:
- A scheduled job (cron under @ubiquity-os) runs daily or weekly.
- Checks if `development` has had no commits for a defined timeout period (default: 3 months).
- If no commits within that period, automatically merges `development` → `main`.
- If new commits are pushed to `development`, reset the inactivity timer.

Remarks:
- Authenticate as our GitHub App.

I_kwDOM2UEbM6h828r.title: Custom Notifications Viewer
I_kwDOM2UEbM6h828r.body:
- I did some research in the past with custom notifications viewer. https://github.com/ubiquity/work.ubq.fi/pull/126
- Original specification here https://github.com/ubiquity/work.ubq.fi/issues/117

Fork work.ubq.fi because it has a lot of good qualities for list viewing, progressive web app features, styling etc. Tear out all the proposal/task related viewing logic, and focus only on notification viewing so that the code is simpler. 

## Custom Notifications Viewer

As activity continues to pick up and my notifications become slower to get through, it would be useful to make a simple ui that allows for more intelligent notification filtering. 

### Prioritization Sorting Algorithm

- We need to detect the priority level of the task the notification is associated with.
   - If the notification is not associated with a task, consider not showing it on this view at all. 
   - If the notification is a pull request, it must be linked to a task with a priority label.
- Reverse chronological order, meaning that neglected/old notifications should appear higher than new ones. 
   - The reason for this is that active conversations will continue to produce more notifications, which can easily cause the reviewer to check it multiple times in a sitting (as I try to get to inbox 0 this happens to me, so I intentionally start from older notifications for this reason.)
- Top of displayed list is highest priority, then work your way down to low priority. 

### Blocking Tasks

If it thinks without my input the task is blocked, it's a higher priority notification.
- Linkbacks: if the notification is associated with a task with many linkbacks then it should be considered higher priority, it usually means its blocking other pulls/tasks

Perhaps we could have an AI layer on top one day to summarize and/or reorder notifications from most important to least.

### Remarks
- Always exclude draft pulls.

## Notification Scoring

We should associate each notification with "scores" so that we can quantify where it would render on the page. 
- Time score: the older it is, the higher its score. We can take the current posix timestamp and subtract the timestamp of the notification to achieve this. 
- Linkback score: we can count how many times it was cross-referenced. The more times it was cross-referenced, the higher it should render on the page. We should prioritize those with higher linkbacks. If two notifications have the same amount of linkbacks i.e. 0, then we sort based on the time score only. 

We need to ensure that priority level always has precedence. For example, `priority 2` associated notifications should always appear higher than `priority 1`.

I_kwDOM2UEbM6s8n2c.title: User XP CLI
I_kwDOM2UEbM6s8n2c.body:
Generated by ChatGPT 


A minimal command-line interface (CLI) to calculate XP earned by users.

Read all earnings from supabase. Sum them up to figure out users XP. 

### Usage

Calculate XP by user:

- **Global XP** (all orgs/repos):
  ```bash
  xp-cli calculate --user=username
  ```

- **Organization-specific XP**:
  ```bash
  xp-cli calculate --user=username --org=organization_name
  ```

- **Repository-specific XP**:
  ```bash
  xp-cli calculate --user=username --org=organization_name --repo=repository_name
  ```

### Output Format
```
User:  username
Global XP: 1234
```

For org-specific or repo-specific calculations, clearly specify the context:
```
User: john_doe
Organization (my-org) XP: 567
```
```
User: john_doe
Repository (my-repo) XP: 89
```

### Optional Caching
- Caching support optional, enabled if performance is slow.

I_kwDOJhaJks7aJ9DK.body: All of the [Matrix Sync jobs](https://github.com/devpool-directory/devpool-directory/actions) are failing due to the following error: ``/home/runner/work/_temp/73a5cb8d-8400-461e-94f6-055506307e4b.sh: line 1: bun: command not found`` --- ```yml - uses: actions/setup-node@v4 with: { node-version: 20 } - name: Install deps run: | bun install ``` The above needs replaced with ```yaml - name: Setup Bun uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2 - name: Install toolchain run: bun install --frozen-lockfile ``` - https://github.com/devpool-directory/devpool-directory/actions/runs/19643200431/workflow#L36

I_kwDOJhaJks7QDWHT.title: Redo Code + Migrate to new organization (@devpool-directory)
I_kwDOJhaJks7QDWHT.body:
I migrated to a new organization in order to avoid problems with our GitHub app being added to every repository, except for one. 

The problem is that some of the code logic required the system to run from the UbiquityOS org, and a lot of little things broke because of this. 

I realized that a lot of the code is quite brittle and the Twitter tweets aren't even working according to the original spec where it was supposed to delete completed tasks from Twitter. 

I figured it would be cleaner to simply rewrite the entire system to make things more robust.

Figure out why and fix.

I_kwDOJhaJks7IQTyz.title: DevPool Directory Matchmaking UI
I_kwDOJhaJks7IQTyz.body:
Instead of the developer browsing and choosing tasks, we should make the primary flow a sorted list based on the match making match. When the developer signs in to the Directory, we immediately begin scraping all of their closed as complete GitHub issues. It would be quite interesting to stream in the list as we generate all the embeddings.

Then the developer can quickly sort to the tasks that are the most relevant to them.

I_kwDOJhaJks6YZskT.title: Improve/Fix Jest Tests
I_kwDOJhaJks6YZskT.body: I broke them and we need to get them fixed. More tests are a plus!

I_kwDOJhaJks6avFnw.title: `Unavailable` label unexpected behavior
I_kwDOJhaJks6avFnw.body:
Turns out some logic was introduced that it no longer would be added to tasks. This breaks a lot of logic related to calculating the statistics of the network, as well as showing what is available in work.ubq.fi

When initializing a new repository, the correct labels must be on the repository before it can be added to any issues, so for developer friendliness, the script should automatically search for the Unavailable label, and create it if it doesn't exist.

I_kwDOJhaJks6Tcrol.title: Close issues from archived repositories
I_kwDOJhaJks6Tcrol.body:
There may be cases when issue exists in an archived repository (i.e. read only) so issues can't be self assigned there although all issue labels would be totally valid.

What should be done:
- if project issue exists in an archived partner's repository then the bot should close such issue

I_kwDOJhaJks6ZT_-5.title: Fork Linkback Fix
I_kwDOJhaJks6ZT_-5.body: > Yes but to clarify this used to work as expected. The problem now is that it seems when the task is assigned and unassigned, it links back even on forks. So the issue is: 1. New devpool issue is created 2. Fork of https://github.com/ubiquity/devpool-directory creates a new issue with `www` prefix (which is expected) in order not to reference (i.e. spam) the original issue ([example](https://github.com/jordan-ae/devpool-directory/issues/1025)) 3. Devpool issue is closed because somebody was assigned 4. Devpool issue is reopened because somebody was unassigned 5. Now the forked issue displays github issue URL without the `www` prefix (while expected behavior to display URL with the `www` prefix) thus spamming original issue ([example](https://github.com/jordan-ae/devpool-directory/issues/867)) --- https://github.com/jordan-ae/devpool-directory/issues/867 When it is reopened, it writes the non `www.` prefixed link to the issue, which "references" the original issue which appears as spam ![github com_ubiquity_ubiquity-dollar_issues_937](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fc1a3abd-c9a7-4588-b966-8e90995875de)

I_kwDOJhaJks6atZzO.title: Too Long of Partner Names Can't Sync
I_kwDOJhaJks6atZzO.body:
If the org/repo name is too long, the issue will not sync.

### Context

Based on the error messages in the log, it appears there are recurring issues with creating new GitHub issues. The main problem seems to be related to label validation. Here's a summary of the types of issues:

### Label Validation Errors:
The most common error is: 
```
"Validation Failed: {"value":"Partner: [repository name]","resource":"Label","field":"name","code":"invalid"}"
```

This occurs for various partner repositories, including:

- ubiquity/uniswap-v3-liquidity-provider-indicator
- ubiquity-os-marketplace/tet-conversation-rewards
- ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-start-stop
- ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-disqualifier

### Summary

The root cause appears to be an issue with the label names being used when creating new issues. The "Partner: [repository-name]" label format is being rejected by the GitHub API as invalid. This could be due to length restrictions, character limitations, or other label naming constraints imposed by GitHub.

# Task

I think we can safely delete this Partner label because it doesn't seem to be read anywhere.

I_kwDOJhaJks6avGEN.title: `__STORAGE__` branch
I_kwDOJhaJks6avGEN.body: To reduce merge conflicts, store all rendered artifacts (like the statistics) on `__STORAGE__` branch. I did [some research](https://github.com/0x4007/devpool-directory/tree/__STORAGE__) on this and it works as expected.

I_kwDOJhaJks6TbHKq.title: Delete issues created by contributors

There is also the [limit interaction](https://docs.github.com/en/communities/moderating-comments-and-conversations/limiting-interactions-in-your-repository#about-temporary-interaction-limits) option but if we enable it then new contributors won't be able to open PRs towards https://github.com/ubiquity/devpool-directory

I_kwDOJhaJks6CH8fq.title: More Robust Server Error Handling
I_kwDOJhaJks6CH8fq.body:
I was checking the entire flow of how new people perceive our site, and our developer onboarding flow through dao.ubq.fi/devpool etc as I do regularly these days (to ensure seamless onboarding!) and realized that work.ubq.fi only rendered nine tasks. 
![work ubq fi_ (1)](https://github.com/ubiquity/devpool-directory/assets/4975670/64e027ac-21eb-427f-9da7-0536bd3c0998)

I was quick to assume I got rate limited but realized that there indeed are only nine tasks open in the devpool-directory "backend"

Fortunately GitHub Actions has logs and I was able to discover

> [Getting ubiquity org repositories failed: HttpError: Server Error](https://github.com/ubiquity/devpool-directory/actions/runs/8259431257/job/22593325855#step:7:12)

This is a problem because it actually closes all the issues when this occurs! 

The script should be smart enough to ignore the @ubiquity org tasks in this situation in case there are 5xx level errors!

I_kwDOJhaJks5v6yCx.title: Hide unavailable bounties
I_kwDOJhaJks5v6yCx.body:
Check the [list](https://github.com/ubiquity/devpool-directory/issues) of all available partners' bounties.

When a bounty (i.e. issue) has the `Unavailable` label it means that the bounty is already assigned to some bounty hunter. We should hide such bounties from the [list](https://github.com/ubiquity/devpool-directory/issues).

What should be done:
- if bounty is unavailable (i.e. assigned to some bounty hunter) and does not exist in the list (i.e. first sync) then don't sync such bounty
- if bounty is unavailable (i.e. assigned to some bounty hunter) and exists in the list with the state `open` then close such bounty
- if bounty is available (i.e. not assigned to some bounty hunter) and exists in the list with the state `closed` then reopen such bounty

I_kwDOJhaJks5_SUDP.title: Forks should not tag and link back to UBQ issues
I_kwDOJhaJks5_SUDP.body:
It's probably a good idea that forks of the dev-pool not link back to UBQ issues so that it prevents issues being spammed with comments like:

``@[korrrba-bot](https://github.com/apps/korrrba-bot) korrrba-bot bot mentioned this issue [on Nov 6, 2023](https://github.com/ubiquity/audit.ubq.fi/issues/4)
https://github.com/korrrba/devpool-directory/issues/16``

The example shows three links and I could fetch more examples but you get the gist. I was scanning to see what other bounties I'd be able to take on and while atm it's not so bad you can see how it might snowball as the hunter base grows and may potentially confuse first timers but it would keep things cleaner regardless.

I'm not 100% on how to implement this exactly with all the moving parts at the minute so rfc from core team

### Objective

- Prefix any GitHub links with `www.` so that the GitHub UI does not "link back"
- Refer to https://github.com/ubiquity/devpool-directory-bounties/issues/15#issuecomment-1945733522 for more information

I_kwDOJhaJks5mIe7L.title: Opt Out & Opt In
I_kwDOJhaJks5mIe7L.body:
Enable support for both "opting in" and "opting out" model on the repository-level. 
- The main issue I was inspired by right now is that I would like to be able to quickly create new repos and issues in old repos under @ubiquity without having to manually add each repo here every time. 
- I would like for us to be able to add approved partners, like us (@ubiquity) and automatically import all of their public repositories. 

`opt.json`

```json
{
    "out": [
        "ubiquity/devpool-directory",
        "ubiquity/.github"
    ],
    "in": [
        "alpha-labs-global/blockalizer",
        "uniswap/interface"
    ]
}
```

- Most partners will use `opt.in` to opt in specific repositories. 
- Special partners, including @Ubiquity will automatically sync all of their issues to the directory, and will be using `opt.out` to exclude repositories.

---

### Notice

- It is implied that we automatically import all of the repositories from the `ubiquity` organization. 
- "`ubiquity/`" can be parsed within `opt.out` as an organization name.

I_kwDOJhaJks5llHJO.title: Add project category label
I_kwDOJhaJks5llHJO.body:
What should be done: 1. Partner should be able to add a `category` field to his project [here](https://github.com/ubiquity/devpool-directory/blob/development/projects.json) 2. If a new issue is created from a partner's repository then if the `category` field exists then it should be added as a label to the issue Don't forget to handle edge cases: 1. If partner already has "synced" issues in the https://github.com/ubiquity/devpool-directory/issues those issues should also be labeled with the `category` field. 2. If the `category` field is updated or deleted the partner's issues "category" label should also be updated 3. The `category` field can be empty (not set) ### Discussed in https://github.com/ubiquity/devpool-directory/discussions/476
Originally posted by **pavlovcik** May 1, 2023 We started experimenting with using the DevPool system for roles beyond software development. - https://github.com/ubiquity/grants is a good example of our initiative for grant writing. - We also have a miscellaneous tasks repo at https://github.com/ubiquity/.github Perhaps in the pull request to monitor the repository, we should add a property, e.g. "category" that will automatically tag those issues with a certain customizable label. For every repository added so far, the category can be "Development". Perhaps we could also populate all of the extra labels for extra metadata as we sometimes label bounties as "CI/CD" or "Solidity" or "UI/UX" --- Ultimately we want to allow non developers to contribute to DAOs and I think we might be able to get it to work with this current infrastructure.

This is especially useful for external contributors to create proposals, and allow us core contributors to approve of them as bounties before they are published to the directory.

I_kwDOJhaJks5nWXB7.title: Display Currently Available Bounties
I_kwDOJhaJks5nWXB7.body:
We should have a filter that indicates the current assignment status of a bounty. This way, bounty hunters can filter the view for only available bounties. It could be annoying and tedious if all the bounties they are interested in handling are taken. It takes a few clicks right now to view the status of the bounty.

Proposed solutions:

1. Directly assign the assignee in the `devpool-directory` mirror of the issue. This may lead to unnecessary notifications (or not!) but it will be the most direct way to express that the bounty is taken.
2. Add a label on bounties that are currently assigned e.g. `Unavailable`

Both solutions will allow bounty hunters to filter for only available bounties.

Another problem is that errors get printed as `{}` so every time I have to manually add a `console.log` to know what the actual error is.


I_kwDOK3SaHs6qltm8.title: Display `toString` properly
I_kwDOK3SaHs6qltm8.body:
The problem with aggregate error is that it accepts pretty much anything, so if you push an array of logger errors then it looks like this:

https://github.com/Meniole/plugin-template/issues/1#issuecomment-2657899904

Corresponding code:
```ts
  throw new AggregateError([new Error("error 1"), new Error("error 2"), logger.error("logger error"), new RangeError("range error")]);
```

But I think it would be the `logger` responsibility to properly handle `toString()` method so it doesn't display this way.

_Originally posted by @gentlementlegen in https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugin-sdk/issues/69#issuecomment-2657902847_
            



The logger should properly handle `toString` so it displays the error instead of `[object Object]` when printed.
