I_kwDOK3SaHs6i0Gab.title: Logger Comment Formatting
I_kwDOK3SaHs6i0Gab.body:
Change all logger comments to "new format" which looks more aesthetically pleasing. 

## Old Format

We should color code essentially the equivalent of HTTP 1xx 2xx 3xx 4xx 5xx class errors in these bot comments. 

```diff
# 1xx Class (Informational Responses)
```

```diff
+ 2xx Class (Successful Responses)
```

```diff
@@ 3xx Class (Redirection Messages) @@
```

```diff
! 4xx Class (Client Error Responses)
```

```diff
- 5xx Class (Server Error Responses)
```

## New Format

As it turns out, GitHub flavored markdown includes these handy color indicators. We can use the same color keys but instead of diff syntax, we use this syntax. 

This should be applied across all kernel and plugin messages.

It might even be handy to rename the methods in our logger if its still attached to posting comments. The inspiration behind the rename is so that the class of message is no longer subjective.

```typescript
logger.info();
logger.success();
logger.redirect();
logger.clientError();
logger.serverError();
```

### Status Codes

Based on HTTP status codes, we should color code the responses. The only problem is that some of the headers can be misleading, but it does look a lot more aesthetically pleasing than the diffs. The left border in particular I think is a great minimal representation of the status. 

> [!NOTE]
> 1xx

> [!TIP]
> 2xx

> [!IMPORTANT]
> 3xx

> [!WARNING]
> 4xx

> [!CAUTION]
> 5xx

### Source Code

```markdown
> [!NOTE]
> 1xx

> [!TIP]
> 2xx

> [!IMPORTANT]
> 3xx

> [!WARNING]
> 4xx

> [!CAUTION]
> 5xx
```

### About HTTP Status Code Classes

> HTTP status codes are standardized codes returned by web servers to indicate the result of a client's request. These codes are grouped into five classes, each signifying a different category of response:
>
> ---
> 
> ### **1xx: Informational**
> 
> **Description:** The 1xx class of status codes indicates that the server has received the request and is continuing the process. These are provisional responses, providing interim information while the server continues to process the request.
> 
> **Common Status Codes:**
> 
> - **100 Continue:** Indicates that the initial part of the request has been received and the client should continue with the rest of the request.
> - **101 Switching Protocols:** Informs the client that the server is switching to a different protocol as requested.
> - **102 Processing (WebDAV):** Signals that the server has received and is processing the request, but no response is available yet.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### **2xx: Success**
> 
> **Description:** The 2xx class signifies that the client's request was successfully received, understood, and accepted by the server.
> 
> **Common Status Codes:**
> 
> - **200 OK:** The request has succeeded. The meaning varies depending on the HTTP method used.
> - **201 Created:** The request has been fulfilled, resulting in the creation of a new resource.
> - **202 Accepted:** The request has been accepted for processing, but the processing is not complete.
> - **204 No Content:** The server successfully processed the request and is not returning any content.
> - **206 Partial Content:** The server is delivering only part of the resource due to a range header sent by the client.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### **3xx: Redirection**
> 
> **Description:** The 3xx class indicates that further action is needed to complete the request. This usually means a redirection to a different resource.
> 
> **Common Status Codes:**
> 
> - **301 Moved Permanently:** The resource has been permanently moved to a new URL.
> - **302 Found:** The resource resides temporarily under a different URL.
> - **303 See Other:** The response can be found under a different URI and should be retrieved using a GET method.
> - **304 Not Modified:** Indicates that the resource has not been modified since the last request.
> - **307 Temporary Redirect:** The request should be repeated with another URI; however, future requests should still use the original URI.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### **4xx: Client Error**
> 
> **Description:** The 4xx class signifies errors that appear to have been caused by the client. These codes indicate that the request contains bad syntax or cannot be fulfilled.
> 
> **Common Status Codes:**
> 
> - **400 Bad Request:** The server cannot process the request due to a client error (e.g., malformed request syntax).
> - **401 Unauthorized:** Authentication is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.
> - **403 Forbidden:** The client does not have access rights to the content.
> - **404 Not Found:** The server cannot find the requested resource.
> - **405 Method Not Allowed:** The request method is known by the server but is not supported by the target resource.
> - **408 Request Timeout:** The server timed out waiting for the request.
> - **409 Conflict:** The request could not be completed due to a conflict with the current state of the resource.
> - **410 Gone:** The resource requested is no longer available and will not be available again.
> - **429 Too Many Requests:** The user has sent too many requests in a given amount of time ("rate limiting").
> 
> ---
> 
> ### **5xx: Server Error**
> 
> **Description:** The 5xx class indicates that the server failed to fulfill a valid request. The server is aware that it has encountered an error or is otherwise incapable of performing the request.
> 
> **Common Status Codes:**
> 
> - **500 Internal Server Error:** A generic error message when the server encounters an unexpected condition.
> - **501 Not Implemented:** The server either does not recognize the request method or lacks the ability to fulfill it.
> - **502 Bad Gateway:** The server was acting as a gateway or proxy and received an invalid response from the upstream server.
> - **503 Service Unavailable:** The server is not ready to handle the request, often due to maintenance or overload.
> - **504 Gateway Timeout:** The server was acting as a gateway or proxy and did not receive a timely response from the upstream server.
> - **505 HTTP Version Not Supported:** The server does not support the HTTP protocol version used in the request.
> 
> ---
> 
> Understanding these status codes is essential for debugging web applications and ensuring efficient client-server communication. Each code provides specific information about what happened with a request, allowing developers to handle responses appropriately.

I_kwDOK3SaHs6OVj6k.title: Compatibility for CommonJs and ESM
I_kwDOK3SaHs6OVj6k.body:
The package is currently only compiled for ESM projects, which implies adding the `"type": "module"`. This usually breaks projects that are using older techs requiring `"type": "commonjs"`, which is broadly used within our organization and the packages we include. It would be better to offer both solutions, as this package doesn't require to be in ESM to work properly.

Tasks to be carried out:
- bundle for both ESM and CommonJs
- pack them properly with input entries
- make sure it works with `module` and `commonjs`

I_kwDOMDzQsM7c6Hhk.title: slash command: `/deadline`
I_kwDOMDzQsM7c6Hhk.body:
It should be easier and clearer for contributors of all types to be able to see current state of this plugin within the context of any pull request. This can be done simply by building a `/deadline` slash command which details all of the active timers, durations, counts, etc.

- we should ignore the use of this command as activity
- it should be completely transparent in showing all of the active timers
- it should also show how much XP the contributor is about to lose if they are disqualified (I assume it scales with time and/or priority)
- it should work in both the issue and pull request context
- if used on the issue, it should detail only the assignee's pr details. This allows for situations where there might be two active PRs against an issue and the command is used within the PR that does not belong to the assignee. Times where tasks are taken over by staff or contributors that start a new PR and there is delays between the first contributor being unassigned etc.


_Original Context: https://github.com/ubiquity/arbitrage-bot/issues/3#issuecomment-3626571975_
<!-- @ubiquity-os/text-vector-embeddings update 2025-12-08T13:35:51.306Z -->

I_kwDOMDzQsM7ZFg3l.title: Bot doesn't warn as it should
I_kwDOMDzQsM7ZFg3l.body:
- https://github.com/ubiquity-os/.github/issues/11#issuecomment-3550307985

I'm not sure if this is intended behaviour or not:

The bot seemed to jump straight to `3/3` extensions used but hasn't actually warned me that I've used any until now. I've had a look for other warning comments elsewhere and cannot find any in these three spots. 

- https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-merging/pull/60
- https://github.com/ubiquity-os/.github/issues/11
- https://github.com/ubq-testing/auto-merge-dev-branch/pull/2

I wondered if it has tried to comment on my `ubq-testing` PR and has failed, but I doubt that would ever be the intent.

I_kwDOMDzQsM7HpAwF.title: Handle `issue.reopened` event
I_kwDOMDzQsM7HpAwF.body:
Currently, when an issue gets closed, the issue to watch is removed from the list. But it is possible that the issue gets reopened, either manually or because other pull-requests were linked to that issue and the bot re-opened it because the task is not finished, like [here](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugin-sdk/issues/107#issuecomment-3216536437) for example.

The item should be added back on `issue.reopened` event.
<!-- @ubiquity-os/text-vector-embeddings update 2025-08-24T12:38:35.303Z -->

I_kwDOMDzQsM69GvgU.title: Fix Invalid Date UTC on the reminder content
I_kwDOMDzQsM69GvgU.body: @gentlementlegen, you have used **1** of **6** available deadline extensions. Please provide an update on your progress. The new deadline is on **Invalid Date UTC**. _Originally posted by @ubiquity-os-beta in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-conversation-rewards/pull/361#issuecomment-2982360310_ The output should have been a correct date.

I_kwDOMDzQsM7FEfts.title: Migrate from `db.json` to KV storage
I_kwDOMDzQsM7FEfts.body:
Like we previously did for https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-merging/issues/51 we would benefit from moving to storage from `db.json` to Deno KV, which would make CRON updates from this plugin much smoother.
<!-- @ubiquity-os/text-vector-embeddings update 2025-08-09T13:34:24.847Z -->

I_kwDOMDzQsM6sk8mM.title: Write Deadline Extension New Date
I_kwDOMDzQsM6sk8mM.body: We should state the new deadline for clarity. Try something like: @user, you have used **3** of **3** available deadline extensions. Please provide an update on your progress. The new deadline is on 6 March 16:08 UTC. > @user, you have used **3** of **3** available deadline extensions. Please provide an update on your progress. _Originally posted by @ubiquity-os-beta[bot] in https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-ask/issues/70#issuecomment-2693229538_

I_kwDOMDzQsM6wcRWo.title: Fix unexpected disqualifications
I_kwDOMDzQsM6wcRWo.body:
There have been a couple of instances where the assignee was unassigned due to supposedly using all available deadline extensions but should not have been.

First case is where PR was approved but the assignee was unassigned anyway
Examples: 
https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-pull-review/issues/23#issuecomment-2739822030
https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-pricing/issues/64#issuecomment-2707859584
The problem in the code is that the PR is checked for approvals only if the reminder has been sent, it should be checked even if there weren't any reminders.

Second case is where the assignee got unassigned but no reminders have been sent.
I can't find any examples but I'm pretty sure there was such a case.
The solution is to first send a reminder and then disqualify the user.

I_kwDOMDzQsM6gitED.title: Time "Top Up" Limits
I_kwDOMDzQsM6gitED.body:
A quick idea that can use improvement:

Assignee can restart the disqualification timer by continuing to try. There's many clear examples of the task not being the right fit for the assignee and they try i.e. for months on projects that should be doable in a day.

We could consider doing limits on time limit "top ups." So for example:
```
Priority 1 - 5 top ups
Priority 2 - 4 top ups 
Priority 3 - 3 top ups 
Priority 4 - 2 top ups 
Priority 5 - 1 top up. 
```

I suspect that priority 5 tasks will border on eliminating most even capable assignees but once the task is complete even if just slightly after deadline we can always retroactively assign credit. 


---

- We should be able to toggle this behavior in the config. 
- If it is enabled the message should clearly explain the amount of remaining top ups. 

I foresee a bit of complexity around the logic related to dealing with the "time limits" for follow up/disqualify and the top up counter. 
- If the limits are 7/14 as they are now, then the first top up should occur after 14 days on a priority 1 normal project. 
- priority 5 top up would be after `14 / 5 = 2.8` days.
- Check the assignment timestamp and use that as a point of reference to calculate the current top up counter/state. i.e. if the task was assigned 35 days ago, and the clock speed is 14 days, then `35/14=2.5` meaning we are still at top up number 2. 

## Example Context
- https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugins-wishlist/issues/46#issuecomment-2450630585
- Assigned 1 November, but this should have been disqualified by 10 November. 

Logic: 
```
14 days base timer / priority 4 = 3.5 days. 
this means that top up counter increments every 3.5 days. 
priority 4 has 2 additional top ups. 

1 November is the original granted 3.5 day time limit. 
after 3.5 days pass, the first top up is spent. 
after another 3.5 days pass, the second top up is spent. 

3.5 + 3.5 + 3.5 = 10.5 days has elapsed 

on the next attempt to follow up disqualification occurs because no more top ups remain. 
```

Remarks:

I wonder if the original time that we grant should be based on the `Time: ` label because I see problems with tasks that are set to `Time: <1 Week` and `Priority: 5 (Emergency)` they would only be able to get 5.6 days to work on it.

The disqualifier should not run on task that are not funded (e.g. have no price label).
            
## What happened

The `daemon-disqualifier` ran and sent a message on an issue that was not priced, thus not subject to disqualification (no deadline needed).

## What was expected

`daemon-disqualifier` should run on priced tasks, and ignored unpriced tasks.

## How to reproduce

- create a task without any price label
- assign a user to that task
- notice the notification message being sent by `daemon-disqualifier`

I_kwDOMDzQsM6lWLMZ.title: Do not blame user with a reminder if the review is taking too long
I_kwDOMDzQsM6lWLMZ.body:
In relation to https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-disqualifier/issues/51 I think maybe the reminder system should be changed a bit when the review is taking too long, so long that a reminder is sent to the user.

I think in this scenario, the user should not be blamed because reviewers are too slow. Maybe it would make more sense to remind the reviewers instead, which improves two points:

- the author of the PR does not have to manually remind the reviewers assigned;
- the author of the PR does not get blamed for deadlines being overshot

I thought of this since we mentioned having a quota (or top up) of reminders before definitive disqualification.

I_kwDOMDzQsM6fupAC.title: Reviewer Follow Ups
I_kwDOMDzQsM6fupAC.body:
Sometimes reviewers are slow and the follow ups will keep pinging the assignee even though they are finished with their work. 

This will require us to change our standard operating procedure to convert back to draft pull request if we think the pull is not ready to go. This I am hesitant for because if it's a private repo then they must pay for GitHub premium to support draft pulls. Perhaps we can incorporate some logic for when we request changes, then we know the pull is not ready. We can discuss. 

--

If the pull is ready then it should follow up with reviewers. If it isn’t ready (draft) then it should follow up with the assignee(s).  

If the contributor stops working on the task they will be reminded. Either they should unassign themselves, continue working, or convert the draft to a finalized pull. 

https://t.me/UbiquityDevPool/2960

I_kwDOMDzQsM6YFm1F.title: Priority Speed
I_kwDOMDzQsM6YFm1F.body:
Divide the config timeouts by the priority level. This makes follow ups more aggressive based on the priority level. 

`Priority: 5 (Emergency)` Means divide by 5. 

- warning = 3.5 days / 5 = 16.8 hours
-  disqualify = 7 days / 5 = 33.6 hours


Should be configurable (priority enabled: Boolean)

I_kwDOMDzQsM6L0hv3.title: Flexible timing
I_kwDOMDzQsM6L0hv3.body:
Depends on https://github.com/ubiquibot/user-activity-watcher/pull/1

Right now this plugin supports only thresholds in minutes:
```
- plugin: ubiquibot/user-activity-watcher
  type: github
  with:
    unassignUserThreshold: 7
    sendRemindersThreshold: 3.5
```

It would be useful to support custom string thresholds like: `1 minute`, `2 hours`, `3 days` etc... which could be converted to seconds under the hood.

Example:
```
- plugin: ubiquibot/user-activity-watcher
  type: github
  with:
    unassignUserThreshold: "1 week"
    sendRemindersThreshold: "3 days"
```

What should be done:
- take any battled tested "string to seconds" library ([example](https://www.npmjs.com/package/ms)) and add a feature of supporting string thresholds

I_kwDOOXbSr863Eqzf.title: Repo activity insights
I_kwDOOXbSr863Eqzf.body:
The goal is to provide simple, useful statistics such as:

- **Heat maps of activity** (by day/time)
- **Trends in contribution patterns**

### Possible Features

- Adjustable time duration (e.g. past week, month, year)
- GitHub-style contribution grid (colored by activity level)
- Per-repository and organization-wide views
- Exportable stats and visualizations
- Time-of-day and day-of-week breakdowns
- Weekly/monthly trend summaries

This will help teams understand when contributors are most active and adjust workflows for better efficiency (for example our reminders)

I_kwDOOXbSr86yNLrB.title: XP MVP - Blockers
I_kwDOOXbSr86yNLrB.body:
## Blockers
- **Timeline and analytics views are currently blocked.**
- The root cause is that artifact data does not include enriched XP event metadata (timestamp, node_id, and precise URL for each XP event).
- The current artifact only points to the parent issue, not the specific comment/PR/etc.
- Fetching this data at runtime is not feasible due to the volume of fetches and the IndexedDB-backed instant analytics requirement.
- The required solution is to enrich artifact data at build time (using the GitHub API) to include all necessary metadata for each XP event.

I_kwDOOXbSr86yNLjF.title: XP MVP
I_kwDOOXbSr86yNLjF.body:
## Known Issues
- Timeline and analytics views are blocked until artifact data includes enriched XP event metadata (timestamp, node_id, precise URL). Runtime fetches are not feasible due to performance and architectural constraints.
- Large artifacts may cause memory pressure during unzipping.
- Error handling during unzip operations needs improvement.
- Limited feedback during long-running operations.
- Browser memory constraints may affect very large artifacts.

I_kwDOIaGAy86szXgq.title: Unified Authentication
I_kwDOIaGAy86szXgq.body:
As we begin to integrate UbiquityOS across various platforms, we need to associate one's GitHub identity with all their other user accounts.

1. Fork demo.ubq.fi
2. Use Supabase to support auth across the following[^1^]:
   - GitHub (inherited from fork)
   - Google Drive
   - Telegram

The vision is that we can monitor events and know who invoked them if we save this identity information.

[^1^]: If Supabase doesn't support, we can break off into a separate task.

I_kwDOIaGAy86sv6NA.title: New Proposal Router
I_kwDOIaGAy86sv6NA.body:
Using vector embeddings(?) we should make a simple UI that will allow us to create any new proposal and it will automatically route it to the correct repository. 

I envision something similar to the new issue ui: https://github.com/ubiquity/.github/issues/new

But the key difference is that we will have a dropdown of where to post the issue to across all of our orgs. It will intelligently hide and show what it thinks is relevant as the user starts typing out the specification, live. 

I imagine with sufficient enough unique detail, we can turn that massive list into something as short as a few target repository options. [^01^] 

Given that we already have issue dedupe tech, I think we can achieve this pretty easily. But it isn't clear to me how to leverage that tech for this solution. This same backend will be immensely useful for the Telegram interface. You can, at any time (even in a dm) write something like: 

```
@ubiquityos we need to fix the cash out interface on pay.ubq.fi because its currently not vertically center aligned. 
```

From there we can preprocess it, and automatically file the proposal...then of course, later it can automatically provide the time/priority/price estimates and distribute it to the DevPool network for somebody to start working. 

[^01^]: ⚠ 78% possible duplicate - [Add support for proposals on `work.ubq.fi` ](https://www.github.com/ubiquity/.github/issues/115#115)

I_kwDOIaGAy86QXK0y.title: Add support for proposals on `work.ubq.fi`
I_kwDOIaGAy86QXK0y.body:
Related https://github.com/ubiquity/work.ubq.fi/issues/54

There's a lot of unaccounted for/half completed proposals and it will be best to have a dedicated view to sort and browse. 

I recreated the task here because this involves work across several repositories. 

- The first thing that needs to be addressed is to upgrade the [backend](https://github.com/ubiquity/devpool-directory/issues). Perhaps we can make a new repository with a better name vs `devpool-directory` although it isn't clear to me what the name should be yet. I_kwDOIaGAy86RCx5m.title: DoraHacks Bounty Post
I_kwDOIaGAy86RCx5m.body:
https://dorahacks.io/daobounty
https://dorahacks.io/blog/guides/publish-a-bounty/

We are going to create a `daobounty` to post on `DoraHacks`. Essentially this will act as advertising space to a user base of 156K+ web3 centric users.

DoraHacks requires that you put up the bounty value via a transfer of the amount into an escrow pool. Since we handle payments we have a two options:

1. Ignore this completely and treat the value as an advertising fee for the space.
2. Create an actual reward pool and payout rules such as - $1,000 spread between 10 winners over the space of three months and anyone that successfully gets a PR merged in qualifies for an additional $100 on top of their task reward (handled manually).

I think two is the better option. It sticks to the rules of the platform and has legitimate payout and submission info. It further increases the incentive for newcomers and would be a healthy way to determine how successful this has been.

typically bounties are posted in a format such as:

#### intro

##### Task 1: Build a plugin

##### Task 2: Build devtools

##### Task 3: Wildcard

#### Submission Rules

---

Gnosis is not a supported network although BNB, MATIC, and a few others are but this is trivial.

With any decent traction from this it makes the xp-system evermore relevant

I_kwDOIaGAy86AEEJh.title: Sync ERC20 Permit and ERC721 Types System Wide
I_kwDOIaGAy86AEEJh.body:
This is a large project because it requires two tasks:

1. Designing a robust and sensible schema for all permits in our system.
2. Updating the code across all of our repositories. This will require updating the database to accomodate the updated schema as well. 

This is especially relevant in the: 
1. pay.ubq.fi interface
2. the UbiquiBot permit generation code
3. audits app
4. nft-rewards repository

> > I'm having type issues due to trying to process ERC20 and ERC721 permits in the UI code (the schemas don't line up at all.)

> We should try and sync the properties as much as possible. I'm working on that now on my branch. I hope that it won't cause issues but I'm looking for input on constraints for the property names. 

> From what I understand if we change the property names then the old permits will be incompatible.

> @whilefoo why not just copy the schema as much as possible of ERC20 permits to the ERC721 permits?

_Originally posted by @pavlovcik in https://github.com/ubiquity/pay.ubq.fi/pull/146#discussion_r1498792922_

I_kwDOIaGAy85-pwlW.title: Command-Line Tool Totaling Contributor Rewards for UBQ Airdrop
I_kwDOIaGAy85-pwlW.body:
## Overview

We are looking to develop a command-line tool that automates the tallying of contributions across all Ubiquity repositories for the last year. This tool will play a crucial role in recognizing and rewarding our contributors' efforts through UBQ token payouts.

## Objectives

### Tally Contributions

The tool should accurately parse through all the "permits" (or payments for recognized contributions) generated across our repositories to identify and sum up each contributor's total contributions in terms of dollars earned.

### UBQ Token Calculation

Based on the dollar value of each contributor's total contributions, calculate the equivalent amount in UBQ tokens, with the conversion rate being 1 UBQ per dollar earned.

### Output Format

The results should be compiled into a comprehensive CSV file for each repository, detailing the contributors' names, total dollar earnings, and corresponding UBQ tokens. This CSV file will serve as the basis for UBQ token payouts.

### Automation and Execution
This script should be designed for easy execution at specific times, particularly when enabling UBQ token payouts to ensure a seamless distribution process.

## Requirements
- Use TypeScript for development to ensure type safety and maintainability.
- Utilize the @octokit/rest library for GitHub API interactions to fetch permits across repositories.
- Ensure the tool is executable through `tsx` to facilitate integration into our existing workflow.
- Include a testing mechanism that prints test results, which will be a critical part of the pull request QA process.

## Final Deliverables
- A fully functional command-line tool meeting the above specifications.
- Documentation on how to set up, use, and contribute to the tool.
- Test cases and results demonstrating the tool's effectiveness and accuracy.
- This tool is integral to our efforts to automate and streamline our reward system for contributors. It will not only enhance our operational efficiency but also ensure transparency and fairness in UBQ token distribution.

We look forward to seeing innovative solutions and contributions from the community to bring this project to fruition.

I_kwDOPH7VPM6_q8UE.title: Create an Eslint rule and use AST
I_kwDOPH7VPM6_q8UE.body:
There are many more bugs than that. As seen in the tests, strings into strings do not work, I've seen cases with environment variables on top level considered as strings etc...

As @whilefoo pointed out this would be better as an eslint rule. And I think for parsing this, AST would be much more reliable and would not give false positives.

I left the script as is because I don't think this is part of the issue I am solving (removing Octokit rest package from the SDK).

I can make a task to rewrite this properly

_Originally posted by @gentlementlegen in https://github.com/ubiquity-os/empty-string-checker/pull/1#discussion_r2194501849_
            

This script is flawed and not very convenient to use. Two main changes should be made:

- change this to be an eslint rule so it can be easily enabled / disabled and run on specific files
- parse it with proper techniques like AST to extract string elements properly

I_kwDOK6Xdq87eJUqU.body:

- Fix org/repo config merge behavior.

I_kwDOK6Xdq87ee0v-.title: Allow `html_homepage` overrides for local testing
I_kwDOK6Xdq87ee0v-.body:
Since the changes for the plugin configuration have been implemented, it became complex to setup local calls since we cannot use an html identifier with the configuration anymore. The most straightforward approach would be to allow overriding the `html_homepage` through the configuration so we can override that url.

For security purposes, we should allow that override only on local environments, otherwise that would allow users to shadow the real plugin and intercept the plugin calls, redirecting them to another url.

## What is to be done
- implement an override option for `homepage_url` within the manifest
- make sure it is only allowed on local environments
- eventually have a test to make sure the override works fine

I_kwDOK6Xdq86YbhW8.title: KV Limits
I_kwDOK6Xdq86YbhW8.body: - We hit 90% daily quota today. Given that we don't even have any partners using the system yet, this is looking grim. - I hope that we can find a way to optimize our KV usage (or we can figure some cheaper alternative, but I am skeptical.) - - ### Projected Costs - The good news is that our limits go from 1000 -> 33,333, so we get essentially 33x capacity for $5/month. - I estimate that once we get most of the planned plugins up and running, we'll be closer to 5k a day. - `6,666.6666666667` per $1 of cost - I think each large partner will cost us approximately $1 a month on KV - Smaller ones will probably be closer to the 1k we are using now. ### Next Steps Let's discuss how we can optimize the KV usage of the kernel.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86jvDdB.title: Add support for calling worker plugins from URL set in the manifest
I_kwDOK6Xdq86jvDdB.body:
Depends on https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugin-template/issues/33

[This](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugin-template/issues/33) task introduces a new property `homepage_url` (which is a plugin worker URL) in the [manifest.json](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugin-template/blob/8d8f06958ca1273f299ae0bc2a51a6c512411af9/manifest.json) file.  

The idea is that now the bot's config will contain only `org/repo` in the plugin's config. For example [here](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/.ubiquity-os/blob/67df7f5643edcaa3899304481f7ff4ef74a1041a/.github/.ubiquity-os.config.dev.yml#L3) instead of
```
- plugin: https://ubiquity-os-comment-vector-embeddings-development.ubiquity.workers.dev
```
we will set `org/repo` value:
```
- plugin: ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-vector-embeddings
```

The kernel should support calling a worker URL if it is set in the [manifest.json](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugin-template/blob/8d8f06958ca1273f299ae0bc2a51a6c512411af9/manifest.json).

How it is supposed to work:
1. If `plugin` config property contains URL then call it as a worker (this is to support close sourced plugins + for backwards compatibility)
2. If `plugin` config property contains `org/repo` value then fetch plugin's [manifest.json](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugin-template/blob/8d8f06958ca1273f299ae0bc2a51a6c512411af9/manifest.json):
  - If `homepage_url` property is present in the manifest then call the plugin as a worker
  - If `homepage_url` property is missing then call plugin as a github action

Original [comment](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/plugin-template/issues/33#issuecomment-2549092026)

I_kwDOK6Xdq86k0dJJ.title: Tagging issue author maps to @UbiquityOS

The idea is that many new contributors tend to ask things to the issue author. I think we should map @UbiquityOS to the issue author name so that it can facilitate simple things. For example, elaborating more on the spec or maybe even handling assignments.

I_kwDOK6Xdq87b0_vc.title: Remove plugin chain logic
I_kwDOK6Xdq87b0_vc.body:
The plugin chain logic is not relevant anymore since we cannot chain plugins. All the logic around it should be removed, like the `pluginChainSchema` and `KvStore`, to avoid bloated code.

## Tasks

- remove the code related to plugin chains
- update tests accordingly

I_kwDOK6Xdq86L87fF.title: Merge configurations by unique IDs
I_kwDOK6Xdq86L87fF.body:
We should look into a more robust configuration merge.

Quote from discussion:

I know there isn't a concept of merging configurations together on GitHub Actions. 

This doesn't address the key point I am making. The cause we are discussing is a collision with dependency IDs. And whether there are real-world scenarios where configurations might lead to duplicate dependencies. 

From my experience creating GitHub Actions CI scripts, I haven't encountered situations where adding redundant dependencies was necessary. This makes the theoretical merging logic problem irrelevant to our practical implementation. 

We should focus on the actual conditions under which our configurations operate, rather than hypothetical scenarios that don't align with real-world use cases.

Basically I'm saying to drop support for using the same dependency more than one, particularly when associated with the same webhook event. See [this comment](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquibot-kernel/pull/47#issuecomment-2154146051) for a suggestion on how to handle that.

_Originally posted by @0x4007 in https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquibot-kernel/issues/47#issuecomment-2162159354_

I_kwDOK6Xdq87O2axP.title: Integrate personal agent bridge into the kernel
I_kwDOK6Xdq87O2axP.body:
Currently the [bridge](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/personal-agent-bridge) only extracts the username and forwards the event using the kernel Github app credentials. The core functionality is only 90 lines of code.

The problem we already experienced is that the kernel changed the way the payload is sent (compression) - this made the bridge outdated but if it was in the kernel this wouldn't be a problem. Another inconvenience is that there's another HTTP request from the kernel to the bridge which additionally delays the execution.

It only makes sense that the bridge is a part of the kernel because it already uses kernel credentials, the code is minimal and reduces overhead and maintenance of the plugin.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86tiane.title: Callbacks - event handlers and hybrid plugins
I_kwDOK6Xdq86tiane.body:
I want to improve the experience of setting up event handlers and hybrid plugins.
I imagine something like this

```js
.on("issue_comment.created", "action", handler1)
.on("issue.closed", "worker", handler2)
```

The SDK would handle calling the Action or run it normally as a Worker and in case you're running it locally it would execute Action handlers locally too. 
Sometimes the reason why we need to call it as an Action is because some libraries are not supported by CF Workers so it'd be also good to have an option to run it as an Action even if you're running it locally.
Current hybrid plugins use `ACTION_REF` to know which Action to call but it'd be great if we could somehow get it automatically but I realize that may be not feasible.

At this point I would also consider merging `createActionsPlugin` and `createPlugin` for seamless experience.

I also realized that maybe we don't need kernel credentials to trigger Action from the Worker but only a fine-grained Github token that would only have access to the repo. Because we don't change the payload but only forward it to the Action, we don't need to generate a new signature and we can keep the original.

I_kwDOK6Xdq87GCNgf.title: Adopt pino for structured logging
I_kwDOK6Xdq87GCNgf.body:
The console is cluttered with information that saturates Deno logs, and locally makes it impossible to debug. We should adopt a logger that is production ready and let us filter logs properly. A few options are there like Pino, Wisnton or log4js-node. Let's try with Pino but if it gives too much trouble with Deno we can consider the others. This will greatly improve locall debugging as well as Deno logs that are unreadable at the moment.

Use pino for core logging (fast JSON, child loggers, redaction, Bun-friendly). Ranking considered: 1) pino 2) log4js-node 3) winston.

Alternatives (brief):
- winston: large ecosystem, flexible transports; heavier & slower, more config surface.
- log4js-node: simple categories & appenders; weaker native structured JSON & redaction.

Scope / Tasks:
- [ ] Add deps: pino, pino-pretty (dev only)
- [ ] Create src/logger/logger.ts exporting base logger (level via LOG_LEVEL, pretty in non-production)
- [ ] Implement redaction for tokens / secrets (/_?token|private_key|authorization/i)
- [ ] Provide helper to create child logger per event: repo, issue/PR number, installation id, stateId
- [ ] Replace console.* in handlers (start with issue-comment-created, push-event, repository-dispatch)
- [ ] Update README: logging usage + environment variable (LOG_LEVEL)
- [ ] Add minimal test ensuring child logger preserves base bindings & level

Acceptance:
- All previous console outputs replaced or intentionally retained (none left in src except pre-init warnings)
- JSON logs in production; pretty output locally
- Redaction verified (no raw tokens in test log sample)

I_kwDOK6Xdq864RXhB.title: Create company account for Azure
I_kwDOK6Xdq864RXhB.body: https://github.com/ubiquity/work.ubq.fi/issues/206#issuecomment-2910100809 We exceeded Azure quotas and should create a new subscription using only free tiers. We should be good if we do not use logs because they seem to eat a lot of data storage space. Curent usage: --- After trying multiple times with multiple cards and multiple accounts / countries, I was never able to get access to the free tier, only the "pay-as-you-go" feature which is not wanted. I even got charged 60$ for our usage last month. Instead, I've been digging on Deno deployments yet again. It appears that it is actually not so complicated to migrate if we simply use the hybrid mode from Deno. This means only changing the import paths in the file to include the extension ".ts" so Deno properly imports the files. I have successfully re-deployed `command-start-stop` that way quite easily. However, I did not find an option yet to deploy by branch with different `env` values, but I believe that should be possibly (hopefully). The only downside is that the hybrid mode is deemed to be buggy sometimes, and that the limit of the free tier in Deno is still very low. If that proves to be successful, the only change required in the SDK would be to add a `Deno` class handling the logs and creating the proper URL in the metadata. We should also create a deployment Action that would streamline this. Afterwards, we should be able to migrate any of our projects to Deno.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86uevBy.title: Enhance Kernel with Generalized Request Handling & Secure External API
I_kwDOK6Xdq86uevBy.body:
#### **Summary**  
Expand the kernel's ingress beyond GitHub webhooks by introducing a secure public API endpoint. This will allow external services to trigger GitHub Actions workflows, effectively turning Actions into a general-purpose backend.  

#### **Key Features**  
- **Public API Endpoint**:  
  - Accepts external requests to execute plugins via the kernel.  
  - Supports dynamic execution of actions, enabling broader integrations.  

- **Authentication & Security**:  
  - Requests must include:  
    - `installation ID` (this allows us to target the correct org)  
    - `repository ID` (optionally, to load a specific repo config)  
  - Partners must provide an **encrypted password** stored in their config.  
    - Uses **X25519 encryption** (same system as EVM private key storage).  
    - Clients pass a password in the request header, which the kernel decrypts.  
  - Rate-limiting to prevent abuse.  

- **Execution Flow**:  
  1. Kernel receives an authenticated request with at least `installation ID` and `password`.  
  2. It decrypts the password and verifies the request.  
  3. Executes the specified plugin and returns the response.  

- **Webhook Replies**:  
  - Requests can include a `response URL` header.  
  - The kernel posts the result from the action plugin to this URL upon completion.  

- **Future Enhancements**:  
  - Investigate **cross-plugin communication** via the kernel relay.  

#### **Next Steps**  
- Define API Contract (Headers, Payload Format)
- Implement Authentication & Decryption Logic
- Extend Kernel Request Handling to Invoke Plugins
- Add Support for Webhook Response Delivery

I_kwDOK6Xdq86qC9fR.title: Allow commands and `issue_comment.created` to coexist
I_kwDOK6Xdq86qC9fR.body:
If the manifest describes a command for a plugin, and the plugin also listens for `issue_comment.created`, the `issue_comment.created` does not get called if it doesn't match the command. This can be problematic if a plugin needs to both listen to the command and take other actions on `issue_comment.created`.

The current workaround is to not specify the command, and just handle handle `issue_command.created`. The downside is that the command will not show when invoking the `/help` command. This is the case for `/demo` for example.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86mRcoU.title: Investigate `skipBotEvents` always true even when defined `false` in the configuration
I_kwDOK6Xdq86mRcoU.body:
## What happened

Even when set to `false` the `skipBotEvents` key within the configuration seems to be evaluated to `true` when decoded. Example:
```yaml
  - skipBotEvents: false
    testUbqKey: 1234
    uses:
      - plugin: ubiquity-os-marketplace/daemon-disqualifier@main
        skipBotEvents: false
        with:
          warning: 7 days
          pullRequestRequired: true
          eventWhitelist:
            - pull_request.review_requested
            - pull_request.ready_for_review
            - pull_request_review_comment.created
            - issue_comment.created
            - push
          watch:
            optOut:
              - ubiquibot
              - launch-party
              - staging
              - production
          disqualification: 14 days
```

Once decoded by the kernel gives

```json
  {
    "skipBotEvents": false,
    "testUbqKey": 1234,
    "uses": [
      {
        "plugin": {
          "owner": "ubiquity-os-marketplace",
          "repo": "daemon-disqualifier",
          "workflowId": "compute.yml",
          "ref": "main"
        },
        "skipBotEvents": true,
        "with": {
          "warning": "7 days",
          "pullRequestRequired": true,
          "eventWhitelist": [
            "pull_request.review_requested",
            "pull_request.ready_for_review",
            "pull_request_review_comment.created",
            "issue_comment.created",
            "push"
          ],
          "watch": {
            "optOut": [
              "ubiquibot",
              "launch-party",
              "staging",
              "production"
            ]
          },
          "disqualification": "14 days"
        },
        "runsOn": [
          "issues.assigned"
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
```

which is incorrect.

## What was expected

The key should be `true` or `false`, same value as specified in the configuration.

## How to reproduce

- use the configuration mentioned above
- log the parsed configuration from the kernel
- observe the wrong values for `skipBotEvents`

I_kwDOK6Xdq86krZgg.title: Kernel gets stuck and does not compute any comment if too many simultaneous requests are sent

I_kwDOK6Xdq86RBuui.title: Configuration validation and annotations on errors
I_kwDOK6Xdq86RBuui.body:
In the bot v1, when the configuration is changed, annotations are added to the configuration file if any error is encountered within the configuration. It would be really nice to have for v2 as it is a common scenario to have an invalid configuration and have no feedback about it because the error would only be visible within the worker logs.

The main challenge is that only plugins are aware of their configuration shape, so probably the kernel should call every plugin to check their validity, which would require an endpoint or some access to the configuration validators. At the same time, the `manifest.json` validity could be checked, relating to https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquibot-kernel/issues/78.

Since this might imply quite a few network requests, we can also consider having this functionality as a separate Worker using [service binding](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/runtime-apis/bindings/service-bindings/).

I_kwDOK6Xdq86gjjjR.title: Do not log "manifest not found" as errors
I_kwDOK6Xdq86gjjjR.body: When the kernel tries to fetch a manifest and fails, it logs an error to the console. The problem is that it clutters the logs within the Cloudflare Log Dashboard, because these errors are "expected". We should keep logging an error if the query to the manifest fails for something else than a 404, and simply log a normal log when not found saying "manifest not found for repo xxx". See the CloudFlare logs: Removing these expected errors would help focusing on the real runtime errors needing attention.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86hpKPK.title: Use `hono`
I_kwDOK6Xdq86hpKPK.body:
Right now the kernel is deployed on a cloudflare worker which starts [throwing errors](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-kernel/issues/195) under heavy load.

It makes sense to refactor the kernel to use https://hono.dev/ in order to be able to deploy the kernel to different platforms like [vercel](https://hono.dev/docs/getting-started/vercel) or [azure](https://hono.dev/docs/getting-started/azure-functions).

I_kwDOK6Xdq86b9Kv_.title: Interactive setup
I_kwDOK6Xdq86b9Kv_.body:
The goal of this task is to simplify developer experience when setting up the kernel.
To do this we need to create an interactive CLI tool that will help with setting up the Github App and preparing Cloudflare for deployment of the kernel

This setup tool should be inspired by [Probot's setup flow](https://probot.github.io/docs/development/#generating-a-new-app)

### 1. Setup Cloudflare and Wrangler
- Make the user login with Wrangler
- Create a KV namespace and put the ID in wrangler.toml (we already have a [script](https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-kernel/blob/development/deploy/setup-kv-namespace.ts) that can be used and/or modified)

### 2. GitHub App Setup Automation
This can be done with [Github App Manifest](https://docs.github.com/en/apps/sharing-github-apps/registering-a-github-app-from-a-manifest)
- Automate creation of a GitHub App
- Subscribe to all required GitHub events
- Set necessary permissions for the App
- Convert the private key to PKCS#8
- Populate App ID, private key and webhook secret into `.dev.vars` file and in Cloudflare Worker secrets.

Additionally we can make an option in the SDK to disable signature verification in order to ease the development of new plugins.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86ba43c.title: LLM Command Router
I_kwDOK6Xdq86ba43c.body:
I would like to abstract all of the configured capabilities behind tagging @UbiquityOS. For example, we converted `/ask` to @UbiquityOS so you can more naturally ask a context aware question to your "AI team member." 

I think its useful to retain the `/` syntax, defined within the plugins, for shorthand. However we should have a small and cheap model (like 4o-mini?) that can read the repo's configured help menu, read their descriptions, and then invoke them on behalf of the user query. I think we can optimize costs further without the use of an LLM on a later date, as I don't see costs being a problem for awhile. 


| Command | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `/help` | List all available commands. | `/help` |
| `/allow` | Allows the user to modify the given label. | `/allow @user1 label` |
| `/query` | Returns the user's wallet, access, and multiplier information. | `/query @ubiquibot` |
| `/start` | Assign yourself to the issue. | `/start` |
| `/stop` | Unassign yourself from the issue. | `/stop` |
| `/wallet` | Register your wallet address for payments. | `/wallet ubq.eth` |

Examples:

@ubiquityos can i be assigned to the issue?
@ubiquityos please set my wallet address to 0x0000...
@ubiquityos let @0x4007 set priority labels.[^1^] 

This could be really nice as we grow the commands set further over time. 

[^1^]: We might need a more detailed description for this to work, or consider another approach like codebase search.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86CIIPo.title: Migration system for Supabase database
I_kwDOK6Xdq86CIIPo.body:
We should have a migration system set for our new Supabase instance. To this day, everything is done through the Supabase Dashboard and there is no track of the changes. This become even more critical as we are starting to setup RLS and CLS, which won't be reflected by a db diff dump, as stated in their docs.

This means if we need to create a new db, move it, rollback, run it locally, we would be unable to do so and should do everything manually to mimic the current db's state.

Supabase has a migration system implemented, and a CLI, so we should use these to keep our db updated with the latest schema. Maybe it is better to create a separate repository, as this doesn't really belong to this project specifically. Relates to https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquibot/issues/919

We need to the use plugin repo's Github token instead of the token passed by the kernel. 

This also means that every `compute.yml` action needs to pass the token (`secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN`) as an environment variable with appropriate permissions set (for `repository_dispatch`)

I_kwDOK6Xdq86a5-VK.title: Make plugin invocation concurrently
I_kwDOK6Xdq86a5-VK.body:
Just curious but does it make sense to Promise.all and invoke all requests concurrently?

Alternatively we should make the ordering more explicit in our config so that its more apparent we intend to take outputs from one and make it accessible to the others? 

The concept of plugin chains is useful but seems very abstract with our current config layout. Hence why im inquiring about concurrency. 

It could be interesting to support in series for configs that explicitly need that, and concurrency by default?

_Originally posted by @0x4007 in https://github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-kernel/pull/156#discussion_r1807218564_

I_kwDOK6Xdq86YYIFG.title: Do not break the loop when a plugin fails to run
I_kwDOK6Xdq86YYIFG.body:
The `command-start-stop` was never called which is why it never worked. The reason is that a plugin is failing on a fatal error because of the new orgs that have been created and somehow doesn't have the proper access level
```json
{
      "message": [
        "Calling handler {\"owner\":\"ubiquibot\",\"repo\":\"user-activity-watcher\",\"workflowId\":\"compute.yml\",\"ref\":\"development\"} for event issue_comment"
      ],
      "level": "log",
      "timestamp": 1727697850120

      ],
      "level": "error",
      "timestamp": 1727697850679
}
```
I fixed by making the plugin higher on the list. I believe the loop should not break if a plugin is not working properly but just log the error. Also maintaining 4 repos is complex, these repos won't get moved?

If a plugin is not reachable or does not start properly, the loop should continue as plugins are independent.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86ZDL14.title: Config (dev/prod) Environment Switch
I_kwDOK6Xdq86ZDL14.body:
> So we need both bots to have access on marketplace. 
> 
> We can have a new switch in the config that defines it's only for beta or production bot to read. 
> 
> If the bot doesn't have a valid config then it shouldn't respond to any user actions, like comments and commands. 

_Originally posted by @0x4007 in https://github.com/ubiquity/work.ubq.fi/issues/54#issuecomment-2394988488_

The problem to solve is that we can't have both bots installed normally on an org or else we have both responding to user actions and it gets noisy. We need to install both to be able to dispatch actions/events on the ubiquity-os-marketplace. But we need to disable the config for one or the other so that it won't respond to user actions like comments/commands. 

> we could have two configs `.ubiquibot-config.yml` and `.ubiquibot-config.dev.yml` and env variable that switches the bot between production and development

Lets do that.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86UZf82.title: Action plugin SDK
I_kwDOK6Xdq86UZf82.body: SDK is currently only available for Worker plugins, but it should also be made for Action plugins, additionally we should handle posting error comment inside the SDK so that all plugins will have it.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86X4UaJ.title: Environments for deployments
I_kwDOK6Xdq86X4UaJ.body: We are currently unable to deploy both a dev and prod version, due to the secrets being invalid (`APP_ID` would be different between two apps). We should have a development and production environments which would help deploying both.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86VmWT3.title: Encrypted values can be stolen and reused
I_kwDOK6Xdq86VmWT3.body:
I know I've highlighted this issue in the past, but I'm not sure where. 

As I'm going around these conferences and trying to fundraisers/find partners we realize that to quickly set up partners, we basically just send them the ubiquibot config file. 

So the encrypted private key was leaked unintentionally. 

As part of the decryption process, we should also include the organization ID or repo ID as part of the salt. 

That way, if I copy my ubiquibot config in my organization and I leak it, and somebody else tries to use my same exact EVM private key encrypted the bot, it will fail to decrypt correctly because their repository ID is different for example.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86UXO6z.title: The plugin should be considered valid when the shape is `plugin@a/b/c/d`
I_kwDOK6Xdq86UXO6z.body:
The kernel will consider invalid an Action plugin with the following pattern:
`my-plugin@my/nested/branch`

It only accepts one branch level. Any branch level should be valid.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86VvNSv.title: Accept commands that contain a carriage return
I_kwDOK6Xdq86VvNSv.body:
@0x4007 It seems that when a carriage return is in the body, it gets stripped on display by GitHub but is not recognized as a command by the kernel. I my two commands above I used:
```
/start
```
and
```
/start

```

The latter one should be valid as well.

_Originally posted by @gentlementlegen in https://github.com/ubiquity/devpool-directory-bounties/issues/27#issuecomment-2336533065_

I_kwDOK6Xdq86RK_od.title: The manifest is not fetch from the proper branch when used with Actions
I_kwDOK6Xdq86RK_od.body: When a specific branch or version is specified for a Github Action plugin, the Kernel tries to look within the default branch if the `manifest.json` exists. It should use the specified branch to retrieve the configuration.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86S7Tqs.title: Post deploy updated for Ubiquibot v2
I_kwDOK6Xdq86S7Tqs.body:
A few updates should be carried out post deployment of the new version of the bot:
- [x] the worker should be deployed on `Ubiquity DAO workers` Cloudflare account
- [x] the worker should be renamed `ubiquibot-kernel`
- [x] the webhook url of the app should be updated accordingly

I_kwDOK6Xdq86PT9qB.title: Add a `runs_on` section to the configuration and the manifests
I_kwDOK6Xdq86PT9qB.body:
We will have a `runs_on` item that will describe which events the plugin will be subscribed to, according to the following discussion.

Expected format:
- on the manifest side:
```diff
{
  "name": "query-user",
+ "ubiquity:runsOn": [ "issues.opened", "issues.closed" ]
}
```
- on the configuration side: 
```diff
plugins:
- 'issues.opened':
  - uses:
     - plugin: org/query-user
+      runsOn: [ 'issues.opened' ]
```

The configuration takes over the manifest is present, otherwise the manifest is the default.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86PtNqj.title: Allow multiple commands for the same plugin
I_kwDOK6Xdq86PtNqj.body:
The Kernel does not forward commands if the configuration does not list the command, as seen in [this code](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquibot-kernel/blob/41dd0cef127607e568c2ad8cab68de6b35d99d8a/src/github/handlers/index.ts#L38).
It works usually fine except for plugins that can handle multiple commands, such as [start stop](https://github.com/ubiquibot/command-start-stop).

Once the [manifests](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquibot-kernel/issues/73) are up and running, it would be important to update the logic so multiple commands can be handled by the same plugin.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86MtvmI.title: `command` array for help menu clarity
I_kwDOK6Xdq86MtvmI.body:
> @0x4007 Can be done just that the current design only allows for one command per `uses` configuration element, c.f. https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquibot-kernel/blob/570b68e18639e9c38a90872cfb8cbfbbdf661481/src/github/types/plugin-configuration.ts#L60
We could consider supporting an array of `command` instead of just one element.

_Originally posted by @gentlementlegen in https://github.com/Meniole/ubiquibot-kernel/issues/2#issuecomment-2176990975_

I_kwDOK6Xdq86OwKGu.title: Remove Unnecessary Secrets
I_kwDOK6Xdq86OwKGu.body:
Upon deploy of v2 we had to add several new secrets which were not necessary for v1. This indicates that they are not essential and must be removed. 

- `WEBHOOK_SECRET`
- `WEBHOOK_PROXY_URL`

I_kwDOK6Xdq86M8RlP.title: KV namespace setup
I_kwDOK6Xdq86M8RlP.body:
When the script `setup-kv-namespace.ts` is ran, an issue arises due to multiple accounts present on our CloudFlare.
Current issue:
https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquibot-kernel/actions/runs/9578178823/job/26407867903#step:5:84

According to the docs, this can be resolved by setting a `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` env variable:

>account_id  optional
>
>This is the ID of the account associated with your zone. You might have more than one account, so make sure to use the ID of the account associated with the zone/route you provide, if you provide one. It can also be specified through the CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID environment variable.

Which would fix the script and help automation for deployments.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86KJJbI.title: Remove type from configuration in plugins
I_kwDOK6Xdq86KJJbI.body:
The `type` element should be removed as the actual type of the plugin will be inferred from the data passed inside the `plugin` field (URL is a Worker, otherwise a GitHub Action).

[Related conversation](https://github.com/ubiquibot/command-query-user/pull/1#discussion_r1614781222)

Check [this](https://github.com/rndquu-org/test-repo/blob/9a57f04eae649d31b7564541a2408055ef17bafe/.github/.ubiquibot-config.yml) config which is meant to call the `pull/1` branch of the https://github.com/rndquu/user-activity-watcher plugin via github actions.

```

It seems that the kernel doesn't support calling a specific branch of the plugin which is really useful for QA.

What should be done:
- kernel should be able to call a specific branch of a plugin

I_kwDOK6Xdq86KSZVh.title: Run with default configuration if none is found
I_kwDOK6Xdq86KSZVh.body: The Kernel does not forward events if the target repo does not contain any configuration. It should either use a default, or use an Org configuration and forward events regardless.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86B0FpV.title: Expression produces a union type that is too complex to represent.
I_kwDOK6Xdq86B0FpV.body:
We need to fix a Typescript [issue](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquibot-kernel/blob/c000369cb11cdcf67e1ac6678df403149e699f43/src/github/handlers/repository-dispatch.ts#L52): `Expression produces a union type that is too complex to represent.`
I've tried multiple things:

- function only accepting context of certain events
```js
export async function getDefaultBranch(context: GitHubContext<"repository_dispatch">, owner: string, repository: string)
```

- making the function generic
```js
export async function getDefaultBranch<T extends EmitterWebhookEventName>(context: GitHubContext<T>, owner: string, repository: string)
```

- casting a Context<"repository_dispatch"> to Context<T> - it seems to work in my IDE but fails in CI but it's also a real solution
```js
const defaultBranch = await getDefaultBranch(context as GitHubContext, nextPlugin.plugin.owner, nextPlugin.plugin.repo);
```

---

I've also got this problem when developing [assistive-pricing](https://github.com/ubiquibot/assistive-pricing) plugin. I was trying to create typeguards like this:
```js
export function isIssueCommentCreatedEvent(context: Context): context is Context<"issue_comment.created"> {
  return context.eventName === "issue_comment.created";
}
```
`context is Context<"issue_comment.created">` triggers the error

I_kwDOK6Xdq86J0Ftb.title: Secure Worker routing
I_kwDOK6Xdq86J0Ftb.body:
We used to only have GitHub Actions, which can be secured using GitHub Authentication system. Now that we have workers, they are currently open endpoints, which implies security risks as anyone can access them and potentially trigger them outside of our control.

This is why we should require authentication to reach these endpoints as well. After looking at what Cloudflare offers, I stumbled upon https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/ which provides authentication system Cloudflare wide.

I need to do some more research but from what I understand, we can provide routes we would like to protect, by only allowing users, IPs, domain to be able to reach it. Before doing any request, the caller should ask to exchange some credentials for a token, used for subsequent calls. Then we would just have to check for that token within our Workers, and deny the request if none is present.

The challenge is for 3rd parties, that would have to provide their endpoint for acquiring the token so we can call their services. Maybe that's where the IP whitelisting could come in handy.

Likewise, the invocation of these workers should be secured on the kernel side, to properly handle their callback, or, directly pickup the result of the `fetch` call to trigger next plugins in the chain.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86Iwcu3.title: Worker plugins
I_kwDOK6Xdq86Iwcu3.body:
We should have the capability of running the plugins inside Workers, which would be very useful for short-lived tasks that require responsiveness like commands for example. Currently, we only support Actions, which works fine but takes ~1 minute response time, which is not user friendly when it comes to using commands with the bot.

In the configuration, for each plugin it is possible to specify a [`type`](https://github.com/ubiquity/ubiquibot-kernel/blob/c8da3c0e07eb2735b0e7d63d5f2997a138fd2135/src/github/types/plugin-configuration.ts#L37) that should determine what kind of plugin it is. A new value like `worker` should be added, and should make the plugin run on a worker instead of calling a GitHub Action.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86Csvw4.title: Create correct Knip configuration for ubiquibot-kernel repository
I_kwDOK6Xdq86Csvw4.body: To goal is to fix Knip warnings to have clean Knip runs for CI and pull request checks.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86BY6ix.title: Testing with Jest

Since the repository was created before the `ts-template` repository was created, it does not include a basic test setup with Jest. It would be very helpful to have it here as well, to better understand how the kernel is articulated and having a basic setup out of the box.

What would be needed:
- jest testing system
- coverage
- a basic use case that shows a successful workflow

Refer [here](https://github.com/ubiquity/ts-template) for Jest setup.

I_kwDOK6Xdq86BY8hq.body: Outdated boilerplate code here so just need to update it.

I_kwDOMoorW86h8B2x.title: Improving Recommendations
I_kwDOMoorW86h8B2x.body:
Cool to see it always recommends at least one person but it is very much not relevant. 

Whats the timeline looking like to populate the database by scraping the existing closed issues and their assignees @sshivaditya2019? 

### repo/org/global Context Matching

I think we should optimize our logic. Tasks that may look similar but posted in two different repositories may in reality be quite different. Normally I would prefer to matchmake against those who completed similar tasks within the same repository, but we can "fallback" to org context (try to matchmake against any repository's task in the org) and then a final "fallback" to global context. 

Perhaps the simplest way to calculate this could be to subtract relevance "points"

Example:
```
0% adjustment: Same repository matching percentage 
-25% adjustment: Same organization
-50%: Global
```

Meaning for an identical task posted in three different locations:
```
same repo = 100% match
same org, different repo = 75% match
different org = 50% match
```

Just an idea but open to discussing to make this more accurate and simple to implement. 

### Recent Code Changes Fallback

Another idea to always try and match somebody is to check how much of the codebase was authored by whom. If nobody hits a relevance of greater than 25% we can fallback to assigning whoever wrote most of the code in the repository. Surely we can find the stats somewhere. 

### Future Considerations

Because we want the system to always be assigning the right contributor at the right time in the future, we should consider the output of the `/start` command as well, with all its checks. If UbiquityOS is able to run `/start` on behalf of others this can solve most of the problems I can think of. 

Specifically, imagine the scenario where the same top contributor is always recommended to work on the newly posted tasks. [^05^] [^04^] [^03^] [^01^] With a concurrent task limit of 2, they will quickly be saturated with work. We should be able to detect this and fallback/recommend/assign the next best. 

### Original Context

>[!NOTE]
>The following contributors may be suitable for this task:
>### [gentlementlegen](https://www.github.com/gentlementlegen)
> `1% Match` [ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-kernel#125](https://www.github.com/ubiquity-os/ubiquity-os-kernel/issues/125)

### Summary of Review Comments for PR #91

The following improvements have been identified from the review comments:

1. **User-Friendly URL Handling**
   - [Comment](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-vector-embeddings/pull/91#discussion_r1359852530)
   - Make URL handling more user-friendly in the regex implementation

2. **Logger Implementation**
   - [Comment](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-vector-embeddings/pull/91#discussion_r1359852534)
   - Update logger usage to follow original design pattern: `throw context.logger.info("...")`

3. **Footnote ID Prefixing**
   - [Comment](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-vector-embeddings/pull/91#discussion_r1359852533)
   - Replace zero-prefixed footnote IDs with more meaningful prefixes (e.g., `deduplication-`)
   - Make footnote IDs more precise and sensible

4. **Validation Description Enhancement**
   - [Comment](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-vector-embeddings/pull/91#discussion_r1359852540)
   - Update description for LLM to be more precise regarding contributor recommendations
   - Suggested text: "this amount of contributors will always be recommended regardless of the similarity score."

5. **Timeout Configuration**
   - [Comment](https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/text-vector-embeddings/pull/91#discussion_r1359852539)
   - Consider using the `ms` library for more ergonomic timeout handling

### Tasks

- [ ] Implement user-friendly URL regex handling
- [ ] Update logger implementation pattern
- [ ] Refactor footnote ID system with meaningful prefixes
- [ ] Enhance validation descriptions for clarity
- [ ] Integrate `ms` library for timeout configuration

I_kwDOMoorW87CqGd7.title: Fix duplicate results being returned from similarity matching
I_kwDOMoorW87CqGd7.body:
## Problem

The similarity matching system is returning duplicate results instead of unique matches. This is a reproducible bug where the same contributor recommendations appear multiple times.

## Context

Referenced from: https://github.com/ubiquity-os-marketplace/command-config/issues/16#issuecomment-3121778678

The issue shows that when querying for suitable contributors, the system consistently returns 2 identical results instead of a single result or different candidates.

## Expected Behavior

The similarity matching should return unique results without duplicates.

## Suspected Cause

The bug likely stems from:
- Multiple event listeners being attached
- The same event being invoked multiple times
- Duplicate vector similarity calculations

## Investigation Needed

- Check if there are multiple event handlers processing the same request
- Verify vector similarity calculation logic for deduplication
- Review the response formatting to ensure unique results only

I_kwDOMoorW87HodcS.title: Nomic Embeddings Model for +10% Accuracy
I_kwDOMoorW87HodcS.body:
The vector embedding model that we use is slightly outdated. I did some research to see what has higher accuracy, and I found one with 10% increased accuracy. We should make use of it as well as our old model. 

## Leaderboard

Model | Top-5 Retrieval Accuracy (%) | Notes
-- | -- | --
Nomic Embed v1.5 | ~86.2% | Highest overall retrieval accuracy in semantic search benchmarks, especially strong on short & direct queries. Open source and highly accurate.
Voyage-3-large | ~75-77% | Best model from Voyage AI, state-of-the-art in industry, notably outperforms OpenAI text-embedding-3-large by ~9.7%. Strong on domain-specific tasks.

## Tradeoff

> When you submit a GitHub comment that exceeds the token capacity of the embedding model, typically:
> - The model will truncate the input at its token limit (e.g., 8,192 tokens for Nomic or 32,000 tokens for Voyage).
> - This means any text beyond the max token limit is ignored, which can lead to loss of context and reduced embedding quality if important content is cut off.

## Solution

> 2. Similarity Functions
> - Key Point: Embeddings from different models generally cannot be directly compared using the same similarity function (e.g., cosine or dot product) unless their vector spaces are aligned.
> - Each model produces numerical vectors in its own latent space, meaning the scale, direction, and semantic meaning may differ dramatically.
> - Comparing Nomic vectors against Voyage vectors directly will yield misleading results.
> 
> What To Do:
> - In practice, keep embeddings from each model in separate indices or collections.
> - For each query or search, use the correct index according to the embedding model used: maintain “Nomic space” and “Voyage space” separately.

## Remarks

GitHub comment limit is 65,536 characters ≈ 16,384 tokens (rough estimate.) This means that especially comments with metadata unlikely will be able to rely on Nomic (8,192 max tokens)

## Research

https://www.perplexity.ai/search/best-vector-embeddings-model-CSpgO23jTBW5AHrh30a0cA

I_kwDOMoorW86lalGA.title: `/annotate`
I_kwDOMoorW86lalGA.body:
There are situations where we write about certain issues that were likely to have been posted in the past, but are difficult to find. 

Imagine if using vector embeddings, we can instantly link to whatever issues are being referenced: 

> We had some problems with KV, so I don't know if it's wise to enable plugin communication without fixing the problem (I can't find the issue about this). Alternatively we could make this as part of a module inside  `text-conversation-rewards`

From https://github.com/kingsley-einstein/contributions-scan/pull/4#issuecomment-2577468623

Using this command, we should replicate the behavior of issue deduplication on the existing comment and edit/add the footnotes with links to the source issues.

A limitation that I see is how wide of a search we should conduct. Within the same repository is somewhat useless, and globally might incur too much noise. 

Organization wide might be the best default, but sometimes it would be very useful, especially for us using three organizations, to do a global annotation/search. 

Perhaps we can have optional arguments to scope the search.

```
/annotate https://github.com/kingsley-einstein/contributions-scan/pull/4#issuecomment-2577468623 global
```

But default can be just

```
/annotate
```

And it will automatically annotate the previous comment with an organization wide search.

I_kwDOMoorW86WHU7T.title: Codebase semantic search
I_kwDOMoorW86WHU7T.body:
For on boarding new developers, or just to synchronize existing developers, we should be generating embeddings of our code base. We can do per file or on the function level, depending on which has better performance.

I asked GPT for the best sorting method on the [embedding leaderboard](https://huggingface.co/spaces/mteb/leaderboard). 

Later it clarified that we should be sorting by "retrieval with instructions" for best results. 

[FollowIR-7B](https://huggingface.co/jhu-clsp/FollowIR-7B) is the highest ranked. 

We should be doing this in two steps:
1. Primarily, we should be generating embeddings of the new files when pull requests are merged.
2. We should have a tool that we can run manually (manual dispatch on GitHub actions ok) to generate all the embeddings. This would be expected to be used if there were pushes directly to the default branch and were not added via pull request

### additional context

> For this use case (codebase question-answering), your focus should be on retrieval tasks. Based on the criteria provided, here’s how I would prioritize the metrics:

> 1. **Retrieval Average (15 datasets)**: This should be your highest priority. The better the retrieval capability of the model, the more relevant code chunks it will retrieve when you ask a question about the codebase.

> 2. **Embedding Dimensions**: A higher embedding dimension may provide more nuanced representations of code and questions, improving retrieval accuracy. However, this needs to be balanced with memory usage and model size.

> 3. **Model Size (Million Parameters)**: Larger models tend to perform better in generating high-quality embeddings, but at the cost of memory and speed. Consider how much memory and computational power you can afford.

> 4. **Max Tokens**: A higher max token limit is useful for code because some functions or files can be quite large. You'll want a model that can handle bigger chunks of code.

> 5. **Classification Average (12 datasets)**: Code-related tasks sometimes involve classification (e.g., determining the type of question or identifying sections of code). A higher classification score can help in such scenarios.

> 6. **STS Average (10 datasets)**: Semantic Textual Similarity (STS) is also important as it measures how well the embeddings capture semantic meaning, which is useful for understanding the context and retrieving the right code section.

> ### Sorting Criteria:
> Sort by **Retrieval Average** first, then consider **Max Tokens** and **Embedding Dimensions** for practical handling of code and performance optimization.

> The other metrics like **Classification**, **Clustering**, and **Reranking** are less critical for your specific use case, but they can help refine the quality if you have a secondary need for such tasks.

I_kwDOMoorW86Y2UeG.title: Similar Issue Editing Specification Timeout
I_kwDOMoorW86Y2UeG.body:
I generally write specifications, post, edit, post, edit etc.

While I am editing, the script runs again and changes my specification body. This is a problem because when I complete my edit, GitHub refuses to accept my changes because there were other changes that occurred in the background from the bot. It tells me I need to refresh the page, and try again. 

This is obviously annoying so we need to make a timeout for edit. The timeout feature should be mindful of two things:
1. It should restart the timer if there is another edit. 
2. It should be configurable, with the default being 30 minutes.

I_kwDOMoorW86pVkJT.title: `/start` git submodule
I_kwDOMoorW86pVkJT.body:
Task matchmaking will be very powerful when coupled with the `/start` logic in this plugin.   

The key magic of `/start` is that it is designed to check if the assignee is already saturated with work (i.e. [^01^]         two concurrent tasks max) or needs to place their attention on any existing open pull reviews etc. 

Task recommendation should have the ability to automatically assign team members based on how saturated they are. If for example the top three recommendations are 

1. [^05^]      gentlementlegen 
2. whilefoo 
3.  shiv810 

But gentlementlegen is already working on two projects concurrently, then the matchmaking should automatically go to the next suitable candidate, Whilefoo. 

The purpose of this proposal is so that we don't need to duplicate the logic in that plugin for matchmaking. [^02^]   Instead, we should expose an API to have this plugin compute whether gentlementlegen can start or not.  

1. Let's clone the `command-start-stop` repository as a submodule inside of the `text-vector-embeddings` repository and then invoke the logic locally. 
2. Let's add the capability to assign collaborators based on the output of start logic. [^04^] 

### cron future 

We can constantly check the saturation state of team members and when they finish a project they can be automatically assigned to any that need to be completed, in order of priority. Then the AI manager will always be pushing to get urgent tasks done. 

It will also intelligently not assign those who are already disqualified because that's also in the start logic! [^03^]

I_kwDOMoorW86pA85-.title: Double Task Recommendation
I_kwDOMoorW86pA85-.body:
I've noticed that this happened a few times in the last few days. Please diagnose the root problem and fix.

To reproduce you need to file a new issue and set both labels before submitting. 

>[!NOTE]

I_kwDOMoorW86h8vYt.title: Scraper: Populate "Closed As Complete" Issue Specifications
I_kwDOMoorW86h8vYt.body:
We need to populate our matchmaking database. We "register" users in our system when they "Sign in with GitHub" on https://devpool.directory/

I suppose with GraphQL it may be simple to check every issue that is closed as complete with these assignees. 

- We need to capture all of the metadata to make it easier to work with later.
- We need to be able to determine the location of the issue, which means organization and repository. We should capture both the names as well as the IDs, in case of renames. 
- We need to generate vector embeddings and store the specifications in plain text. 

For now we can work off of a static snapshot of registrants. In the future, we should automatically scrape their completed issues as soon as they register on our site. 

[auth.users.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18005367/auth.users.json)
